title: classic-cli + classic-node: control-socket bridge for spawn frames
description:
Follow-up to classic-vo5. The arg parser for 'classic spawn' is in place (validates --requires non-empty, KEY=VAL env entries, stdin file existence, requires --, etc.) but invocation currently exits with 'spawn-via-control-socket not yet wired'. To complete plan 04 task 7:

1. Add a SpawnRequest+frame relay over the existing classic-node admin UDS (state_dir/admin.sock), OR add a dedicated UDS for binary frames.
2. CLI side: open the socket, send SpawnRequest, drive stdio relay (CLI stdin -> ChildStdio frames; incoming ChildStdio frames -> stdout/stderr writers), exit with the propagated child exit code (0-255 normal, 128+sig on signal, 2 on spawn failure, 1 on usage).
3. Daemon side: route inbound SpawnRequest frames to classic_spawn::run_originator with a Placer backed by classic-place + an AdMatcher backed by classic-ad's AdStore, and a PeerSpawn impl that uses PeerMesh.
4. Local-target shortcut: when place() returns the local NodeId, run classic_spawn::run_executor in-process with NoOpScopeProvider for now (real ScopeProvider that wires ScopeHandle + BPF requires root and lands separately).
5. Tests: end-to-end CLI smoke against a single in-process daemon.

This is intentionally a separate bead because the bridge is significant work (~300+ LOC of UDS framing, frame routing, state-machine wiring) and the partial commit landed the user-visible CLI surface so other tasks aren't blocked.

title: classic-spawn + classic-cli: integration tests (single-node, two-node, no-match, fallback)
description:
# Task 9 — Integration tests: single-node spawn, two-node spawn, no-match, fallback

Parent epic: classic-1j3 (Feature: Spawn pipeline). Plan: plans/04-spawn-pipeline.md @ commit a7b6585. Read with: git show a7b6585:plans/04-spawn-pipeline.md.

## Reading first

Read plans/04-spawn-pipeline.md "Testing plan" section in full (Unit / Integration / End-to-end / Hardware-dependent). Skim plan 01 testing plan for `tokio::LocalSet` + in-memory `Connection` test utilities.

## Scope

End-to-end coverage that exercises the headline AC list from the plan. Mostly black-box: no internal mocks; spin up real classicd instances (or in-process equivalents) and drive `classic spawn` against them.

In scope:
- Two-daemons-in-one-process integration via `tokio::LocalSet` + `classic-proto` test-utils `Connection`. Stubbed `CapBroker` (no-op) + stubbed BPF (no-op). Exercises every spawn frame path without root.
- A `tests/` harness that brings up:
  - 2x classicd peered over an in-memory transport.
  - 1x CLI client connected to classicd-A's control socket (Unix or in-memory).
  - A scripted ad cache so predicates can target a specific node deterministically.
- Tests:
  - **single-node spawn**: predicate `"true"`, argv `["/bin/echo", "hi"]`. Expect stdout=`hi\n`, exit 0. (plan AC-2).
  - **two-node spawn**: predicate forces node-B placement (e.g. ad cache says only B has matching hardware). Expect child runs on B; observable by the executor's `local_node` matching B in trace logs OR by exec'ing `/bin/hostname` if test fixtures permit. (plan AC-3).
  - **stdio fidelity**: 4 MiB random buffer through `--stdin file -- /bin/cat`; assert sha256 round-trips. (plan AC-4).
  - **exit codes**: `/bin/false` -> 1; `/bin/sleep 100` SIGTERM'd by test -> 128+15. (plan AC-5).
  - **no-match**: predicate guaranteed unsatisfiable -> CLI exit 2; stderr contains `no node matches predicate`. (plan AC-6).
  - **fallback**: scripted executor where node A always returns `SpawnDeny{DeviceTaken}` and node B accepts; assert `Trying(0) -> Trying(1) -> Running`. (plan §"placement-failure fallback").
  - **SIGINT propagation**: launch `/bin/sleep 100`, send SIGINT to the CLI; child receives SIGTERM within 1s; caps released. (plan AC-8).
  - **scope lifecycle**: with a tempdir-rooted Sysroot, assert `task-<MboxId>.scope` exists during the spawn and is removed after exit. (plan AC-9).
  - **concurrent exclusive contention**: two spawns claim the same exclusive `GpuMinor(0)`; second fails with `DeviceTaken`. (plan AC-7).

Out of scope:
- Hardware-gated tests (`hw-bpf`, `hw-gpu`) — covered in Task 3 + plan AC-10; leave a `#[ignore]` slot here pointing at the gate.
- Real cgroup/BPF — those run separately on a Linux 6.1+ root host (plan §End-to-end manual recipe).

## Design slice

> **Single-process loopback.** Two `classicd` instances in one process via `tokio::LocalSet` and the in-memory `Connection` impl from `classic-proto` test utils. Exercises every frame path without root or BPF.
>
> **Two daemons over UDS** (no root): no-op `CapBroker`, BPF attach stubbed; byte-perfect `cat` of `/dev/urandom`-1 MiB round-trip.
>
> **Predicate fallback.** Scripted ad cache where node A refuses and node B accepts; assert `Trying(0) -> Trying(1) -> Running`.

## Inputs

- All previous tasks (1-8). This task is the capstone.
- `classic-proto` test utils for in-memory Connection (from plan 01).
- Stubbed CapBroker/BPF in test-only modules (a `cfg(test)` flag or a feature `test-stubs`).

## Outputs

Files to create:
- `crates/classic-spawn/tests/integration_loopback.rs` — two-daemons-in-one-process harness.
- `crates/classic-cli/tests/e2e_spawn.rs` — drives the CLI binary or its library entry against the loopback harness.
- `tests/fixtures/` (workspace-level if needed) — scripted ads for the fallback test.
- A small `tests/common/mod.rs` with the harness builder.

## Test plan

Run via `cargo test --workspace --test integration_loopback --test e2e_spawn`:
- All AC tests above (plan AC-2, AC-3, AC-4, AC-5, AC-6, AC-7, AC-8, AC-9) pass on Linux x86_64 in CI without root, GPU, or BPF.
- Hardware-gated tests are present but `#[ignore]`d in default runs; documented in test module header how to run with `--features hw-bpf -- --ignored`.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] AC-T9.1: `cargo test -p classic-spawn --test integration_loopback` green on Linux x86_64 (no root, no GPU, no BPF) — covers single-node spawn, fallback, scope lifecycle (tempdir Sysroot), exclusive contention.
- [ ] AC-T9.2: `cargo test -p classic-cli --test e2e_spawn` green — covers AC-2, AC-3, AC-4, AC-5, AC-6, AC-8.
- [ ] AC-T9.3: `--stdin` 4 MiB random round-trip via `/bin/cat` is byte-perfect (plan AC-4) — sha256 match in the test.
- [ ] AC-T9.4: NoCandidates path produces stderr containing `no node matches predicate` and exit code 2 (plan AC-6).
- [ ] AC-T9.5: Fallback test asserts state transitions `Trying(0) -> Trying(1) -> Running` via tracing assertions or exposed test hooks.
- [ ] AC-T9.6: SIGINT to the CLI causes child SIGTERM within 1s; release_all is called (verified via mock broker call log).
- [ ] AC-T9.7: Tempdir Sysroot test asserts `task-<MboxId>.scope` is created during spawn and rmdir'd after child exit (plan AC-9).
- [ ] AC-T9.8: Concurrent `--exclusive-device` test: second spawn fails with `DeviceTaken`; first still running (plan AC-7).
- [ ] AC-T9.9: Hardware-gated test stubs are present in source, `#[ignore]`'d, with module-level docs explaining how to run them.

## Intra-epic dependencies

- Parent: classic-1j3.
- Prereqs (within this epic): Task 6 (state machines), Task 7 (CLI), Task 8 (failure-mode rendering for the no-match assertion).
- Blocks: nothing (capstone).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] AC-T9.1: `cargo test -p classic-spawn --test integration_loopback` green on Linux x86_64 (no root/GPU/BPF) — covers single-node spawn, fallback, scope lifecycle (tempdir), exclusive contention.
- [ ] AC-T9.2: `cargo test -p classic-cli --test e2e_spawn` green — covers plan AC-2/3/4/5/6/8.
- [ ] AC-T9.3: `--stdin` 4 MiB random round-trip via `/bin/cat` is byte-perfect (plan AC-4) — sha256 match in the test.
- [ ] AC-T9.4: NoCandidates path produces stderr containing `no node matches predicate` and exit code 2 (plan AC-6).
- [ ] AC-T9.5: Fallback test asserts `Trying(0) -> Trying(1) -> Running` via tracing assertions or test hooks.
- [ ] AC-T9.6: SIGINT to the CLI causes child SIGTERM within 1s; release_all is called (verified via mock broker call log).
- [ ] AC-T9.7: Tempdir Sysroot test asserts `task-<MboxId>.scope` exists during spawn and is rmdir'd after child exit (plan AC-9).
- [ ] AC-T9.8: Concurrent `--exclusive-device` test: second spawn fails with DeviceTaken; first still running (plan AC-7).
- [ ] AC-T9.9: Hardware-gated test stubs are present in source, `#[ignore]`d, with module-level docs explaining how to run them.

title: classic-spawn + classic-cli: failure-mode polish (NoCandidates, ranked-fallback, deny rendering)
description:
# Task 8 — Failure-mode handling: no-match -> SpawnDeny path; ranked-fallback when first choice refuses

Parent epic: classic-1j3 (Feature: Spawn pipeline). Plan: plans/04-spawn-pipeline.md @ commit a7b6585. Read with: git show a7b6585:plans/04-spawn-pipeline.md.

## Reading first

Read plans/04-spawn-pipeline.md "Sequence: placement-failure fallback" + "Failure handling" + "Acceptance criteria" sections in full.

## Scope

Polish the failure-mode UX end-to-end. Tasks 6 + 7 implement the mechanics; this task makes them human-friendly and locks the matrix down with explicit tests:

- CLI rendering of every terminal `SpawnDeny` reason: print the documented prefix to stderr, exit 2.
- Originator's terminal SpawnDeny chooses the most informative reason from the list of per-candidate denies (e.g. if all candidates returned `DeviceTaken`, the terminal reason is `AllCandidatesRefused` with detail listing each candidate's reason).
- Detail-string convention: human-only, never parsed. Originator preserves per-candidate detail in the terminal SpawnDeny's `detail` field as `"node=<NodeId>: <reason>; ..."`.
- A dedicated test module covering each plan AC-1..AC-9 failure case.

In scope:
- `crates/classic-cli/src/cmd/spawn.rs` (MOD): `render_deny(deny: &SpawnDeny)` -> stderr message; specific prefixes:
  - `NoCandidates` -> `"no node matches predicate: <detail>"` (matches plan AC-6 wording).
  - `AllCandidatesRefused` -> `"all candidates refused: <detail>"`.
  - `DeviceTaken` -> `"device unavailable: <detail>"` (rarely terminal; only if every candidate returns it).
  - `ExecFailed` -> `"exec failed on <node>: <detail>"`.
  - others -> a default `"spawn refused: <reason> (<detail>)"`.
- `crates/classic-spawn/src/originator.rs` (MOD): aggregator that builds the terminal SpawnDeny detail from the per-candidate denies; verifies retry-vs-terminal mapping with explicit tests for every DenyReason.
- Telemetry hooks: `tracing::info!` per state transition; `tracing::warn!` when a candidate refuses; `tracing::error!` on terminal denial.

Out of scope:
- Mechanics of retry loops (Task 6).
- Single/two-node spawn happy-path tests (Task 9).

## Design slice

> | Stage                  | Failure                  | Outcome                                                          |
> |------------------------|--------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------|
> | Originator `place()`   | empty result             | `SpawnDeny{NoCandidates}` -> CLI exits 2.                         |
> | Originator peer dial   | TCP connect fails        | Skip candidate; all-fail -> `AllCandidatesRefused`.               |
> | Executor predicate     | stale ad                 | `SpawnDeny{PredicateNotSatisfied}`; originator tries next.       |
> | Executor `CapBroker`   | exclusive held           | `SpawnDeny{DeviceTaken}`; originator tries next.                 |
> | Executor cgroup mkdir  | EPERM/ENOSPC             | `SpawnDeny{CgroupSetupFailed, detail: <errno>}`.                 |
> | Executor BPF attach    | EINVAL                   | `SpawnDeny{CgroupSetupFailed}`; caps released.                   |
> | Executor fork/exec     | ENOEXEC/ENOENT           | `SpawnDeny{ExecFailed}`; caps released; **no retry**.            |
> | Child crash            | segfault/SIGKILL         | `ChildExit{signal: Some(n), code: None}`; caps released.         |
> | Originator disconnect  | CLI Ctrl-C / socket loss | Executor `release_all`s caps; SIGTERM child, SIGKILL after 10 s. |

> ```
> CLI       local classicd          remote N2         remote N1
>  |--SpawnReq->|                       |                 |
>  |            |--place() -> [N2, N1]  |                 |
>  |            |--SpawnRequest-------->|                 |
>  |            |                       |--broker.acquire |
>  |            |                       |   -> Err(Taken) |
>  |            |<--SpawnDeny(DevTkn)---|                 |
>  |            |   (try next)                            |
>  |            |--SpawnRequest---------------------------|
>  |            |                                         |--ok
>  |            |<--------------------SpawnAck------------|
>  |<-SpawnAck--|                                         |
> ```

## Inputs

- Task 6 originator/executor (state machines) — extend with detail aggregator.
- Task 7 CLI — extend with render_deny.
- All five spawn frames from Task 5.

## Outputs

Files to modify:
- `crates/classic-spawn/src/originator.rs` — `aggregate_terminal_deny(per_candidate: &[(NodeId, SpawnDeny)]) -> SpawnDeny`.
- `crates/classic-cli/src/cmd/spawn.rs` — `render_deny`; CLI exit-2 path goes through this.
- `crates/classic-spawn/src/lib.rs` — re-export `aggregate_terminal_deny` for tests.
- New test module `crates/classic-spawn/tests/failure_matrix.rs`.
- New test module `crates/classic-cli/tests/render_deny.rs`.

## Test plan

`cargo test -p classic-spawn --test failure_matrix` (table-driven, mocked transport):
- Row per failure-table entry above; assert each input yields the documented terminal vs retry behaviour.
- Multi-candidate fallback: 3 candidates, idx 0 -> DeviceTaken, idx 1 -> PredicateNotSatisfied, idx 2 -> SpawnAck. State transitions: `Trying(0) -> Trying(1) -> Trying(2) -> Running`.
- All-fail aggregation: 3 candidates, all return DeviceTaken -> terminal SpawnDeny{AllCandidatesRefused, detail contains all 3 nodes' reasons}.
- ExecFailed on idx=0 short-circuits — no further candidates tried.

`cargo test -p classic-cli --test render_deny`:
- Each DenyReason renders with the documented stderr prefix.
- `NoCandidates` rendering contains `"no node matches predicate"` substring (plan AC-6).
- ExitCode is always 2 for terminal SpawnDeny rendering paths.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] AC-T8.1: `cargo test --test failure_matrix -p classic-spawn` green: every row in the failure-handling table is asserted.
- [ ] AC-T8.2: `cargo test --test render_deny -p classic-cli` green: each DenyReason renders with the documented prefix, exit code 2.
- [ ] AC-T8.3: Terminal SpawnDeny detail aggregates per-candidate reasons in a stable, human-readable format ("node=<NodeId>: <reason>; ...").
- [ ] AC-T8.4: ExecFailed short-circuits the candidate loop (no retry).
- [ ] AC-T8.5: NoCandidates rendering contains `no node matches predicate` (plan AC-6).
- [ ] AC-T8.6: tracing events fire for every state transition + per-candidate refusal + terminal denial; verifiable via `tracing-test`.

## Intra-epic dependencies

- Parent: classic-1j3.
- Prereqs (within this epic): Task 6 (state machines), Task 7 (CLI).
- Blocks: Task 9 (integration tests rely on these AC for the no-match and fallback paths).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] AC-T8.1: `cargo test --test failure_matrix -p classic-spawn` green: every row of the failure-handling table is asserted.
- [ ] AC-T8.2: `cargo test --test render_deny -p classic-cli` green: each DenyReason renders with the documented stderr prefix, exit code 2.
- [ ] AC-T8.3: Terminal SpawnDeny detail aggregates per-candidate reasons in a stable, human-readable format ("node=<NodeId>: <reason>; ...").
- [ ] AC-T8.4: ExecFailed short-circuits the candidate loop (no retry).
- [ ] AC-T8.5: NoCandidates rendering contains `no node matches predicate` (plan AC-6).
- [ ] AC-T8.6: tracing events fire for every state transition + per-candidate refusal + terminal denial; verifiable via `tracing-test`.

title: classic-cli + classic-node: classic spawn subcommand + control-socket bring-up
description:
# Task 7 — CLI `classic spawn` subcommand: arg parsing + control-socket bring-up + local↔remote orchestration

Parent epic: classic-1j3 (Feature: Spawn pipeline). Plan: plans/04-spawn-pipeline.md @ commit a7b6585. Read with: git show a7b6585:plans/04-spawn-pipeline.md.

## Reading first

Read plans/04-spawn-pipeline.md "CLI surface" subsection in full. Also read plans/03-placement-predicates.md (for the predicate string format the CLI passes through). Skim the existing `classic-cli` crate from plan 01 for clap conventions.

## Scope

Wire up the user-facing `classic spawn` CLI subcommand and the control-socket transport between CLI and local daemon.

In scope:
- `crates/classic-cli/src/cmd/spawn.rs` (NEW) — clap subcommand parser + execution: open the control-socket Frame connection, send SpawnRequest, relay stdin from the CLI process / file as ChildStdio frames, print stdout/stderr from incoming ChildStdio frames, exit with the propagated code.
- `crates/classic-cli/src/bin/classic.rs` (MOD) — register the `spawn` subcommand on the root `classic` binary.
- Control-socket server side in `classic-node`: bind `/run/classicd/control.sock` (fallback `$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/classicd.sock`), accept connections, run them through the same FrameMux as peer connections, dispatching spawn frames to the SpawnDispatcher (registered in Tasks 5/6).
- Predicate empty-string rejection: `--requires ""` -> exit 1 with usage error message.
- CLI exit-code translation per plan: child exit (0-255) on normal; `128 + signum` on signal death; 2 on any spawn failure (predicate mismatch, all candidates refused, exec failure); 1 on usage error.

Out of scope:
- Originator/executor state machines (Task 6).
- End-to-end integration tests (Task 9).
- Failure-mode UX polish for SpawnDeny -> CLI rendering (Task 8).
- Plan-03 predicate parser (lives in classic-place).

## Design slice

> ```
> classic spawn --requires "<predicate>" [--rank "<expr>"] [--exclusive-device] \
>               [--stdin <file>] [--env KEY=VAL]... -- <argv>...
> ```
>
> - `--requires <expr>` (REQUIRED) — plan-03 predicate. Empty string forbidden; use `true` for "any node".
> - `--rank <expr>` — optional rank; default `least_loaded` (plan 03).
> - `--exclusive-device` — mark every acquired device cap exclusive (default: shared).
> - `--stdin <file>` — stream `<file>` as child stdin; `-` means inherit the CLI's stdin.
> - `--env KEY=VAL` — repeatable; passed verbatim. CLI's own env is **not** forwarded.
> - `--` then `<argv>...` — mandatory separator; everything after is the child's argv.
>
> CLI exit code: child's exit code on normal exit (0-255); `128 + signum` on signal death; `2` on any spawn failure; `1` on CLI usage error.

> CLI <-> local daemon: Unix domain socket (`/run/classicd/control.sock`, fallback `$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/classicd.sock`) carrying the same `Frame` codec from `classic-proto`.

## Inputs

- Task 5: spawn frames + classic-node SpawnDispatcher hook for the control mux.
- Task 6: originator state machine (the CLI talks to it via control socket).
- Existing `classic-cli` from plan 01 (clap setup, CLI entry point).
- `classic-proto` Frame codec.

## Outputs

Files to create/modify:
- `crates/classic-cli/src/cmd/spawn.rs` (NEW): clap parser; control-socket dial; SpawnRequest construction with `req_id` chosen by CLI; stdio relay loop; exit code translation.
- `crates/classic-cli/src/bin/classic.rs` (MOD): wire `spawn` subcommand and a `--connect <path>` flag for the control socket (default the platform paths above).
- `crates/classic-cli/src/cmd/mod.rs` (MOD): re-export.
- `crates/classic-node/src/control_socket.rs` (NEW): UDS listener that accepts connections and adapts each into a `Connection` for the FrameMux; SpawnDispatcher (from Task 5) is mounted here too.
- `crates/classic-node/src/lib.rs` (MOD): bring-up wires the control socket on daemon start; `--listen` and config knob for the UDS path.

## Test plan

`cargo test -p classic-cli`:
- Arg parsing — every error case yields exit 1 with the expected message:
  - missing `--requires`
  - empty `--requires ""`
  - missing `--` separator
  - empty argv after `--`
  - malformed `--env` (`FOO` without `=`)
- `--stdin -` and `--stdin <file>` both parse; differ in `StdinKind` value sent.
- Non-error parse: full example from the plan ("train.py with --exclusive-device") parses to expected `SpawnRequest` field values.

`cargo test -p classic-node` (control socket):
- UDS listener creates the socket file at the configured path; permission 0600 (matches NodeId convention from plan 01).
- A frame written to the UDS reaches the SpawnDispatcher.
- Two CLIs connecting concurrently each get their own SpawnRegistry slot (no req_id collision when CLIs choose req_id randomly).

End-to-end (requires Tasks 4-6 done; covered fully in Task 9):
- `classic spawn --requires "true" -- /bin/echo hi` exits 0 and prints `hi`.
- `--stdin <file>` round-trips through `/bin/cat`.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] AC-T7.1: `classic spawn --help` lists every documented flag with the documented semantics.
- [ ] AC-T7.2: Empty `--requires` and missing `--` produce exit-1 with a clear usage error.
- [ ] AC-T7.3: CLI dials the control socket from `/run/classicd/control.sock` (or `$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/classicd.sock` fallback), or the path supplied with `--connect`.
- [ ] AC-T7.4: SpawnRequest sent to the daemon contains exactly: `req_id` (CLI-chosen), `requires`, `rank`, `argv`, `env`, `exclusive_device`, `stdin_kind`, `hop=0`.
- [ ] AC-T7.5: Stdout/stderr from incoming ChildStdio frames is written to the CLI's stdout/stderr; an empty-data ChildStdio frame is treated as EOF.
- [ ] AC-T7.6: Exit code translation per plan: 0-255 on ChildExit{code:Some}; 128+sig on ChildExit{signal:Some}; 2 on terminal SpawnDeny; 1 on usage error.
- [ ] AC-T7.7: Two concurrent CLIs against the same daemon work without req_id collision (each chooses its own random req_id).
- [ ] AC-T7.8: classicd creates the control socket on startup with mode 0600 (per plan 01 convention).

## Intra-epic dependencies

- Parent: classic-1j3.
- Prereqs (within this epic): Task 5 (frames + dispatcher), Task 6 (state machines). Task 4 also implicit because Task 6 depends on it.
- Blocks: Task 8 (failure-mode handling — CLI must render SpawnDeny), Task 9 (integration tests need CLI).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] AC-T7.1: `classic spawn --help` lists every documented flag with documented semantics.
- [ ] AC-T7.2: Empty `--requires` and missing `--` produce exit-1 with a clear usage error.
- [ ] AC-T7.3: CLI dials `/run/classicd/control.sock` (or `$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/classicd.sock` fallback), or the path supplied via `--connect`.
- [ ] AC-T7.4: SpawnRequest sent to the daemon contains exactly: req_id (CLI-chosen), requires, rank, argv, env, exclusive_device, stdin_kind, hop=0.
- [ ] AC-T7.5: Stdout/stderr from ChildStdio frames is written to CLI stdout/stderr; empty-data frame == EOF.
- [ ] AC-T7.6: Exit-code translation per plan: 0-255 on ChildExit{code:Some}; 128+sig on ChildExit{signal:Some}; 2 on terminal SpawnDeny; 1 on usage error.
- [ ] AC-T7.7: Two concurrent CLIs against the same daemon work without req_id collision.
- [ ] AC-T7.8: classicd creates the control socket on startup with mode 0600 (per plan 01 convention).

title: Human sign-off checklist + troubleshooting README expansion
description:
Part of epic classic-o4c (Multi-node demo + integration tests).

Plan: plans/08-multinode-demo.md @ commit a7b6585. Read with:
  git show a7b6585:plans/08-multinode-demo.md

## Reading first
- git show a7b6585:plans/08-multinode-demo.md §Acceptance criteria (AC-5, AC-7, AC-8)
- git show a7b6585:plans/08-multinode-demo.md §Definition of multi-node demo success — human sign-off checklist

## Excerpted design slice

### From plan §Acceptance criteria
- AC-5: All five scenarios pass on real hardware on the documented topology (signed off via the human checklist below).
- AC-7: examples/multinode/README.md walks a fresh developer from `git clone` to a green `make scenario-1` with no undocumented steps (validated by a colleague new to the project).
- AC-8: README's troubleshooting section names at least: peer unreachable, NVML missing, GPU passthrough not configured, libnvidia-ml ABI mismatch, port 7421 firewalled, hostname not resolvable from peers.

### From plan §Definition of multi-node demo success — human sign-off checklist
A reviewer signs the demo off only after running, on real hardware, all of the following and recording the output in the PR description:
- [ ] `make -C examples/multinode setup` returned 0 and listed all three peers as reachable.
- [ ] `make scenario-1` printed OK; captured stdout contained `hello from gpu 0 on node-b`.
- [ ] `make scenario-2` printed OK; CLI exit non-zero; `classic ps --all` on every node shows no record of the rejected task.
- [ ] `make scenario-3` printed OK; coordinator received exactly one `hello` from a NetId whose node component matches node-c's NodeId.
- [ ] `make scenario-4` printed OK; the pci_lspci_loop BDF set matches `lspci -n` on node-b.
- [ ] `make scenario-5` printed OK; `classic ps --json` shows both members placed_node = node-b with >= 500 ms time overlap.
- [ ] `cargo xtask demo-smoke` passes locally as well.
- [ ] `make clean` returns the tree to no leftover Compose volumes or built binaries.

## Inputs
- examples/multinode/README.md (skeleton from classic-lqx) — extend troubleshooting section
- examples/multinode/Makefile (from classic-d2p, extended by all scenario tasks and classic-dpk) — referenced by the checklist
- All five scenarios working (classic-d2p, classic-qdy, classic-3ab, classic-wpq, classic-5xc)
- xtask demo-smoke working (classic-dpk)

## Outputs
Files to create / modify:
- examples/multinode/README.md — append a 'Sign-off Checklist' section with the verbatim checklist above (markdown task list). Expand the 'Troubleshooting' section to cover, in detail, with diagnosis steps and remedies:
    1. Peer unreachable (firewall, classicd not running, wrong listen address)
    2. NVML missing on node-b (libnvidia-ml.so.1 not on linker path; `ldconfig -p | grep nvidia-ml` diagnosis)
    3. GPU passthrough not configured (NVIDIA Container Toolkit / nvidia-cdi for containerized runs; `nvidia-smi` baseline check)
    4. libnvidia-ml ABI mismatch (driver vs. NVML version skew; check `nvidia-smi --query-gpu=driver_version`)
    5. Port 7421 firewalled (`ss -ltn | grep 7421`; `nc -vz <peer> 7421` from each peer)
    6. Hostname not resolvable from peers (`getent hosts node-b`; /etc/hosts vs. DNS fallback)
- (Optional) docs/PR-template snippet — but only if a PR template already exists at .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md or similar; otherwise leave the checklist living solely inside examples/multinode/README.md so the PR author copy-pastes it.

## Test plan
- Manual review: a colleague new to the project reads README from top to bottom; reports zero undocumented steps required to reach a green `make scenario-1` (AC-7).
- Lint: `markdownlint` (if used in repo) on examples/multinode/README.md — clean.
- Sign-off rehearsal: on real hardware, run through the entire checklist top-to-bottom; record outputs in a draft PR description; confirm every box can be ticked from the documented commands alone (no implicit knowledge required).
- Negative coverage: temporarily break one of the troubleshooting trigger conditions (e.g. close port 7421 with iptables); confirm the README's diagnosis steps actually identify the cause.

## Task-specific Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] examples/multinode/README.md contains a 'Sign-off Checklist' section verbatim from plan §Definition of multi-node demo success.
- [ ] examples/multinode/README.md 'Troubleshooting' section names AND provides diagnosis + remedy steps for: peer unreachable, NVML missing, GPU passthrough not configured, libnvidia-ml ABI mismatch, port 7421 firewalled, hostname not resolvable from peers.
- [ ] A colleague new to the project reads README and reports no undocumented steps to a green `make scenario-1` (AC-7 — manual sign-off).
- [ ] If a repo PR template exists, it includes a placeholder for the sign-off output; otherwise the README is the source of truth.

## Non-obvious context
- AC-7 ('validated by a colleague new to the project') is explicitly a human review gate — the task is not closed until a non-author runs through it. Note that in the bead history.
- The sign-off checklist is the bridge between automated CI (smoke runs scenarios 1, 2, 3) and the human-validated real-hardware path (scenarios 4 and 5). It is the contract for what 'demo green' means at PR time.
- AC-9/AC-10 are verified during PR review by inspecting cargo metadata + classic-proto::FrameKind diff — call those out in the README's checklist as auxiliary checks the reviewer should run.

## Intra-epic deps
- Depends on classic-lqx (README skeleton)
- Depends on classic-d2p, classic-qdy, classic-3ab, classic-wpq, classic-5xc (all five scenarios must work for the checklist to be meaningfully runnable)
- Depends on classic-dpk (xtask demo-smoke must work for the local-CI checkbox to be runnable)
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] examples/multinode/README.md contains a 'Sign-off Checklist' section verbatim from plan §Definition of multi-node demo success.
- [ ] README 'Troubleshooting' section provides diagnosis + remedy for peer unreachable, NVML missing, GPU passthrough not configured, libnvidia-ml ABI mismatch, port 7421 firewalled, hostname not resolvable from peers.
- [ ] A colleague new to the project validates README produces a green `make scenario-1` with no undocumented steps (AC-7 manual sign-off).
- [ ] If a repo PR template exists, it points to the README sign-off section; else README is the canonical home.

title: classic-spawn: originator + executor state machines (originate + execute)
description:
# Task 6 — Originator + Executor state machines (`classic_spawn::originate` / `classic_spawn::execute`)

Parent epic: classic-1j3 (Feature: Spawn pipeline). Plan: plans/04-spawn-pipeline.md @ commit a7b6585. Read with: git show a7b6585:plans/04-spawn-pipeline.md.

## Reading first

Read plans/04-spawn-pipeline.md sections "Sequence: remote-spawn happy path", "Originator state machine", and "`classic-spawn` public API" in full. Re-read "Failure handling" — this task implements the table.

## Scope

Implement the originator and executor state machines in `crates/classic-spawn`. This is where the frames from Task 5 turn into actual spawn-pipeline behaviour:
- Originator: receive SpawnRequest from CLI, call `place()` (plan 03), iterate the ranked candidate list, dial each peer, forward SpawnRequest, on SpawnAck enter Running and relay stdio + ChildExit to the CLI; on SpawnDeny try next or surface terminal denial.
- Executor: receive SpawnRequest from a peer, allocate MboxId, acquire caps via `CapBroker`, build `ScopeGuard`, call Task 4's `exec_in_scope`, frame stdout/stderr into `ChildStdio` frames, forward `ChildExit` on completion. Drop everything on originator disconnect.

In scope:
- `crates/classic-spawn/src/originator.rs` (NEW) — async `originate()` entry point, full state machine (`Submitted -> Placing -> Trying(idx) -> Running(net_id) -> Done | Denied`).
- `crates/classic-spawn/src/executor.rs` (NEW) — async `execute()` entry point: predicate revalidation against local ad, cap acquire, scope create, exec, stdio relay, exit propagation.
- `OriginatorCtx`, `ExecutorCtx` per plan API.
- Loop guard: `hop > 2` -> SpawnDeny{HopExceeded}.
- Originator-disconnect handling: when peer connection drops or CLI closes the control socket, executor calls `release_all`, SIGTERMs the child, SIGKILLs after 10s.
- Implements (replaces) the SpawnDispatcher stubs from Task 5.
- Self-targeted spawn: when `place()` returns the local node, run `execute()` in-process on the originator side (no peer hop) using `OriginatorCtx::local_executor`.

Out of scope:
- CLI (Task 7).
- Frame definitions (Task 5).
- exec/cgroup/cap primitives (Task 4).
- Exhaustive failure-mode UX polish — that's Task 8.

## Design slice

> Originator state machine per outstanding spawn:
>
> ```
> Submitted -> Placing -> Trying(idx) -> Running(net_id)
>                    \\          |
>                     `------>  Denied(reason)
> ```
>
> `Trying(idx)` cycles through the ranked candidate list; `SpawnDeny` goes to `Trying(idx+1)` or, if exhausted, `Denied(AllCandidatesRefused)`. `SpawnAck` -> `Running`; thereafter stdio / exit frames are relayed verbatim between the CLI socket and the chosen executor connection.

> ```rust
> pub async fn originate(ctx: &OriginatorCtx, req: SpawnRequest, cli: ControlConn)
>     -> Result<(), SpawnError>;
> pub async fn execute(ctx: &ExecutorCtx, req: SpawnRequest, peer: PeerConn)
>     -> Result<(), SpawnError>;
> pub struct OriginatorCtx {
>     pub ad_cache: Arc<AdCache>, pub peers: Arc<PeerSet>,
>     pub local_node: NodeId, pub local_executor: ExecutorCtx,
> }
> pub struct ExecutorCtx {
>     pub broker: Arc<CapBroker>, pub mbox_alloc: Arc<MboxAllocator>,
>     pub local_node: NodeId,
> }
> ```

Failure-mapping table from the plan §"Failure handling" governs which DenyReason maps to retry vs terminal. Implement the table exactly. Specifically: retry on `PredicateNotSatisfied`, `DeviceTaken`, `CgroupSetupFailed`, and connect failures; never on `ExecFailed` or `HopExceeded`. (Task 8 will add e2e coverage of the fallback.)

## Inputs

- Task 1-3: `CapBroker`, cgroup helpers, `BpfLoader`/`ProgHandle`.
- Task 4: `exec_in_scope`, `ScopeGuard`, `ChildHandle`, `MboxAllocator`.
- Task 5: spawn frames + SpawnDispatcher hook.
- Plan 03 (filed elsewhere): `place(ads, requires, rank) -> Vec<NodeId>` — assume the API exists; if not yet present, keep the call to a small trait `Placer` that the test suite mocks and the bring-up code wires to plan-03's implementation.
- Plan 02 (filed elsewhere): AdCache trait — mock for unit tests.

## Outputs

Files to create/modify:
- `crates/classic-spawn/src/originator.rs` — `pub async fn originate(...)`. Internal state types `OriginatorState` mirroring the diagram; helper for "dial peer, send SpawnRequest, await terminal frame".
- `crates/classic-spawn/src/executor.rs` — `pub async fn execute(...)`. Includes the predicate-revalidation step (re-check the local ad with the requires predicate; mismatch -> SpawnDeny{PredicateNotSatisfied}).
- `crates/classic-spawn/src/lib.rs` (MOD) — wire `originate`/`execute` as public; provide `dispatch_frame(...)` impls so `classic-node`'s SpawnDispatcher can route inbound frames to in-flight state machines (req_id-keyed).
- `crates/classic-spawn/src/orchestrator.rs` (NEW, optional) — a small per-daemon `SpawnRegistry` mapping `req_id -> mpsc::Sender<Frame>` so dispatcher can route by req_id.
- `crates/classic-node/src/lib.rs` (MOD) — install the registry into the SpawnDispatcher from Task 5; replace stubs with real calls.
- `crates/classic-spawn/src/error.rs` — `SpawnError` enum covering every DenyReason variant + transport errors.

## Test plan

Unit (mocked transport, no real cgroup/BPF):
- Originator state machine: scripted `PeerSet` returning SpawnAck on candidate idx=1 after candidate idx=0 returned SpawnDeny{DeviceTaken} -> assert state transitions `Trying(0) -> Trying(1) -> Running`.
- Originator: SpawnDeny{ExecFailed} on idx=0 -> NO retry; reason returned to CLI.
- Originator: hop=3 incoming on the originator path is rejected before forwarding (programming-error guard).
- Originator: empty `place()` result -> SpawnDeny{NoCandidates} sent to CLI.
- Executor: stale predicate (mocked AdCache says no-match) -> SpawnDeny{PredicateNotSatisfied}; broker.acquire NOT called.
- Executor: broker.acquire returns Taken -> SpawnDeny{DeviceTaken}; no scope created.
- Executor: ScopeError::CgroupSetup -> SpawnDeny{CgroupSetupFailed, detail:<errno>}; caps released.
- Executor: ExecError::Exec(ENOENT) -> SpawnDeny{ExecFailed}; caps + scope released.
- Self-target: `local_node == place()[0]` -> `execute()` runs on originator's side without a peer dial.
- Originator-disconnect kill path: drop the CLI ControlConn while child is running -> executor SIGTERMs child; assert `ChildExit{signal: Some(15)}` is emitted within 1s of disconnect; SIGKILL fallback after 10s.

Integration (single-process loopback per plan testing plan):
- Two `classicd`s in one process via `tokio::LocalSet` and an in-memory `Connection` impl from `classic-proto` test utils. Drive a SpawnRequest through the full pipeline with a no-op CapBroker and stubbed BPF; expect SpawnAck + ChildExit.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] AC-T6.1: `cargo test -p classic-spawn` green; the state-machine unit tests above all pass.
- [ ] AC-T6.2: Originator retries match the failure table: retry on `{PredicateNotSatisfied, DeviceTaken, CgroupSetupFailed, ConnectFail}`, terminal on `{ExecFailed, HopExceeded}`.
- [ ] AC-T6.3: Self-targeted spawn (local node is chosen executor) runs `execute()` in-process without a peer hop.
- [ ] AC-T6.4: Originator-disconnect SIGTERMs the child within 1s; SIGKILLs after 10s if still alive (matches plan AC-8).
- [ ] AC-T6.5: SpawnDispatcher in classic-node routes inbound frames to in-flight state machines by `req_id`.
- [ ] AC-T6.6: `hop > 2` is dropped with `HopExceeded` (matches plan AC implicit; see plan FR-10).
- [ ] AC-T6.7: Single-process loopback integration test exercises every spawn frame with no real cgroup/BPF.

## Intra-epic dependencies

- Parent: classic-1j3.
- Prereqs (within this epic): Task 4 (classic-spawn-core), Task 5 (frames + dispatcher hook).
- Blocks: Task 7 (CLI — needs working originator), Task 8 (failure-mode handling tests need the state machine), Task 9 (integration tests).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] AC-T6.1: `cargo test -p classic-spawn` green; state-machine unit tests pass.
- [ ] AC-T6.2: Originator retries match the failure table: retry on {PredicateNotSatisfied, DeviceTaken, CgroupSetupFailed, ConnectFail}; terminal on {ExecFailed, HopExceeded}.
- [ ] AC-T6.3: Self-targeted spawn (local node is chosen executor) runs `execute()` in-process without a peer hop.
- [ ] AC-T6.4: Originator-disconnect SIGTERMs the child within 1s; SIGKILLs after 10s (plan AC-8).
- [ ] AC-T6.5: SpawnDispatcher in classic-node routes inbound frames to in-flight state machines by `req_id`.
- [ ] AC-T6.6: `hop > 2` is dropped with `HopExceeded` (plan FR-10).
- [ ] AC-T6.7: Single-process loopback integration test exercises every spawn frame with no real cgroup/BPF.

title: cargo xtask demo-smoke: Compose harness + NVML stub + CI integration
description:
Part of epic classic-o4c (Multi-node demo + integration tests).

Plan: plans/08-multinode-demo.md @ commit a7b6585. Read with:
  git show a7b6585:plans/08-multinode-demo.md

## Reading first
- git show a7b6585:plans/08-multinode-demo.md §Design / CI Compose layout
- git show a7b6585:plans/08-multinode-demo.md §Testing plan / End-to-end smoke (CI)
- git show a7b6585:plans/08-multinode-demo.md §Acceptance criteria (AC-6: smoke passes scenarios 1, 2, 3 in <= 90 s wall time)
- git show a7b6585:plans/02-node-ad-hw-discovery.md (which NVML symbols are called — confirm the stub list)

## Excerpted design slice

### From plan §Design / CI Compose layout
examples/multinode/ci/compose.yaml — three classicd containers (node-a 10.42.0.10, node-b 10.42.0.11, node-c 10.42.0.12) plus a driver container. node-b sets LD_PRELOAD=/usr/local/lib/nvml-stub.so and CLASSIC_NVML_STUB=1. cap_add: [SYS_ADMIN, SYS_RESOURCE]; security_opt: [seccomp:unconfined]; subnet 10.42.0.0/24.

Dockerfile.node — thin Ubuntu 24.04 image with classicd, classic, the demo binaries, and nvml-stub.so copied into place.

NVML stub exposes: nvmlInit_v2, nvmlDeviceGetCount_v2, nvmlDeviceGetHandleByIndex_v2, nvmlDeviceGetName, nvmlDeviceGetMemoryInfo_v2, nvmlDeviceGetUUID, nvmlShutdown — reports a single fake 24 GiB device.

### From plan §Design / Architecture
xtask/src/
├── main.rs                        # MODIFIED — registers demo-smoke
├── demo_smoke.rs                  # NEW — orchestrates compose + smoke.sh
└── nvml_stub.rs                   # NEW — builds nvml-stub.so for CI

`cargo xtask demo-smoke` chain: build release binaries -> build nvml-stub.so and host-only cuda_hello -> docker compose up --build -d -> compose exec driver smoke.sh -> compose down -v -> exit with the inner code.

### From plan §Testing plan / End-to-end smoke (CI)
`cargo xtask demo-smoke` runs scenarios 1, 2, 3 inside the Compose stack. Scenarios 4 and 5 are NOT run.

### From plan §Acceptance criteria
- AC-6: `cargo xtask demo-smoke` passes scenarios 1, 2, 3 in CI in <= 90 s wall time.
- AC-9: No new crates added (cargo metadata workspace-member diff is empty).

## Inputs
- examples/multinode/lib/assert.sh (from classic-lqx)
- examples/multinode/config/{node-a,node-b,node-c}.toml (from classic-lqx)
- examples/multinode/programs/cuda_hello (from classic-sea) — host-only stub mode for CI
- examples/multinode/scenarios/{01-hw-affinity,02-no-match,03-service-lookup}.sh (from classic-d2p, classic-qdy, classic-3ab)
- Existing xtask crate (workspace member; see existing xtask/src/main.rs structure if present, or extend the existing xtask)
- classic, classicd binaries built in release mode by the xtask
- Docker / docker compose v2 on the CI runner

## Outputs
Files to create / modify:
- examples/multinode/ci/compose.yaml — content per plan §Design / CI Compose layout, byte-equivalent.
- examples/multinode/ci/Dockerfile.node — Ubuntu 24.04 base, copies classicd + classic from a build stage, copies cuda_hello stub + pci_lspci_loop + nvml-stub.so into /usr/local/{bin,lib}.
- examples/multinode/ci/nvml-stub.c — implements the seven NVML symbols listed; reports a single fake 24 GiB NVIDIA device. Buildable to nvml-stub.so via `cc -shared -fPIC -o nvml-stub.so nvml-stub.c`.
- examples/multinode/ci/smoke.sh — runs scenarios 01, 02, 03 from inside the driver container with CLASSIC_PEER=10.42.0.10:7421; aggregates results; exits with non-zero on any scenario failure.
- xtask/src/demo_smoke.rs (NEW) — orchestrates: cargo build --release -p classic-node -p classic-cli; build cuda_hello stub via CLASSIC_NVML_STUB=1 make; build nvml-stub.so via cc; docker compose -f examples/multinode/ci/compose.yaml up --build -d; docker compose exec driver /workspace/examples/multinode/ci/smoke.sh; capture exit code; docker compose down -v; return inner exit.
- xtask/src/nvml_stub.rs (NEW) — small helper module that knows where nvml-stub.c lives and how to compile it.
- xtask/src/main.rs (MODIFIED) — registers `demo-smoke` subcommand.
- examples/multinode/Makefile — add a `smoke` target that delegates to `cargo xtask demo-smoke`; ensure `make clean` removes Compose volumes/networks (`docker compose -f ci/compose.yaml down -v --remove-orphans` style).

## Test plan
- Unit: `cargo build -p xtask` — compiles clean.
- Stub link test: `cc -shared -fPIC -o /tmp/nvml-stub.so examples/multinode/ci/nvml-stub.c` — produces a valid .so with the seven required symbols. Verify with `nm -D /tmp/nvml-stub.so | grep nvml`.
- Compose dry-run: `docker compose -f examples/multinode/ci/compose.yaml config` — parses cleanly.
- End-to-end (requires Docker): `cargo xtask demo-smoke` from a fresh tree — exits 0; total wall time <= 90 s on a 4-core runner; no leftover containers or volumes after the run.
- Negative: stop one of the daemons inside compose mid-run; assert the smoke script exits non-zero.
- CI wiring: `cargo xtask demo-smoke` is invoked from CI on every PR; failures gate the merge. (Wiring of CI YAML itself is part of this task — extend existing CI or document the pipeline addition.)

## Task-specific Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] examples/multinode/ci/{compose.yaml, Dockerfile.node, nvml-stub.c, smoke.sh} all exist with content matching plan §Design / CI Compose layout.
- [ ] nvml-stub.c compiles cleanly via `cc -shared -fPIC` and exposes nvmlInit_v2, nvmlDeviceGetCount_v2, nvmlDeviceGetHandleByIndex_v2, nvmlDeviceGetName, nvmlDeviceGetMemoryInfo_v2, nvmlDeviceGetUUID, nvmlShutdown reporting a single fake 24 GiB device.
- [ ] xtask/src/demo_smoke.rs and nvml_stub.rs are NEW; xtask/src/main.rs is MODIFIED to register `demo-smoke`.
- [ ] `cargo xtask demo-smoke` builds release binaries, builds the stub, runs Compose up + smoke.sh + down, exits with the inner code; total wall time <= 90 s on a 4-core runner.
- [ ] smoke.sh runs scenarios 01, 02, 03 (NOT 04, 05); aggregates and exits with non-zero on any failure.
- [ ] examples/multinode/Makefile gains a `smoke` target that delegates to `cargo xtask demo-smoke`; `make clean` removes Compose volumes, networks, and orphan containers.
- [ ] CI is wired to invoke `cargo xtask demo-smoke` on every PR; failures gate merge.
- [ ] No new crates in the cargo workspace (xtask is presumed to already exist; this task only adds modules to it).

## Non-obvious context
- The xtask crate must already exist in the workspace. If it does NOT, file a follow-up bead — do NOT add a new workspace member here (epic AC-9 forbids new crates). The task description assumes plan 01 / classic-fxu carries an xtask stub.
- nvml-stub.so is loaded via LD_PRELOAD on node-b only; it is NOT used by cuda_hello (which has its own CLASSIC_NVML_STUB=1 host-only build path from classic-sea).
- Compose subnet 10.42.0.0/24 is fixed — must match config/*.toml peer addresses verbatim.
- 90 s wall-time budget includes Docker build, compose up, three scenario runs, compose down. Use `docker compose up --build -d` (parallel) and pre-cache base images where possible.
- The plan's compose.yaml shows `build: { context: ../../.., dockerfile: examples/multinode/ci/Dockerfile.node }` — the xtask is responsible for ensuring the build context (repo root) contains the just-built classic + classicd binaries, either by COPY from a build stage or via volume mount.

## Intra-epic deps
- Depends on classic-lqx (configs + assert.sh + directory layout)
- Depends on classic-sea (cuda_hello stub mode used inside Compose)
- Depends on classic-d2p (scenario 1 + Makefile skeleton)
- Depends on classic-qdy (scenario 2)
- Depends on classic-3ab (scenario 3)
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] examples/multinode/ci/{compose.yaml, Dockerfile.node, nvml-stub.c, smoke.sh} exist matching plan §Design / CI Compose layout.
- [ ] nvml-stub.c compiles cleanly via `cc -shared -fPIC` and exposes the seven NVML symbols reporting one fake 24 GiB device.
- [ ] xtask gains demo-smoke subcommand (xtask/src/{demo_smoke.rs,nvml_stub.rs} NEW; main.rs MODIFIED). No new workspace crates.
- [ ] `cargo xtask demo-smoke` runs scenarios 1, 2, 3 in Compose with NVML stubbed and exits with the inner code; total wall time <= 90 s on a 4-core runner.
- [ ] Makefile gains `smoke` (delegates to xtask); `make clean` removes Compose volumes/networks/orphans.
- [ ] CI invokes `cargo xtask demo-smoke` on every PR.

title: classic-proto + classic-node: spawn frames 0x0300-0x0304 + dispatcher registration
description:
# Task 5 — Spawn protocol frames (`SpawnRequest`/`SpawnAck`/`SpawnDeny`/`ChildStdio`/`ChildExit`) + remote-side handler registration

Parent epic: classic-1j3 (Feature: Spawn pipeline). Plan: plans/04-spawn-pipeline.md @ commit a7b6585. Read with: git show a7b6585:plans/04-spawn-pipeline.md.

## Reading first

Read plans/04-spawn-pipeline.md "Data shapes" subsection in full. Read plans/ARCHITECTURE.md "Frame-kind allocation" + "Wire transport" sections — note the `0x0300-0x03FF` range owned by spawn. Read crates/classic-proto for how plan 01 frames are declared (FrameKind enum, bincode v2 encode/decode, Frame struct).

## Scope

Define the spawn frames in `classic-proto`, register `0x0300-0x0304` in the FrameKind enum, expose round-trippable encode/decode, and wire a `FrameHandler` registration on the peer mux + control socket in `classic-node` so spawn frames reach future handlers (Task 6's `originate`/`execute` are stubs at this point — they'll be implemented in Task 6).

In scope:
- `crates/classic-proto/src/frames/spawn.rs` (NEW) — define `SpawnRequest`, `SpawnAck`, `SpawnDeny`, `ChildStdio`, `ChildExit` per plan; enums `StdinKind`, `DenyReason`, `StdioStream`. All derive `Serialize, Deserialize, Debug, Clone` (and `Copy` on the simple enums); bincode v2 fixed-int LE.
- `crates/classic-proto/src/lib.rs` (MOD) — register `FrameKind::SpawnRequest=0x0300`, `SpawnAck=0x0301`, `SpawnDeny=0x0302`, ChildStdio=0x0303, ChildExit=0x0304.
- `crates/classic-node/src/lib.rs` (MOD) — register a stub `FrameHandler` for the `0x03` high-byte range on the FrameMux from plan 01; same handler attached to the local control-socket frame mux. Stub forwards to a not-yet-implemented `classic_spawn::dispatch_frame` (a function declared with a TODO body that Task 6 fills in) and returns Ok.

Out of scope:
- The control-socket bring-up for the CLI (Task 7).
- The originator/executor state machine logic (Task 6) — only handler registration here.
- Full ad/peer wiring beyond what's needed for the dispatch stub.

## Design slice (from plans/04-spawn-pipeline.md)

> Frames live in `crates/classic-proto/src/frames/spawn.rs`. All encoded with `bincode` v2 (fixed-int, little-endian) per ARCHITECTURE.md.
>
> | Kind     | Frame          | Direction                                                          |
> |----------|----------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------|
> | `0x0300` | `SpawnRequest` | CLI -> originator; originator -> executor (forwarded).             |
> | `0x0301` | `SpawnAck`     | Executor -> originator -> CLI.                                     |
> | `0x0302` | `SpawnDeny`    | Executor -> originator (per candidate); originator -> CLI (terminal). |
> | `0x0303` | `ChildStdio`   | Bidirectional, relayed.                                            |
> | `0x0304` | `ChildExit`    | Executor -> originator -> CLI.                                     |
>
> 0x0305-0x03FF reserved.

```rust
pub struct SpawnRequest {
    pub req_id: u64,
    pub requires: String,             // plan-03 predicate; "" forbidden, use "true"
    pub rank: String,                 // "" == default (least-loaded)
    pub argv: Vec<String>,
    pub env: Vec<String>,             // KEY=VALUE; CLI's own env NOT forwarded
    pub exclusive_device: bool,
    pub stdin_kind: Option<StdinKind>,// None == /dev/null on stdin
    pub hop: u8,                      // dropped if > 2
}
pub enum StdinKind { Inherit, File }

pub struct SpawnAck { pub req_id: u64, pub net_id: NetId }

pub struct SpawnDeny {
    pub req_id: u64,
    pub reason: DenyReason,
    pub detail: String,
}
pub enum DenyReason {
    NoCandidates, AllCandidatesRefused, PredicateNotSatisfied, DeviceTaken,
    CgroupSetupFailed, ExecFailed, HopExceeded, Internal,
}

pub struct ChildStdio {
    pub req_id: u64,
    pub stream: StdioStream,
    pub data: Vec<u8>,                // empty == EOF
}
pub enum StdioStream { Stdin, Stdout, Stderr }

pub struct ChildExit {
    pub req_id: u64,
    pub code: Option<i32>,
    pub signal: Option<i32>,
}
```

## Inputs

- `crates/classic-proto/src/lib.rs` — extend `FrameKind` with the 5 new variants.
- Plan 01 `Frame`/`FrameKind`/codec utilities for adding new variants.
- `classic-node` — its FrameMux (or equivalent dispatcher) where handlers are registered.

## Outputs

Files to create/modify:
- `crates/classic-proto/src/frames/spawn.rs` (NEW): all 5 frame structs + `StdinKind`, `DenyReason`, `StdioStream` enums. Encode/decode via bincode v2 helpers from plan 01.
- `crates/classic-proto/src/frames/mod.rs` (MOD): `pub mod spawn;`
- `crates/classic-proto/src/lib.rs` (MOD): extend `FrameKind` enum with 0x0300..=0x0304; ensure unknown-kind round-trip preserved per plan 01.
- `crates/classic-node/src/spawn_dispatch.rs` (NEW): `pub struct SpawnDispatcher; impl FrameHandler for SpawnDispatcher { ... }` that decodes by FrameKind and calls into `classic_spawn::dispatch_*` stubs (declared with `unimplemented!()` until Task 6).
- `crates/classic-node/src/lib.rs` (MOD): register SpawnDispatcher on both peer mux and (later) control socket; expose a constructor for the bring-up code in classicd.

## Test plan

`cargo test -p classic-proto`:
- Round-trip encode/decode for each of the 5 frames with non-trivial values (non-empty argv/env, large stdio at the 1 MiB-stream bound, hop=2, every DenyReason variant).
- Edge cases: empty argv (allowed by codec — rejected at the spawn layer in Task 7), max stdio data size at the frame-size cap (16 MiB total per ARCHITECTURE.md), `Option<StdinKind>` both `None` and `Some(...)`.
- Wire-byte fixture: spot-check at least one SpawnRequest's encoded bytes against a recorded golden vector to lock the codec (catches accidental field-order/encoding changes).

`cargo test -p classic-node`:
- A test injects a frame with `kind=0x0300` into the peer mux; the SpawnDispatcher is invoked and dispatches to the stub (which can panic-on-unimplemented or record-to-mock).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] AC-T5.1: `cargo build -p classic-proto` and `cargo test -p classic-proto` green.
- [ ] AC-T5.2: All 5 spawn frames round-trip byte-equal under bincode v2 (matches plan AC-1: "no frame outside this range is owned by spawn").
- [ ] AC-T5.3: `FrameKind` enum has variants for exactly `0x0300..=0x0304` in the spawn range; nothing in `0x0305..=0x03FF` is allocated.
- [ ] AC-T5.4: A goldenfile fixture pins the SpawnRequest encoding (regression catch).
- [ ] AC-T5.5: `classic-node` registers a `SpawnDispatcher` for the `0x03` range on its peer mux and exposes a hook for the control-socket mux (used by Task 7).
- [ ] AC-T5.6: All handler entry points are typed against the public `Frame` API; no implicit raw-byte parsing in classic-node.

## Intra-epic dependencies

- Parent: classic-1j3.
- Prereqs (within this epic): Task 4 (classic-spawn-core) — the dispatcher stubs reference `classic_spawn` types (StdinKind in particular). If Task 4 isn't ready, this task can stub `StdinKind` locally and Task 4 redirects; cleaner ordering is Task 4 first.
- Blocks: Task 6 (state machines need the frames + dispatcher hook).

- [ ] AC-T5.2: All 5 spawn frames round-trip byte-equal under bincode v2 (plan AC-1: no frame outside 0x0300-0x0304 owned by spawn).
- [ ] AC-T5.3: `FrameKind` enum has variants for exactly `0x0300..=0x0304` in the spawn range; nothing in `0x0305..=0x03FF` is allocated.
- [ ] AC-T5.4: A goldenfile fixture pins the SpawnRequest encoding (regression catch).

title: Scenario 5 script: Placement group PACK (2 members, both land on node-b)
description:
Part of epic classic-o4c (Multi-node demo + integration tests).

Plan: plans/08-multinode-demo.md @ commit a7b6585. Read with:
  git show a7b6585:plans/08-multinode-demo.md

## Reading first
- git show a7b6585:plans/08-multinode-demo.md §Testing plan / Scenario 5 — Placement group PACK (real hardware only)
- git show a7b6585:plans/07-placement-groups.md (PACK strategy, --pack flag, group semantics)
- git show a7b6585:plans/04-spawn-pipeline.md (`classic ps --json` schema, including placed_node and timing fields)

## Excerpted design slice

### From plan §Testing plan / Scenario 5
Prerequisites: all daemons up; node-b GPU device-cap idle.
Command (on node-a):
  classic spawn --pack 2 \
    --requires 'any(gpu, gpu.vendor == 0x10de && gpu.vram_mb >= 8000)' \
    -- ./cuda_hello

Expected stdout: two `hello from gpu 0 on node-b` lines (any order).
Expected exit code: 0 for the group; both members 0.
Success means: plan 07 PACK strategy placed both on node-b; `classic ps --json` shows both placed_node = node-b with >= 500 ms start/end overlap.

### From plan §Acceptance criteria + sign-off checklist
- AC-5: All five scenarios pass on real hardware on the documented topology (signed off via the human checklist).
- Sign-off: `make scenario-5` printed OK; `classic ps --json` shows both members placed_node = node-b with >= 500 ms time overlap.

### From plan §Testing plan / End-to-end smoke
Scenarios 4 and 5 require real device files and a real PACK-able GPU, so they are listed under `make all-real-hardware` and not under `make smoke`.

## Inputs
- examples/multinode/lib/assert.sh (from classic-lqx)
- examples/multinode/Makefile (from classic-d2p) — extend with scenario-5 target wired under all-real-hardware
- examples/multinode/programs/cuda_hello (from classic-sea) — the binary spawned twice as a PACK group of 2
- classic CLI: `classic spawn --pack 2 --requires ... -- ...`, `classic ps --json`
- classicd peers up; node-b GPU device-cap idle
- CLASSIC_PEER env var
- jq for parsing classic ps --json output

## Outputs
Files to create / modify:
- examples/multinode/scenarios/05-pack-group.sh — shebang, executable, sources lib/assert.sh; checks CLASSIC_PEER set and jq is present; runs the pack spawn command above; captures stdout, stderr, exit; asserts group exit 0; asserts stdout contains exactly two `hello from gpu 0 on node-b` lines (any order); after the group exits, queries `classic ps --all --json` and asserts via jq that both members have placed_node = node-b and that the [start_time, end_time) intervals overlap by >= 500 ms; prints OK.
- examples/multinode/Makefile — add scenario-5 target; wire under all-real-hardware (NOT under smoke).

## Test plan
- bash -n examples/multinode/scenarios/05-pack-group.sh — syntax check.
- shellcheck examples/multinode/scenarios/05-pack-group.sh — clean.
- Real test (manual; requires real cluster with idle node-b GPU): `CLASSIC_PEER=10.42.0.10:7421 make -C examples/multinode scenario-5` — prints OK; manual cross-check that classic ps --json shows two members on node-b with >= 500 ms overlap.
- Negative: confirm scenario-5 is NOT triggered by `make smoke` or `cargo xtask demo-smoke`.

## Task-specific Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] examples/multinode/scenarios/05-pack-group.sh exists, is executable, shellcheck-clean.
- [ ] Script invokes `classic spawn --pack 2 --requires 'any(gpu, gpu.vendor == 0x10de && gpu.vram_mb >= 8000)' -- ./cuda_hello` exactly.
- [ ] Script asserts group exit 0; stdout has exactly two `hello from gpu 0 on node-b` lines.
- [ ] Script queries `classic ps --all --json` and asserts both members' placed_node = node-b and start/end intervals overlap by >= 500 ms (jq math acceptable).
- [ ] Makefile gains a scenario-5 target; wired under `all-real-hardware` and NOT under `smoke`.
- [ ] Script prints OK on success.

## Non-obvious context
- `--pack 2` is plan-07 surface; spelling open-question per plan §Open questions. If plan 07 ships a different flag spelling, this scenario updates in the same PR. If `--pack` is missing, file a follow-up bead and pin this scenario as xfail.
- 500 ms overlap requires reading start_time and end_time from `classic ps --all --json` — plan 04 must surface these fields in the JSON schema. If they are missing, file a follow-up bead.
- The PACK strategy is what makes this scenario interesting: with two members both requiring an NVIDIA >= 8 GiB GPU, naive scheduling would land one on node-b and refuse the second (no other matching node). PACK explicitly co-locates them on node-b — so the GPU device-cap must allow shared / oversubscribed access for the test, OR the spawn pipeline must serialize device-cap acquisition. title: classic-place: DSL grammar reference doc (plans/dsl-reference.md)
description:
Epic: classic-i0n (Feature: Placement predicates)
Plan: plans/03-placement-predicates.md @ commit a7b6585. Read with: git show a7b6585:plans/03-placement-predicates.md

## Reading first

1. Plan §Design "Grammar" subsection (PEG productions).
2. Plan §Examples (six example predicates with motivating workloads).
3. Plan §Error-message examples.
4. Plan §Open questions (label access syntax deferral, predicate text size cap, etc.).

## Why this task exists

The plan itself is the *design* doc — exhaustive, aimed at implementers and reviewers. Users writing predicates need a *reference* doc — terse, example-heavy, no rationale. The plan implicitly carries both audiences and is too long for the second one.

This task extracts a user-facing reference from the plan and commits it under `plans/dsl-reference.md`. The plan stays canonical for design choices; the reference is the surface a user lands on when they ask "how do I write a predicate?".

(Open question for human reviewer at filing time: should this live under `plans/` or `docs/`? The plan's §References section indicates the project uses `plans/` for design and lacks a top-level `docs/` tree as of commit a7b6585. Default: `plans/dsl-reference.md`. If the project grows a docs tree later, move with a redirect.)

## Inputs

- `plans/03-placement-predicates.md` @ a7b6585.

## Outputs

- New file `plans/dsl-reference.md`. Sections (in order):
  1. **At a glance** — one-paragraph elevator pitch + one example predicate.
  2. **Syntax** — terse grammar table (operators, precedence, literal forms) extracted from plan §Design "Grammar". No PEG; just a precedence table + per-construct examples.
  3. **Field reference** — the table from plan §Design "Data shapes" (NodeAd / GpuAd / PciAd field paths and DSL types). Include "only valid inside any/all/count" annotations.
  4. **Aggregations** — any/all/count semantics, vacuous truth on empty collections, the iteration variable shadowing rule, with examples.
  5. **`in` operator** — semantics + at least one example.
  6. **Default rank** — the documented expression + plain-English summary of what it rewards/penalizes.
  7. **Examples** — verbatim from plan §Examples (six predicates + motivating workloads + the default rank).
  8. **Errors you might see** — the six error examples from plan §Error-message examples (so users recognize format).
  9. **Limits** — predicate text size cap (64 KiB), eval cost shape (O(ast_size * max_collection_size)), no I/O / no FS / no time / no recursion.
  10. **Not yet supported** — list deferred items (label["key"] indexing, etc.) per plan §Open questions.

Do NOT duplicate the §Reasoning / §Alternatives sections; the reference is for users, not designers. Link back to plan 03 once at the top: "For design rationale see plans/03-placement-predicates.md."

## Test plan

- `cargo doc -p classic-place` continues to build (AC-9): doc-comments on public items can link to dsl-reference.md but should not require it for builds.
- A grep CI check (or manual review) that every example predicate in dsl-reference.md is also covered by a test in tests/eval.rs (so the reference doesn't drift past what the implementation handles).
- Verify the markdown renders cleanly (no broken links to plan 03).

## Acceptance criteria

- AC-D1: `plans/dsl-reference.md` exists and is committed.
- AC-D2: Every grammar production from plan §Design "Grammar" is documented in dsl-reference.md (no missing operators or constructs).
- AC-D3: All NodeAd / GpuAd / PciAd field paths from plan §Data shapes are listed.
- AC-D4: All six example predicates from plan §Examples appear verbatim in the reference.
- AC-D5: Default rank source text appears verbatim and matches default_rank() output.
- AC-D6: Reference notes the deferred features (label indexing, etc.) per plan §Open questions.
- AC-D7: The reference does NOT duplicate §Reasoning / §Alternatives content from plan 03; it links instead.

## Intra-epic deps

- Depends on: nothing within this epic (can be written from the plan alone), but will benefit from being last so the wording settles after impl tasks finalize edge-case behaviors. Wire it as parent-child to the epic only; no prereq tasks.
- Blocks: epic close (this is a doc deliverable AC; not a code AC, but is the user-visible artifact).
acceptance_criteria:
AC-D1: plans/dsl-reference.md exists and is committed.
AC-D2: Every grammar production from plan §Design "Grammar" is documented.
AC-D3: All NodeAd / GpuAd / PciAd field paths from plan §Data shapes are listed.
AC-D4: All six example predicates from plan §Examples appear verbatim.
AC-D5: Default rank source text appears verbatim and matches default_rank() output.
AC-D6: Reference notes deferred features (label indexing, etc.) per plan §Open questions.
AC-D7: Reference does NOT duplicate §Reasoning / §Alternatives content; links to plan 03 instead.

title: classic-node: PeerMesh supervisor (dial+listen+reconnect) + classicd binary + shutdown + integration tests
description:
Part of epic classic-ivc. Plan: plans/01-skeleton-transport.md @ a7b6585.

## Reading first
- git show a7b6585:plans/01-skeleton-transport.md
- git show a7b6585:plans/ARCHITECTURE.md

## Excerpted design slice

### From plan § Design / Architecture
> classicd is one process per Linux host. It hosts:
> - A listener on node.listen_addr accepting inbound TCP.
> - A dialer task per configured peer, driving the reconnect loop.
> - One PeerLink actor per established connection ...
> - A frame multiplexer that holds a map from frame-range to handler. Plan 01 registers only the proto-range handler; later plans register their own ranges at startup.

### From plan § Design / Connection lifecycle (closed -> reconnect)
>    [Closed]
>       |  PeerLink task exits; PeerMesh's reconnect loop (if outbound) re-arms
>       v  with exponential backoff 250 ms -> 30 s, capped, jitter ±20%

### From plan § Design / Public APIs (classic-node)
> ```rust
> pub fn run(cfg_path: Option<PathBuf>) -> Result<(), NodeError>;
>
> pub struct PeerMesh {
>     self_id: NodeId,
>     links: DashMap<NodeId, Arc<PeerLink>>,
>     mux: Arc<FrameMux>,
> }
>
> impl PeerMesh {
>     pub fn new(self_id: NodeId, mux: Arc<FrameMux>) -> Self;
>     pub fn spawn_dialer(&self, peer_addr: String);
>     pub fn spawn_listener(&self, listen_addr: String);
>     pub fn snapshot(&self) -> Vec<PeerStatus>;
> }
>
> pub struct PeerStatus {
>     pub node_id: NodeId,
>     pub addr: String,
>     pub state: PeerState,           // Healthy | Unhealthy | Connecting | Closed
>     pub last_rx: Option<Instant>,
>     pub last_tx: Option<Instant>,
>     pub missed_heartbeats: u8,
> }
> ```

### From plan § Design / CLI surface (classicd)
> classicd [--config PATH]
> Environment: RUST_LOG (defaults to "info")
> Exit codes: 0 clean shutdown; 1 config error; 2 state-dir error; 3 bind error.
>
> A single SIGTERM/SIGINT handler:
> 1. Stops the listener and dialer-spawn loops.
> 2. Sends Bye on every healthy PeerLink, with a 1 s grace period.
> 3. Drops connections, exits 0.

### From plan § Requirements
> FR-4: classicd listens on node.listen_addr and accepts inbound TCP.
> FR-5: classicd dials every entry in node.peers with exponential backoff (250 ms → 30 s, jitter ±20%).
> FR-11: SIGTERM / SIGINT triggers a clean shutdown that sends Bye to every healthy peer.
> FR-12: tracing events at INFO level cover: peer connect, peer disconnect, handshake reject, peer unhealthy, peer recovered.
> FR-14: Frames larger than 16 MiB are rejected on encode and on decode.

### From plan § Testing plan / Integration
> tests/two_node.rs — spin up two classicd library instances on 127.0.0.1:0 (ephemeral ports) inside one tokio runtime, wired to dial each other. Assert both reach Healthy within 1 s and exchange ≥ 2 heartbeats.
> tests/reconnect.rs — kill one of the two peers, observe the survivor's state moves to Closed and its dialer reconnects within 5 s once the peer restarts.
> tests/three_node.rs — three peers form a triangle; mesh.snapshot() reports 2 healthy peers per node.

## Inputs
- classic-proto: FrameMux, NodeId (sibling tasks classic-53m, classic-fb8)
- classic-node::config (sibling task classic-5y9)
- classic-node::node_id (sibling task classic-5y9)
- classic-node::link::PeerLink (sibling tasks classic-zqk + classic-62g)
- classic-node::proto_handler::ProtoHandler (sibling task classic-62g)
- tokio (TcpListener, TcpStream, signal::unix), dashmap, tracing, tracing-subscriber, rand (jitter), clap (CLI)

## Outputs
Files under /home/horde/src/classic/crates/classic-node/:
- src/mesh.rs — PeerMesh struct, links: DashMap<NodeId, Arc<PeerLinkHandle>>; spawn_dialer + spawn_listener + snapshot. Dialer applies exponential backoff with jitter (250 ms .. 30 s, ±20%) on each failed attempt; reset on a successful Healthy. Each accepted connection runs the Hello handshake (sibling task classic-zqk) and on success registers the PeerLink in the links map (also satisfies the ExistingPeerLookup trait used for tiebreak).
- src/shutdown.rs — install SIGTERM/SIGINT handler; on signal: stop the listener accept loop, halt dialer respawns, send Bye on every Healthy link with 1 s grace, return.
- src/lib.rs — pub mod mesh; pub mod shutdown; pub fn run(cfg_path: Option<PathBuf>) -> Result<(), NodeError>; NodeError enum mapping to exit codes 1/2/3.
- src/main.rs — classicd binary: clap parses --config, sets up tracing-subscriber from RUST_LOG (or [log].level), calls classic_node::run(...), maps NodeError to exit code.
- crates/classic-node/Cargo.toml — add tokio (full), tracing, tracing-subscriber, dashmap, rand, clap, anyhow/thiserror

Tests under crates/classic-node/tests/:
- tests/two_node.rs — two classic_node::run instances on 127.0.0.1:0 dialing each other; both reach Healthy < 1 s; ≥ 2 heartbeats exchanged.
- tests/reconnect.rs — two-node mesh; drop one node; survivor's PeerMesh::snapshot reports the peer Closed; restart node; survivor reconnects within 5 s.
- tests/three_node.rs — triangle mesh; snapshot() reports 2 Healthy peers per node.
- tests/large_frame.rs (optional but recommended) — encode 17 MiB frame returns FrameTooLarge; inject a length prefix > 16 MiB on decode and assert FrameTooLarge.

## Public API requirements
- pub fn run(cfg_path: Option<PathBuf>) -> Result<(), NodeError>; — for the binary
- For tests, expose a library entry point that returns a handle holding the PeerMesh so integration tests can call snapshot() while the node is running. Suggested:
```rust
pub struct NodeHandle {
    pub mesh: Arc<PeerMesh>,
    shutdown: tokio::sync::oneshot::Sender<()>,
}
impl NodeHandle { pub async fn shutdown(self); }
pub async fn spawn_node_for_test(cfg: Config) -> Result<NodeHandle, NodeError>;
```

## Test plan
- cargo test -p classic-node --test two_node
- cargo test -p classic-node --test reconnect
- cargo test -p classic-node --test three_node
- cargo test -p classic-node --test large_frame (if added)
- cargo test --workspace — green on Linux x86_64

Manual smoke test (from plan § Testing / End-to-end): spin up two classicd processes via cargo run, observe "peer connected" / "handshake ok" / "peer healthy" log lines.

## Task-specific Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] PeerMesh::new, spawn_dialer, spawn_listener, snapshot compile per plan signatures.
- [ ] Dialer applies exponential backoff 250 ms -> 30 s with ±20% jitter; backoff resets on a successful Healthy.
- [ ] Listener accepts inbound TCP and runs Hello handshake; surviving links registered in DashMap keyed by NodeId.
- [ ] Tiebreak hook: when an inbound or outbound handshake completes for a NodeId already present, the higher-NodeId-initiated connection loses (provided by sibling task classic-zqk through ExistingPeerLookup; this task wires PeerMesh as the lookup).
- [ ] tests/two_node green: both peers Healthy within 1 s; ≥ 2 heartbeats observed.
- [ ] tests/reconnect green: dropped peer detected (Closed) and reconnected within 5 s.
- [ ] tests/three_node green: snapshot reports 2 Healthy peers per node.
- [ ] classicd binary: --config parsed; RUST_LOG subscriber installed; SIGTERM/SIGINT triggers Bye broadcast (1 s grace) and exits 0.
