title: classic-spawn: exec+monitor process under cgroup with caps (stdio pipes + exit reporting)
description:
# Task 4 — `classic-spawn` core: exec+monitor a process under cgroup with caps; capture stdio; report exit

Parent epic: classic-1j3 (Feature: Spawn pipeline). Plan: plans/04-spawn-pipeline.md @ commit a7b6585. Read with: git show a7b6585:plans/04-spawn-pipeline.md.

## Reading first

Read plans/04-spawn-pipeline.md sections "Architecture", "Cgroup setup", "Crate layout", and "Failure handling" in full. Skim plans/ARCHITECTURE.md.

## Scope

Stand up `crates/classic-spawn` with the executor-side primitives: create the per-task scope, acquire caps, fork+exec the user binary into the scope, capture stdio via pipes, wait for exit. Frame plumbing and originator state machine are separate tasks (5 and 6). This task is "executor-side, locally only" — no peer transport here.

In scope:
- `crates/classic-spawn/` Cargo.toml + src/lib.rs.
- `src/cgroup_scope.rs` — wraps Task 2's `cgroup` helpers + Task 3's `BpfLoader::attach` into one ScopeGuard with RAII teardown.
- `src/exec.rs` — `pub async fn exec_in_scope(scope: &ScopeGuard, argv: &[String], env: &[String], stdin_kind: StdinKind, caps: Vec<DeviceCap>) -> ChildHandle` — forks helper, writes helper PID to `cgroup.procs`, exec()s user binary, returns handle with stdout/stderr/stdin pipe FDs and a future that resolves to exit status.
- `src/stdio.rs` — pipe<->channel adapter: pump stdout/stderr bytes into mpsc senders (caller frames them in Task 5); pipe writes from a receiver to the child's stdin.
- `src/lib.rs` — public API stubs for `originate()`/`execute()` (full impl in Task 6); plus this task's exposed primitives (`exec_in_scope`, `ChildHandle`, `ScopeGuard`, `StdinKind`).
- `MboxAllocator` (per-daemon atomic u64 starting at 1; mbox 0 reserved per ARCHITECTURE.md) lives here as a small utility used by `ExecutorCtx`.

Out of scope:
- Spawn frames (Task 5).
- Originator/executor state machines that drive frames (Task 6).
- CLI surface (Task 7).

## Design slice

Per plan: per-task scope path is `/sys/fs/cgroup/classicd.slice/task-<MboxId>.scope/`. Before fork: create scope, write helper PID to `cgroup.procs`, attach BPF program with current cap snapshot, then exec user binary. RAII: ScopeGuard Drop tears down the scope dir AND releases caps.

Helper-PID pattern: parent forks a helper child, helper writes its own PID into the new scope's `cgroup.procs` (so the helper is in the scope), helper then `execve()`s the user argv. Result: user binary runs in the scope.

Exit reporting: `ChildHandle::wait().await -> ChildExitInfo { code: Option<i32>, signal: Option<i32> }`. Signal death yields `signal: Some(n)`, code: None.

```rust
pub struct ChildHandle {
    pub req_id: u64,
    pub stdout: tokio::sync::mpsc::Receiver<Vec<u8>>, // EOF on close
    pub stderr: tokio::sync::mpsc::Receiver<Vec<u8>>,
    pub stdin:  tokio::sync::mpsc::Sender<Vec<u8>>,   // empty == close stdin
    // .wait() consumes self
}
pub enum StdinKind { Inherit, File }
```

Failure mapping (caller in Task 6 will translate to DenyReason):
- mkdir EPERM/ENOSPC -> `ScopeError::CgroupSetup(io::Error)`.
- BPF attach EINVAL -> `ScopeError::BpfAttach(io::Error)`.
- ENOEXEC/ENOENT on exec -> `ExecError::Exec(io::Error)`.

## Inputs

- Task 1 (classic-vmr): `CapBroker`, `DeviceCap` — held alongside the scope; passed in to `exec_in_scope` so Drop order is: child exits -> caps drop -> ScopeGuard drops -> teardown.
- Task 2 (classic-c22): `cgroup::create_scope`, `ScopeHandle`, `open_scope_fd`.
- Task 3 (classic-3xx): `BpfLoader::attach`, `ProgHandle`, `sync`.
- `classic-proto::MboxId`.

## Outputs

Files to create:
- `crates/classic-spawn/Cargo.toml` (deps: classic-proto, classic-cap, tokio with rt+process+sync, libc/nix for fork/exec).
- `crates/classic-spawn/src/lib.rs` — module decls + public re-exports + `MboxAllocator`.
- `crates/classic-spawn/src/cgroup_scope.rs` — `ScopeGuard { scope: ScopeHandle, prog: ProgHandle, caps: Vec<DeviceCap> }` with `new(broker, mbox, kinds, exclusive) -> Result<Self>` and `Drop` ordering: BPF detach -> scope rmdir -> caps drop.
- `crates/classic-spawn/src/exec.rs` — `pub async fn exec_in_scope(...)`. Internally uses `nix::unistd::fork` or `tokio::process::Command` with a `pre_exec` that writes pid to cgroup.procs; latter is preferred since tokio handles awaiting child status.
- `crates/classic-spawn/src/stdio.rs` — pipe pumps using `tokio::io::copy` and bounded mpsc (1 MiB cap per stream; drop-and-close on overflow per plan).

Update root `Cargo.toml` workspace member list.

## Test plan

`cargo test -p classic-spawn` (Linux x86_64; cgroup tests via tempdir Sysroot from Task 2; BPF tests gated on `hw-bpf`):
- `exec_in_scope("/bin/echo hi")` -> exit code 0; stdout receiver yields `b"hi\n"` then EOF.
- `exec_in_scope("/bin/false")` -> exit code 1.
- `exec_in_scope` of a missing binary -> `ExecError::Exec` with errno=ENOENT.
- `ScopeGuard` Drop removes the scope dir and releases caps even when the child is killed mid-run.
- StdinKind::File piped through `/bin/cat` round-trips a small payload byte-for-byte (validates pipe pump).
- Signal death: spawn `sleep 30`, send SIGTERM, observe `ChildExitInfo { signal: Some(15), code: None }`.
- (`hw-bpf`) ScopeGuard with `kinds=[GpuMinor(0)]` allows opening `/dev/nvidia0` and denies `/dev/nvidia1` from inside the child.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] AC-T4.1: `cargo build -p classic-spawn` and `cargo test -p classic-spawn` succeed on Linux x86_64 without root for the non-hw tests (uses tempdir-rooted Sysroot + stub BPF loader trait).
- [ ] AC-T4.2: `exec_in_scope` correctly creates the scope, writes helper PID to `cgroup.procs`, exec()s the user binary, and returns a ChildHandle with working stdio channels.
- [ ] AC-T4.3: `ChildHandle::wait()` returns `code: Some(n)` for normal exit and `signal: Some(n), code: None` for signal death.
- [ ] AC-T4.4: `ScopeGuard` Drop ordering is BPF-detach -> rmdir -> caps-drop; verified by a fault-injection test that asserts cleanup runs in order even when one step errors.
- [ ] AC-T4.5: `MboxAllocator` is monotonic u64 starting at 1; never returns 0 (matches ARCHITECTURE.md).
- [ ] AC-T4.6: ENOENT/ENOEXEC on exec produces `ExecError::Exec` carrying the underlying `io::Error` (so callers in Task 6 can map to `DenyReason::ExecFailed`); caps and scope are torn down before returning.
- [ ] AC-T4.7: stdio pipe pumps respect a 1 MiB bounded buffer per stream (per plan non-functional req).

## Intra-epic dependencies

- Parent: classic-1j3.
- Prereqs (within this epic): Task 1 (classic-vmr), Task 2 (classic-c22), Task 3 (BPF loader).
- Blocks: Task 5 (frames — needs StdinKind), Task 6 (originator/executor state machines wrap this).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] AC-T4.1: `cargo build -p classic-spawn` and `cargo test -p classic-spawn` succeed on Linux x86_64 without root for non-hw tests (tempdir-rooted Sysroot + stub BPF loader trait).
- [ ] AC-T4.2: `exec_in_scope` creates the scope, writes helper PID to `cgroup.procs`, exec()s the user binary, returns a ChildHandle with working stdio channels.
- [ ] AC-T4.3: `ChildHandle::wait()` returns `code: Some(n)` for normal exit and `signal: Some(n), code: None` for signal death.
- [ ] AC-T4.4: `ScopeGuard` Drop ordering is BPF-detach -> rmdir -> caps-drop; verified by a fault-injection test asserting cleanup runs in order even when a step errors.
- [ ] AC-T4.5: `MboxAllocator` is monotonic u64 starting at 1; never returns 0 (matches ARCHITECTURE.md).
- [ ] AC-T4.6: ENOENT/ENOEXEC on exec produces `ExecError::Exec` carrying `io::Error`; caps and scope are torn down before returning.
- [ ] AC-T4.7: stdio pipe pumps respect a 1 MiB bounded buffer per stream (plan non-functional req).

title: classic-place: test fixtures + table-driven test suite + golden errors
description:
Epic: classic-i0n (Feature: Placement predicates)
Plan: plans/03-placement-predicates.md @ commit a7b6585. Read with: git show a7b6585:plans/03-placement-predicates.md

## Reading first

1. Plan §Testing plan in full.
2. Plan §Examples (six concrete predicates).
3. Plan §Error-message examples (golden snapshots for AC-4).
4. Plan §Test fixtures (the six fixture builders).

## Design slice (excerpted from plan §Testing plan)

Test fixtures (verbatim from plan §Test fixtures):
```rust
pub fn ad_cpu_only(node: u8) -> NodeAd;          // 16 cores, 64 GB RAM, no GPU
pub fn ad_a100x1(node: u8, busy: bool) -> NodeAd;  // 1x A100 40GB
pub fn ad_h100x2(node: u8, busy_count: u32) -> NodeAd; // 2x H100 80GB
pub fn ad_h100x8(node: u8, busy_count: u32, cpu_pct: f64) -> NodeAd;
pub fn ad_amd_mi300(node: u8) -> NodeAd;         // vendor 0x1002
pub fn ad_arm_cpu_only(node: u8) -> NodeAd;      // arch=aarch64
```

`node: u8` becomes the first byte of the NodeId so test failures point to a recognizable node.

Six example predicates and the default rank are given verbatim in plan §Examples.

Integration tests required (plan §Testing plan / Integration):
- End-to-end place_str with a 5-fixture cluster (no-GPU node, 1xA100, 2xH100, 8xH100 idle, CPU-loaded 8xH100): assert which node the default rank picks for each of the six example predicates.
- Frame round-trip for PlacementRequest/Response/Error (delegated to the frame task — the test files cross-reference).
- Determinism: same inputs, same outputs across 1000 runs (catch iteration-order bugs in BTreeMap/HashMap should they sneak in).

Golden error tests (plan §Error-message examples + AC-4):
- Unterminated string
- Chained comparison
- Type mismatch
- Top-level non-bool requirement
- Unknown field
- Reserved keyword as field
Each error renders byte-identically to the pinned snapshot text.

Performance smoke (AC-8):
- `place(...)` under 5 ms for 100 ads x 8 GPUs x 30-AST-node predicate, gated under --release with #[test] perf_smoke().

## Inputs

- All previously-shipped tasks: lexer, parser, type-check, evaluator, place(), frames.
- NodeAd / GpuAd / PciAd struct shapes from plan §Data shapes.

## Outputs

- New file `crates/classic-place/src/tests/fixtures.rs` (or `crates/classic-place/tests/fixtures.rs` if the integration tests need it; plan suggests src/tests/fixtures.rs callable from any test):
  - The six `ad_*` builder functions.
  - Helper to make NodeId from a u8 by setting first byte and zeroing the rest.
- New file `crates/classic-place/tests/grammar.rs` (NEW) — table-driven parser tests using all six example predicates + each grammar production (expand on what shipped in parse.rs in-module tests).
- New file `crates/classic-place/tests/eval.rs` (NEW) — table-driven evaluator tests; the (predicate, fixture) -> expected matches/score table for AC-2; the 5-fixture cluster end-to-end place_str tests for AC-3.
- New file `crates/classic-place/tests/golden_errors.rs` (NEW) — snapshot tests for the six error examples in plan §Error-message examples. Use straight assert_eq! against the pinned strings (no external snapshot library needed — keeps AC-6's external-deps clean).
- Performance smoke: a single `#[test]` in tests/eval.rs gated under `#[cfg(not(debug_assertions))]` (or behind a `perf-smoke` cargo feature) that builds 100 ads x 8 GPUs and runs place() under a 5-ms budget. Document how to run: `cargo test -p classic-place --release perf_smoke`.

## Test plan

This task is the test plan. Self-test via `cargo test -p classic-place` and `cargo test -p classic-place --release perf_smoke`.

The (predicate, fixture) eval table for AC-2 must include rows for each of the six example predicates evaluated against each of the six fixtures (36 rows minimum).

The 5-fixture cluster for AC-3 (per plan §Testing plan / Integration): {ad_cpu_only(1), ad_a100x1(2, busy=false), ad_h100x2(3, busy=0), ad_h100x8(4, busy=0, cpu_pct=10.0), ad_h100x8(5, busy=0, cpu_pct=80.0)}. Document the expected best-pick under default_rank for each example predicate.

Determinism: a test that runs place() 1000 times on the 5-fixture cluster with example predicate 1, asserts identical Vec<(NodeId, f64)> output every time.

Golden error tests must include byte-identical assertions for the six error examples in plan §Error-message examples. If wording drift is needed, the snapshot is updated *intentionally* with a commit explaining why (per plan §Testing plan).

## Acceptance criteria

- AC-X1: All six fixture builders compile and produce valid NodeAd values matching their documented shape.
- AC-X2: tests/grammar.rs covers each grammar production at least once and each of the six example predicates parses + type-checks.
- AC-X3: tests/eval.rs has a (predicate, fixture) eval table covering AC-2 (>=36 rows) and a 5-fixture cluster covering AC-3 (one assertion per example predicate of the documented best-pick under default_rank).
- AC-X4: tests/golden_errors.rs has byte-identical snapshot assertions for all six error-message examples in plan §Error-message examples (AC-4).
- AC-X5: Determinism test runs place() 1000 times on the same inputs and asserts identical output.
- AC-X6: perf_smoke test (release mode) shows place() under 5 ms for 100 ads x 8 GPUs x 30-AST-node predicate (AC-8).
- AC-X7: `cargo test -p classic-place` is green on Linux x86_64 (AC-7).
- AC-X8: No unsafe; no new external test deps.

## Intra-epic deps

- Depends on: place() task (classic-kd1) and frames task (classic-rfv).
- Indirect: lexer, parser, type-check, evaluator (transitively via place()).
- Blocks: epic close (this task is the gate for AC-2/3/4/7/8).
acceptance_criteria:
AC-X1: All six fixture builders compile and produce valid NodeAds.
AC-X2: tests/grammar.rs covers every grammar production and all six example predicates parse + type-check.
AC-X3: tests/eval.rs (predicate, fixture) table (>=36 rows) covers AC-2; 5-fixture cluster covers AC-3 best-pick assertions.
AC-X4: tests/golden_errors.rs byte-identical snapshots for all six error examples (AC-4).
AC-X5: Determinism test (1000 runs, identical output).
AC-X6: perf_smoke under 5 ms for 100 ads x 8 GPUs x 30-AST-node predicate in release (AC-8).
AC-X7: cargo test -p classic-place green on Linux x86_64.
AC-X8: No unsafe; no new external test deps.

title: Scenario 3 script: Service registration + cross-node mailbox lookup (coord on B, worker on C)
description:
Part of epic classic-o4c (Multi-node demo + integration tests).

Plan: plans/08-multinode-demo.md @ commit a7b6585. Read with:
  git show a7b6585:plans/08-multinode-demo.md

## Reading first
- git show a7b6585:plans/08-multinode-demo.md §Testing plan / Scenario 3 — Service registration + cross-node lookup
- git show a7b6585:plans/05-mailbox-service-directory.md (mailbox runtime, service directory gossip, CLASSIC_PEER_NETID env var, `classic mail --service`)
- git show a7b6585:plans/04-spawn-pipeline.md (`classic spawn --service`, `classic spawn --node`, `classic kill`)

## Excerpted design slice

### From plan §Testing plan / Scenario 3
Prerequisites: all three daemons up; service name `coord` unused.

Commands: terminal 1 (foreground) on node-a:
  classic spawn --service coord --node node-b -- coord-server

where coord-server is a generated bash one-liner installed by `make setup`:
  while read line; do echo "coord: got $line from $CLASSIC_PEER_NETID"; done

Terminal 2 on node-a:
  classic spawn --node node-c -- \
    sh -c 'classic mail --service coord -- "hello"'

Expected stdout (terminal 1): matches `^coord: got hello from [0-9a-f]+:[0-9]+$`.
Expected exit code: terminal 2 exits 0; terminal 1 is torn down by the scenario script with `classic kill`.
Success means: plan 05 service-directory gossip propagated `coord`; node-c's worker resolved it; mail delivered through plan 05's mailbox.

### From plan §Open questions
Scenario 3's coord-server helper. Decided no for now — lives inline in `make setup` as a generated script. Revisit if a third scenario needs the same shape.

## Inputs
- examples/multinode/lib/assert.sh (from classic-lqx)
- examples/multinode/Makefile (from classic-d2p) — extend with scenario-3 target; `make setup` generates the coord-server helper script
- classic CLI: `classic spawn --service`, `classic spawn --node`, `classic mail --service`, `classic kill`, `classic ps`
- classicd peers up on node-a, node-b, node-c
- CLASSIC_PEER env var

## Outputs
Files to create / modify:
- examples/multinode/scenarios/03-service-lookup.sh — shebang, executable, sources lib/assert.sh; orchestrates two concurrent classic spawn commands as terminal 1 (background, output captured) and terminal 2 (synchronous); waits for terminal 1 stdout to contain a line matching `^coord: got hello from [0-9a-f]+:[0-9]+$`; tears down terminal 1 with `classic kill <task-id>`; asserts terminal 2 exit 0; asserts terminal 1 captured stdout regex matches; prints OK. Use a timeout (e.g. 20 s) on the wait so the script never hangs.
- examples/multinode/Makefile — add scenario-3 target; `make setup` (extending classic-d2p's setup) generates the coord-server one-liner at a known path (e.g. examples/multinode/build/coord-server) with executable bit and the inline body above.

## Test plan
- bash -n examples/multinode/scenarios/03-service-lookup.sh — syntax check.
- shellcheck examples/multinode/scenarios/03-service-lookup.sh — clean.
- Real test (manual; requires real cluster up): `CLASSIC_PEER=10.42.0.10:7421 make -C examples/multinode scenario-3` — prints OK; terminal 1 captured output exactly one line matching the regex; terminal 1 cleaned up after the script exits (verify with `classic ps --all`).
- Idempotency: run the script twice in a row — second invocation should also pass (service name `coord` re-registers cleanly after the previous teardown).

## Task-specific Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] examples/multinode/scenarios/03-service-lookup.sh exists, is executable, shellcheck-clean.
- [ ] Script orchestrates two concurrent classic spawn invocations: coord-server bound to node-b under service name `coord`; worker on node-c that issues `classic mail --service coord -- "hello"`.
- [ ] Script asserts terminal 1 stdout matches `^coord: got hello from [0-9a-f]+:[0-9]+$` (NetId regex).
- [ ] Script tears down terminal 1 with `classic kill` after the assertion succeeds.
- [ ] Script asserts terminal 2 exit code is 0.
- [ ] Script has an upper bound timeout so it cannot hang.
- [ ] examples/multinode/Makefile `make setup` generates the coord-server bash one-liner at examples/multinode/build/coord-server (or equivalent documented path) with executable bit.
- [ ] Makefile `scenario-3` target invokes the script with CLASSIC_PEER propagated.
- [ ] Script prints OK on success.

## Non-obvious context
- The NetId in the regex (`[0-9a-f]+:[0-9]+`) is the wire-format string from CLASSIC_PEER_NETID — node:mbox per ARCHITECTURE.md identity types. Plan 05 must export CLASSIC_PEER_NETID as the spawn-side env var that the coord-server reads.
- coord-server is intentionally an inline bash one-liner generated at `make setup` time per plan §Open questions — no shipped binary, no helper crate.
- The `--node` flag on `classic spawn` is open-question per plan §Open questions; if plan 04 ships a different spelling, this scenario updates in the same PR. If `--node` is missing entirely, file a follow-up bead and pin this scenario as xfail (do NOT retrofit the feature here).
- Concurrency control: terminal 1 must be backgrounded with stdout redirected to a temp file; the script then polls that file for the expected line with a timeout. Use a deterministic pattern (`tail -f | grep -m1 ...` with a kill on parent timeout) to avoid races.

## Intra-epic deps
- Depends on classic-lqx (assert.sh + directory layout)
- Depends on classic-d2p (Makefile skeleton + setup target — this task extends both)
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] examples/multinode/scenarios/03-service-lookup.sh exists, is executable, shellcheck-clean.
- [ ] Script orchestrates coord-server on node-b under service `coord`, worker on node-c sending mail "hello".
- [ ] Script asserts terminal 1 stdout matches `^coord: got hello from [0-9a-f]+:[0-9]+$`.
- [ ] Script tears down terminal 1 with `classic kill`; asserts terminal 2 exit 0; has timeout.
- [ ] Makefile setup generates the coord-server bash one-liner; scenario-3 target wired with CLASSIC_PEER.
- [ ] Script prints OK on success.

title: classic-node: Heartbeat scheduler + Unhealthy detection + ProtoHandler
description:
Part of epic classic-ivc. Plan: plans/01-skeleton-transport.md @ a7b6585.

## Reading first
- git show a7b6585:plans/01-skeleton-transport.md
- git show a7b6585:plans/ARCHITECTURE.md

## Excerpted design slice

### From plan § Design / Connection lifecycle state machine (Healthy/Unhealthy slices)
>    [Healthy]
>       |  every 5s: send Heartbeat
>       |  on rx Heartbeat: reset miss counter
>       |  on rx any frame: forward to FrameMux
>       |  on rx Bye: send Bye, [Closed: PeerBye]
>       |  on rx Error(fatal=true): [Closed: PeerError]
>       |  on 3 missed heartbeats (15s): [Unhealthy]
>       |  on TCP rx EOF or err: [Closed: TransportLost]
>       v
>    [Unhealthy]
>       |  same as Healthy but logged + reflected in PeerMesh status snapshot
>       |  recovery: any inbound frame transitions back to [Healthy]

### From plan § Design / Heartbeat
> - A tokio::time::interval of 5 s ticks per PeerLink. On each tick, send Frame { kind: Heartbeat, payload: bincode(HeartbeatPayload) }.
> - A counter missed: u8 increments on every tick if no inbound frame of any kind arrived since the previous tick. (Any inbound frame, not just Heartbeat, resets the counter — application traffic from plan 02+ implies liveness.)
> - missed >= 3 (so 15 s of silence) flips state to Unhealthy and logs a warn event. The connection stays open; we do not close on unhealthy. Closure is reserved for transport errors and explicit Bye.

### From plan § Design / Frame multiplexer
> A frame whose high byte has no registered handler triggers a single warn-level log and is dropped. The connection stays open.

### From plan § Requirements
> FR-8: Healthy peers exchange Heartbeat every 5 s; a peer is marked Unhealthy after 3 missed heartbeats (15 s).
> FR-9: An inbound frame whose kind has no registered handler is logged and dropped without closing the connection.
> FR-10: A Bye frame triggers a clean close on both sides.
> FR-12: tracing events at INFO level cover: peer connect, peer disconnect, handshake reject, peer unhealthy, peer recovered.

### From plan § Design / Architecture
> Plan 01 registers only the proto-range handler (Hello / Heartbeat / Bye / Error); later plans register their own ranges at startup.

## Inputs
- classic-proto: Frame, FrameKind, encode_frame/decode_frame, HelloPayload, HeartbeatPayload, ErrorPayload, ErrorCode, FrameHandler, FrameMux (from sibling tasks classic-53m, classic-4ef, classic-fb8)
- classic-node::link::PeerLink scaffold from sibling task classic-zqk
- tokio::time::interval, tokio::select!

## Outputs
Files under /home/horde/src/classic/crates/classic-node/:
- src/link.rs — extend with the Healthy-loop: send Heartbeat every 5 s, decrement-or-reset miss counter, flip Healthy <-> Unhealthy on missed thresholds, recovery on any inbound frame.
- src/proto_handler.rs — implement FrameHandler for the proto range (high byte 0x00). Logs Heartbeat receipt; consumes Bye -> initiates clean close (notify the link via a oneshot/notify); maps Error(fatal) to a close.
- src/lib.rs — pub mod proto_handler;

Behavior contracts:
- A monotonic per-link Heartbeat seq: HeartbeatPayload { seq: counter, send_time_ns: monotonic_now }.
- On every 5 s tick: if zero inbound frames since last tick, missed += 1; else missed = 0.
- missed == 3: state -> Unhealthy + warn log (peer_unhealthy).
- Recovery: any inbound frame in Unhealthy returns state to Healthy + info log (peer_recovered).
- ProtoHandler.on_frame:
  - Heartbeat (0x0002): no-op (the link's inbound-frame counter handles liveness reset).
  - Bye (0x0003): notify link to send a Bye reply and transition to Closed(PeerBye).
  - Error (0x0004): decode ErrorPayload; if code is fatal (ProtoVersionMismatch / ProtocolViolation / Internal), close as PeerError.

Note: ProtoHandler must communicate close decisions back to the per-peer link without blocking. Use a per-link channel/notify (not a global mutex).

## Test plan
Use tokio::time::pause + tokio::time::advance to drive heartbeats deterministically.
- cargo test -p classic-node link::tests::heartbeat_unhealthy — with paused clock, advance 16 s without inbound frames; assert state == Unhealthy and a warn-level event was emitted.
- cargo test -p classic-node link::tests::heartbeat_recovers — after entering Unhealthy, deliver any inbound frame; assert state returns to Healthy and an info event was emitted.
- cargo test -p classic-node link::tests::bye_clean_close — peer sends Bye; assert local sends Bye and closes with PeerBye.
- cargo test -p classic-node link::tests::error_fatal_close — peer sends Error{ProtoVersionMismatch}; assert local closes with PeerError.
- cargo test -p classic-node proto_handler::tests::heartbeat_seq_monotonic — consecutive Heartbeats sent by a link have strictly increasing seq.
- cargo test -p classic-node proto_handler::tests::unknown_kind_dropped — frame with kind 0x0723 (no handler registered) is logged and dropped; the link stays Healthy. (This exercises FrameMux.dispatch via the link.)

## Task-specific Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Heartbeat tick interval is 5 s (configurable via test override is fine).
- [ ] missed >= 3 flips Healthy -> Unhealthy with a warn-level tracing event.
- [ ] Any inbound frame returns Unhealthy -> Healthy with an info-level event.
- [ ] HeartbeatPayload.seq is monotonically increasing per link.
- [ ] Bye triggers clean close on both ends; close reason PeerBye.
- [ ] Error frames with ProtoVersionMismatch / ProtocolViolation / Internal close with PeerError.
- [ ] Unknown frame kind (no FrameMux handler) is logged + dropped; link stays open.
- [ ] All listed tests in the test plan green.

## Intra-epic deps
- Blocks-on: classic-zqk (PeerLink scaffold/FSM), classic-fb8 (FrameMux + FrameHandler), classic-4ef (HeartbeatPayload, ErrorPayload, Connection)

title: classic-place: wire frames PlacementRequest/Response/Error (0x0500-0x05FF)
description:
Epic: classic-i0n (Feature: Placement predicates)
Plan: plans/03-placement-predicates.md @ commit a7b6585. Read with: git show a7b6585:plans/03-placement-predicates.md
Foundations: plans/ARCHITECTURE.md @ a7b6585 — frame-kind allocation table.

## Reading first

1. Plan §Design "Wire frames" — payload definitions + frame-kind allocations.
2. ARCHITECTURE.md §Frame-kind allocation — confirm 0x0500-0x05FF is owned by classic-place.
3. Plan §Requirements FR-11 (round-trip codec test).

## Design slice (excerpted from plan §Design)

Allocated kinds:
```rust
// classic-proto::FrameKind
Place_Request   = 0x0501,
Place_Response  = 0x0502,
Place_Error     = 0x0503,
// 0x0500 reserved (no zero-value frame inside a range, by convention)
// 0x0504-0x05FF reserved for future placement RPCs (v2 streaming, scope-
//   limited queries, capability checks, etc.)
```

Frame payloads (bincode v2, fixed-int LE):
```rust
pub struct PlacementRequest {
    pub req_id:  u64,
    pub req_src: String,
    pub rank_src: Option<String>,
    pub max_results: u16,
}

pub struct PlacementResponse {
    pub req_id: u64,
    pub candidates: Vec<PlacedCandidate>,
}
pub struct PlacedCandidate { pub node: NodeId, pub score: f64 }

pub struct PlacementError {
    pub req_id: u64,
    pub kind:   PlaceErrKind, // ParseError | TypeError | NoCandidates
    pub message: String,
    pub line: Option<u32>,
    pub col:  Option<u32>,
}
```

v1 wires these frames into the protocol but only consumes them for in-process plumbing (so we can drive the local matcher through the same path the v2 RPC will use, which makes future migration boring).

## Inputs

- `crates/classic-proto/src/frame.rs` — existing FrameKind enum (allocated ranges per ARCHITECTURE.md table).
- `classic-proto::NodeId` (identity types).

## Outputs

- New file `crates/classic-place/src/frame.rs`:
  - `pub struct PlacementRequest { ... }` with serde::{Serialize, Deserialize}.
  - `pub struct PlacementResponse { ... }`, `pub struct PlacedCandidate { ... }`.
  - `pub struct PlacementError { ... }`, `pub enum PlaceErrKind { ParseError, TypeError, NoCandidates }`.
  - Re-export from lib.rs.
- MODIFIED `crates/classic-proto/src/frame.rs`:
  - Add three FrameKind variants: `Place_Request = 0x0501`, `Place_Response = 0x0502`, `Place_Error = 0x0503`. (Use Rust-idiomatic naming if the project's enum convention differs — e.g. PascalCase `PlaceRequest` etc. — match the surrounding style; the *numeric values* are the contract.)

## Test plan

In-module `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` in frame.rs (table-driven):

- Codec round-trip:
  - PlacementRequest with non-trivial values (req_id=42, req_src="any(gpu, gpu.vram_mb >= 80000)", rank_src=Some("..."), max_results=10) -> bincode encode -> decode -> equality.
  - Same for rank_src=None.
  - PlacementResponse with 3 PlacedCandidates of varying scores (incl. f64 edge values: 0.0, -0.0, +inf, -inf).
  - NaN sort-last (open question 4): document behavior; either reject NaN at encode or accept and sort-last on decode side. Plan currently says "sort-last" — encode/decode passes NaN through; the round-trip test asserts the f64 bit pattern matches via to_bits comparison (since NaN != NaN under PartialEq).
  - PlacementError with all three PlaceErrKind variants, with and without line/col set.
- FrameKind enum:
  - Numeric values asserted: `Place_Request as u16 == 0x0501` etc.
  - Range owner check (ARCHITECTURE.md table): all three values are in [0x0500, 0x05FF].

Run: `cargo test -p classic-place frame` and `cargo test -p classic-proto` for the FrameKind enum tests.

## Acceptance criteria

- AC-F1: PlacementRequest, PlacementResponse, PlacedCandidate, PlacementError, PlaceErrKind defined in classic-place::frame with serde derives.
- AC-F2: classic-proto::FrameKind contains Place_Request=0x0501, Place_Response=0x0502, Place_Error=0x0503; numeric values asserted by test.
- AC-F3: Bincode v2 round-trip passes for each payload struct (incl. NaN bit-pattern preservation).
- AC-F4: All allocated FrameKind values fall inside the 0x0500-0x05FF range owned by classic-place per ARCHITECTURE.md.
- AC-F5: No unsafe; classic-place deps limited to serde + bincode (per AC-6 of the epic).

## Intra-epic deps

- Depends on: nothing within this epic for compilation; can run in parallel with lexer/parser/typeck.
  (NOTE: per task ordering, this is independent. Wire it under the epic only — no prereq tasks. The integration tests task pulls it in.)
- Blocks: integration tests + DSL grammar reference doc do not depend on this; only the epic AC-5 round-trip test depends on this.
acceptance_criteria:
AC-F1: PlacementRequest, PlacementResponse, PlacedCandidate, PlacementError, PlaceErrKind defined in classic-place::frame with serde derives.
AC-F2: classic-proto::FrameKind contains Place_Request=0x0501, Place_Response=0x0502, Place_Error=0x0503 with numeric value asserted by test.
AC-F3: Bincode v2 round-trip passes for each payload struct (incl. NaN bit-pattern preservation via to_bits).
AC-F4: All allocated FrameKind values fall inside 0x0500-0x05FF.
AC-F5: No unsafe; classic-place deps limited to serde + bincode.

title: classic-cap: cgroup-v2 device controller (BPF program + loader for /dev/nvidia* + PCI gating)
description:
# Task 3 — `classic-cap`: cgroup-v2 device controller (BPF program for `/dev/nvidia*` / PCI BDF gating)

Parent epic: classic-1j3 (Feature: Spawn pipeline). Plan: plans/04-spawn-pipeline.md @ commit a7b6585. Read with: git show a7b6585:plans/04-spawn-pipeline.md.

## Reading first

Read plans/04-spawn-pipeline.md "Cgroup setup" -> "Device controller" subsection in full. Skim Linux Documentation/bpf/prog_cgroup_device.rst. Read libbpf-cargo docs (https://github.com/libbpf/libbpf-rs).

## Scope

Implement the BPF cgroup_device program that gates `/dev/nvidia*` and PCI char nodes per scope, plus the loader that compiles the program at crate build time, attaches it to a scope cgroup fd, and keeps its allowlist map in sync with the `CapBroker`.

In scope:
- `crates/classic-cap/src/bpf/devices.bpf.c` — BPF_PROG_TYPE_CGROUP_DEVICE program. Default-deny. Allows the standard `(c,1,*)` set (`/dev/null|zero|random|urandom|tty`). Allows `(c,195,254)` and `(c,195,255)` iff any GpuMinor cap held. Allows `(c,195,N)` per `GpuMinor(N)`. Allows char-device `(major,minor)` per `PciSlot` (resolved separately from `/sys/bus/pci/devices/<bdf>/uevent`).
- `crates/classic-cap/build.rs` — invokes `libbpf-cargo` to compile `devices.bpf.c` into a skeleton-loadable object.
- `crates/classic-cap/src/bpf/loader.rs` — `BpfLoader::attach(scope_fd: RawFd) -> ProgHandle`, `ProgHandle::sync(snapshot: &[CapSnapshot])` updates the BPF map (`BPF_MAP_TYPE_HASH`) atomically. Detach on Drop.
- Hook the loader to `CapBroker` events: every `acquire`/`release` on a holder triggers a `sync()` for that holder's scope. Wire so callers (Task 4) can register a `ProgHandle` per scope.
- PCI BDF -> `(major,minor)` resolution in `nvidia.rs` or new `pci.rs` (parse `/sys/bus/pci/devices/<bdf>/uevent`).

Out of scope:
- Spawn-side wiring (Task 4 attaches the program when creating the scope).
- Multi-tenant policies.

## Design slice

> Device controller. Not a sysfs file in v2 — it's a `BPF_CGROUP_DEVICE` program attached to the scope's cgroup fd:
> 1. Compile `classic-cap/src/bpf/devices.bpf.c` at crate build time via `libbpf-cargo`. Body is a map-driven allowlist: default-deny; allow `(c, 1, *)` (the standard `/dev/null|zero|random|urandom|tty` set); allow `(c, 195, 254)` and `(c, 195, NVIDIA_CTL_MINOR=255)` iff any `GpuMinor` cap is held; allow `(c, 195, N)` per `GpuMinor(N)`; allow PCI device char nodes per `PciSlot` (resolved via `/sys/bus/pci/devices/<bdf>/uevent`).
> 2. Attach with `bpf(BPF_PROG_ATTACH, BPF_CGROUP_DEVICE)` to the scope fd.
> 3. Update its `BPF_MAP_TYPE_HASH` allowlist on every cap acquire/release.

> BPF is the only option (no `devices` controller in v2 unified — and ARCHITECTURE.md mandates v2). We do NOT borrow systemd/runc `device_filter` machinery: `classicd` should own the hierarchy outright.

Failure handling: BPF attach EINVAL or compile failure -> caller (Task 4) maps to `SpawnDeny{CgroupSetupFailed}`.

## Inputs

- Task 1 (classic-vmr): `CapBroker`, `CapSnapshot`. The loader uses `snapshot()` output as input to `sync()`.
- Task 2 (classic-c22): `cgroup::open_scope_fd(scope) -> RawFd`. The loader receives this fd.
- `nvidia::list_nvidia_minors()` from Task 1 — used in tests.
- crate dep: `libbpf-cargo` (build-dep), `libbpf-rs` (runtime).

## Outputs

Files to create/modify:
- `crates/classic-cap/build.rs` (NEW): runs `libbpf_cargo::SkeletonBuilder` against `src/bpf/devices.bpf.c`; emit skeleton to `OUT_DIR`. Required deps in `[build-dependencies]`.
- `crates/classic-cap/Cargo.toml` (MOD): add `libbpf-cargo` build-dep, `libbpf-rs` runtime dep, `[features] hw-bpf = []` gate (default off when running without root/BPF support).
- `crates/classic-cap/src/bpf/devices.bpf.c` (NEW): BPF program described above.
- `crates/classic-cap/src/bpf/loader.rs` (NEW): `pub struct BpfLoader; impl BpfLoader { pub fn attach(scope_fd: RawFd) -> Result<ProgHandle, BpfError>; }`; `ProgHandle::sync(&[CapSnapshot])` updates the map; Drop detaches the program.
- `crates/classic-cap/src/pci.rs` (NEW): `pub fn resolve_bdf(bdf: BdfAddr) -> Option<(u32 /*major*/, u32 /*minor*/)>` parsing `/sys/bus/pci/devices/<bdf>/uevent`.
- `crates/classic-cap/src/lib.rs` (MOD): `pub mod bpf;` + `pub mod pci;`; gate BPF behind `#[cfg(feature = "hw-bpf")]` if building without BPF in CI is required.
- `crates/classic-cap/src/broker.rs` (MOD): callbacks/listeners for acquire/release so loader can sync; or expose a `subscribe(Box<dyn Fn(MboxId, &[CapSnapshot])>)` API.

## Test plan

Unit (no BPF needed):
- `pci::resolve_bdf` against a tempdir-rooted `Sysroot` containing a fake `uevent` -> expected `(major,minor)` parsed.
- BPF program goldenfile: assert the compiled object's bytes hash matches a recorded sha256 in CI (pinned to one libbpf version).

Hardware/Linux gated (`#[cfg(feature = "hw-bpf")]`, `#[ignore]` by default):
- `BpfLoader::attach` to a real cgroup-v2 scope (created in test) succeeds; load test fails fast on kernel rejection (e.g. older than 6.1).
- After `sync(&[GpuMinor(0)])`, opening `/dev/nvidia0` from within the scope succeeds; opening `/dev/nvidia1` returns EPERM.
- After `sync(&[])`, opening any `/dev/nvidia*` returns EPERM.
- Drop on `ProgHandle` removes the program from the cgroup.

CI: non-hw tests must run on Linux 6.1+ x86_64 without root; hw tests require `cargo test --features hw-bpf -- --ignored`.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] AC-T3.1: `cargo build -p classic-cap` succeeds (build.rs compiles devices.bpf.c via libbpf-cargo) on Linux 6.1+ with clang installed.
- [ ] AC-T3.2: Goldenfile test confirms `devices.bpf.o` is byte-stable across runs (deterministic compile).
- [ ] AC-T3.3: `BpfLoader::attach(fd)` returns Ok on a real cgroup-v2 scope; Err with errno on kernel rejection.
- [ ] AC-T3.4 (`hw-bpf`): With only `GpuMinor(0)` synced, `open("/dev/nvidia0")` from a process inside the scope succeeds; `open("/dev/nvidia1")` returns EPERM. Matches plan AC-10.
- [ ] AC-T3.5: `sync(&[])` (empty allowlist) blocks all `/dev/nvidia*` opens; standard `(c,1,*)` set still works.
- [ ] AC-T3.6: `ProgHandle` Drop detaches the program (verifiable via `bpf_prog_query` or by observing subsequent opens are unrestricted on cleanup).
- [ ] AC-T3.7: `pci::resolve_bdf` parses uevent into `(major,minor)`; missing file -> `None`, no panic.
- [ ] AC-T3.8: `CapBroker` exposes a hook (callback or subscriber) such that every acquire/release triggers a `ProgHandle::sync` by the registrant — covered by a unit test using a stub loader.

## Intra-epic dependencies

- Parent: classic-1j3.
- Prereqs (within this epic): Task 1 (classic-vmr), Task 2 (classic-c22).
- Blocks: Task 4 (spawn core — uses ProgHandle when creating the scope).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] AC-T3.1: `cargo build -p classic-cap` succeeds (build.rs compiles devices.bpf.c via libbpf-cargo) on Linux 6.1+ with clang installed.
- [ ] AC-T3.2: Goldenfile test confirms `devices.bpf.o` is byte-stable across runs (deterministic compile).
- [ ] AC-T3.3: `BpfLoader::attach(fd)` returns Ok on a real cgroup-v2 scope; Err with errno on kernel rejection.
- [ ] AC-T3.4 (`hw-bpf`): With only `GpuMinor(0)` synced, `open("/dev/nvidia0")` from inside the scope succeeds; `/dev/nvidia1` returns EPERM. Matches plan AC-10.
- [ ] AC-T3.5: `sync(&[])` blocks all `/dev/nvidia*` opens; standard `(c,1,*)` set still works.
- [ ] AC-T3.6: `ProgHandle` Drop detaches the program from the cgroup.
- [ ] AC-T3.7: `pci::resolve_bdf` parses uevent into `(major,minor)`; missing file -> `None`, no panic.
- [ ] AC-T3.8: `CapBroker` exposes a hook so every acquire/release triggers `ProgHandle::sync` by the registrant (unit test with stub loader).

title: Scenario 2 script: No-match failure (clear error, no process started)
description:
Part of epic classic-o4c (Multi-node demo + integration tests).

Plan: plans/08-multinode-demo.md @ commit a7b6585. Read with:
  git show a7b6585:plans/08-multinode-demo.md

## Reading first
- git show a7b6585:plans/08-multinode-demo.md §Testing plan / Scenario 2 — No-match failure
- git show a7b6585:plans/03-placement-predicates.md (the --requires DSL semantics for non-matching predicates)
- git show a7b6585:plans/04-spawn-pipeline.md (`classic ps --all` semantics; how the placement layer surfaces 'no match')

## Excerpted design slice

### From plan §Testing plan / Scenario 2
Prerequisites: all three daemons up. No node has >= 1 TiB VRAM.
Command (on node-a):
  classic spawn \
    --requires 'any(gpu, gpu.vram_mb >= 1000000)' \
    -- ./cuda_hello
Expected stderr: contains literal `no node matches`.
Expected stdout: empty.
Expected exit code: non-zero.
Success means: placement layer refused before any SpawnRequest was sent. Verified by post-hoc `classic ps --all` showing no record on any node.

### From plan §Acceptance criteria
- AC-4: `make scenario-2` runs its script with the right CLASSIC_PEER env var and prints OK on success.

## Inputs
- examples/multinode/lib/assert.sh (from classic-lqx)
- examples/multinode/Makefile (from classic-d2p) — extend with scenario-2 target
- examples/multinode/programs/cuda_hello (from classic-sea) — referenced as the spawn target binary even though it never actually runs in this scenario
- classic CLI from plan 04
- CLASSIC_PEER env var

## Outputs
Files to create / modify:
- examples/multinode/scenarios/02-no-match.sh — shebang, executable, sources lib/assert.sh; runs the no-match spawn command above; captures stdout, stderr, exit; asserts exit non-zero; asserts stderr contains literal `no node matches`; asserts stdout empty; asserts post-hoc `classic ps --all` (against the same CLASSIC_PEER) shows no record of the rejected task; prints OK.
- examples/multinode/Makefile — add scenario-2 target invoking the script.

## Test plan
- bash -n examples/multinode/scenarios/02-no-match.sh — syntax check.
- shellcheck examples/multinode/scenarios/02-no-match.sh — clean.
- Real test (manual; requires real cluster up): `CLASSIC_PEER=10.42.0.10:7421 make -C examples/multinode scenario-2` — prints OK.
- Negative coverage: ensure that if any node DID match, the assertion fails (e.g. by lowering the threshold during local testing).

## Task-specific Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] examples/multinode/scenarios/02-no-match.sh exists, is executable, shellcheck-clean.
- [ ] Script invokes `classic spawn --requires 'any(gpu, gpu.vram_mb >= 1000000)' -- ./cuda_hello` exactly.
- [ ] Script asserts CLI exit non-zero, stderr contains literal `no node matches`, stdout empty.
- [ ] Script verifies via `classic ps --all` that no record of the rejected task exists on any node.
- [ ] examples/multinode/Makefile gains a scenario-2 target with CLASSIC_PEER propagation.
- [ ] Script prints OK on success.

## Non-obvious context
- 1 TiB VRAM (1000000 MiB) is the deliberately-impossible threshold; no real GPU advertises this. Do NOT change the threshold without updating the plan.
- The literal substring is `no node matches` (case-sensitive, exact bytes); plan 03/04 must produce this exact string in the human-readable error path.
- post-hoc `classic ps --all` must run against any peer (not just node-a) to confirm the spawn never reached a remote SpawnRequest. The script may iterate over all three CLASSIC_PEER values, or trust that gossip is symmetric and only check one. Document the choice inline.
- This scenario is part of the smoke set (CI) — see classic-XXX (xtask demo-smoke). Keep stdin/stdout deterministic.

## Intra-epic deps
- Depends on classic-lqx (assert.sh + directory layout)
- Depends on classic-sea (cuda_hello binary, even though it does not run)
- Depends on classic-d2p (Makefile skeleton — this task adds the scenario-2 target to it)
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] examples/multinode/scenarios/02-no-match.sh exists, is executable, shellcheck-clean.
- [ ] Script asserts CLI exit non-zero, stderr contains `no node matches`, stdout empty.
- [ ] Script verifies via `classic ps --all` that no record of the rejected task exists.
- [ ] Makefile has a scenario-2 target with CLASSIC_PEER propagation.
- [ ] Script prints OK on success.

title: classic-place: place() API + default_rank + place_str
description:
Epic: classic-i0n (Feature: Placement predicates)
Plan: plans/03-placement-predicates.md @ commit a7b6585. Read with: git show a7b6585:plans/03-placement-predicates.md

## Reading first

1. Plan §Design "Interfaces" — full public API surface.
2. Plan §Worked sequence: spawn-time placement (the call sequence place() participates in).
3. Plan §Requirements FR-5, FR-6 (sort order; default rank).

## Design slice (excerpted from plan §Design)

Public API (verbatim):
```rust
pub fn place(req: &Requirement, rank: &Rank, ads: &[NodeAd]) -> Vec<(NodeId, f64)>;
pub fn place_str(req_src: &str, rank_src: Option<&str>, ads: &[NodeAd])
    -> Result<Vec<(NodeId, f64)>, ParseError>;
pub fn default_rank() -> Rank;
```

Sort order (FR-5): primary key score (descending, NaN sorts last), secondary key NodeId bytes (ascending). Total and deterministic.

Default rank source text (FR-6, must parse to this exact expression):
`-load.cpu_pct - 1000.0 * (count(gpu) - count(gpu, gpu.in_use))`

Rationale: rewards low CPU load; rewards nodes with more *idle* GPUs by a heavy multiplier (one idle GPU dominates ~1000 CPU-pct points).

place_str behavior:
- `parse_req(req_src)` — propagate ParseError.
- `rank_src.map(parse_rank).transpose()?.unwrap_or_else(default_rank)`.
- filter ads by matches; score survivors; sort per FR-5; collect into Vec<(NodeId, f64)>.

## Inputs

- `crates/classic-place/src/typeck.rs` (Requirement, Rank).
- `crates/classic-place/src/eval.rs` (matches, score).
- `classic-ad::NodeAd` (NodeAd + NodeId).

## Outputs

- Update `crates/classic-place/src/lib.rs`:
  - `pub fn place(req: &Requirement, rank: &Rank, ads: &[NodeAd]) -> Vec<(NodeId, f64)>`.
  - `pub fn place_str(req_src: &str, rank_src: Option<&str>, ads: &[NodeAd]) -> Result<Vec<(NodeId, f64)>, ParseError>`.
  - `pub fn default_rank() -> Rank` — implemented as `parse_rank("-load.cpu_pct - 1000.0 * (count(gpu) - count(gpu, gpu.in_use))").expect("default rank parses")`. Constant text only; if parser is invoked once at startup, OK; consider lazy-initialization via OnceLock if profiling shows it matters (FR-6 only requires the source text to be the exact default; not a perf gate).

## Test plan

In-module `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` in lib.rs and a new integration test `crates/classic-place/tests/eval.rs` (NEW):

- Sort order:
  - 3 ads with scores [5.0, 5.0, 3.0] and NodeIds [b"\xff...", b"\x01...", b"\x02..."]: place() returns the two 5.0 entries first in NodeId-ascending order, then 3.0.
  - NaN scores sort last (build a fixture that triggers NEG_INFINITY rank or NaN explicitly via a div-by-zero rank).
- Filter then sort: a fixture with one ad failing the requirement is omitted; survivors are sorted.
- default_rank() returns a Rank that parses successfully and matches the documented source text. Add an exact-string assertion: round-trip the Rank back to source text *or* verify parse_rank with the exact string succeeds.
- place_str:
  - Happy path: `place_str(\"any(gpu, gpu.vram_mb >= 80000)\", None, &fixtures)` returns sorted candidates with at least one entry on the H100 fixture.
  - ParseError propagation: bad predicate text returns Err, never panics.
  - rank_src=None uses default_rank.

Run: `cargo test -p classic-place`.

## Acceptance criteria

- AC-PL1: place() filters by matches() then sorts by (score desc, NodeId bytes asc, NaN last). Order is total and deterministic.
- AC-PL2: place_str() parses req + rank, propagates ParseError, evaluates via place().
- AC-PL3: default_rank() returns a Rank that parses from the documented source text byte-for-byte.
- AC-PL4: place() with empty ads returns empty Vec, no panic.
- AC-PL5: place() with all-rejected ads returns empty Vec.
- AC-PL6: No unsafe; only existing classic-place deps.

## Intra-epic deps

- Depends on: evaluator (classic-kp7).
- Blocks: integration tests, frame round-trip tests.
acceptance_criteria:
AC-PL1: place() filters by matches() then sorts (score desc, NodeId bytes asc, NaN last). Total + deterministic.
AC-PL2: place_str() parses req + rank, propagates ParseError, evaluates via place().
AC-PL3: default_rank() returns a Rank that parses from the documented source text byte-for-byte.
AC-PL4: place() with empty ads returns empty Vec.
AC-PL5: place() with all-rejected ads returns empty Vec.
AC-PL6: No unsafe; only existing classic-place deps.

title: Scenario 1 + top-level Makefile: HW-affinity placement (CUDA hello on GPU node)
description:
Part of epic classic-o4c (Multi-node demo + integration tests).

Plan: plans/08-multinode-demo.md @ commit a7b6585. Read with:
  git show a7b6585:plans/08-multinode-demo.md

## Reading first
- git show a7b6585:plans/08-multinode-demo.md §Testing plan / Scenario 1 — HW-affinity placement
- git show a7b6585:plans/08-multinode-demo.md §Acceptance criteria (AC-3, AC-4)
- git show a7b6585:plans/03-placement-predicates.md (the --requires DSL: `any(gpu, gpu.vendor == 0x10de && gpu.vram_mb >= 8000)`)
- git show a7b6585:plans/04-spawn-pipeline.md (`classic spawn` CLI; stdio streaming; exit code propagation)

## Excerpted design slice

### From plan §Testing plan / Scenario 1
Prerequisites: all three daemons up; node-b advertises a GPU with >= 8 GiB VRAM in its NodeAd.
Command (on node-a):
  classic spawn \
    --requires 'any(gpu, gpu.vendor == 0x10de && gpu.vram_mb >= 8000)' \
    -- ./cuda_hello
Expected stdout: `hello from gpu 0 on node-b`.
Expected stderr: empty (modulo [classic]-tagged log lines, filtered).
Expected exit code: 0.
Success means: predicate matched node-b only; spawn pipeline staged the binary, applied the GPU device-cap, ran it on node-b, streamed stdout back, and reported the exit cleanly.

### From plan §Design / Testing plan
Each script lives in examples/multinode/scenarios/ and follows the shape: source lib/assert.sh, check prereqs, run the command, capture stdout / stderr / exit, assert, print OK.

### From plan §Acceptance criteria
- AC-3: `make setup` exits 0 on a healthy cluster, non-0 with a clear remediation hint when any precondition is missing (peer down, daemon down, NVML missing).
- AC-4: `make scenario-1` runs its script with the right CLASSIC_PEER env var and prints OK on success.

## Inputs
- examples/multinode/lib/assert.sh (from classic-lqx) — source for assert helpers
- examples/multinode/programs/cuda_hello (from classic-sea) — the binary spawned
- examples/multinode/config/*.toml (from classic-lqx) — the daemon configs
- classic CLI (from plan 04 / classic-cli crate) — the spawn entry point
- classicd (from plan 01 / classic-node) — running on each peer
- CLASSIC_PEER env var pointing at e.g. 10.42.0.10:7421 (node-a)

## Outputs
Files to create:
- examples/multinode/scenarios/01-hw-affinity.sh — shebang `#!/usr/bin/env bash`, executable bit; sources lib/assert.sh; checks CLASSIC_PEER set; runs the spawn command above with stdout/stderr captured to temp files; asserts exit code 0; asserts stdout filtered against `hello from gpu 0 on node-b`; asserts stderr empty (modulo [classic]-tagged lines); prints OK on success.
- examples/multinode/Makefile — top-level orchestrator. Targets:
    setup       — sanity-check peers reachable, daemons up, NVML visible on node-b; install scenario-3 coord-server bash one-liner; non-zero exit with remediation hint on any failure (AC-3)
    scenario-1  — `bash scenarios/01-hw-affinity.sh` (the only target wired in this task; later scenario tasks add scenario-2..5)
    all-real-hardware — placeholder; later tasks extend
    smoke       — placeholder; later tasks extend
    clean       — remove built binaries (programs/*/cuda_hello, programs/*/pci_lspci_loop), generated logs, Compose volumes/networks (later tasks extend; this task just removes program binaries)

The Makefile sets sensible defaults (CLASSIC_PEER ?= 10.42.0.10:7421) and propagates the env var to scripts.

## Test plan
- bash -n examples/multinode/scenarios/01-hw-affinity.sh — syntax check.
- shellcheck examples/multinode/scenarios/01-hw-affinity.sh — clean.
- Makefile dry-run: `make -C examples/multinode -n scenario-1` — prints the script invocation with CLASSIC_PEER passed through.
- Real test (manual; requires real cluster up): from node-a, `CLASSIC_PEER=10.42.0.10:7421 make -C examples/multinode scenario-1` — prints OK.
- Setup target negative test: kill classicd on node-b, run `make setup` — exits non-zero with a hint mentioning node-b unreachable.

## Task-specific Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] examples/multinode/scenarios/01-hw-affinity.sh exists, is executable, shellcheck-clean.
- [ ] Script sources lib/assert.sh, fails fast if CLASSIC_PEER unset.
- [ ] Script invokes `classic spawn --requires 'any(gpu, gpu.vendor == 0x10de && gpu.vram_mb >= 8000)' -- ./cuda_hello` exactly as plan §Testing plan / Scenario 1.

- [ ] `make clean` removes built program binaries (this task scope; later tasks extend with Compose volume cleanup).

## Non-obvious context
- The script must filter `[classic]`-tagged log lines from stderr before comparing it to empty — those are daemon log noise, not failure signal.
- Predicate spelling `--requires 'any(gpu, gpu.vendor == 0x10de && gpu.vram_mb >= 8000)'` is fixed at this exact form by plan §Testing plan / Scenario 1. If plan 03 or 04 settles on a different DSL spelling, scenario scripts update in the same PR (per plan §Open questions).
- 0x10de is NVIDIA's PCI vendor ID. 8000 MiB ~= 8 GiB. node-b is the only node advertising a matching GPU.
