title: classic-node: PeerLink lifecycle FSM + Hello handshake (version, self-loop, tiebreak)
description:
Part of epic classic-ivc. Plan: plans/01-skeleton-transport.md @ a7b6585.

## Reading first
- git show a7b6585:plans/01-skeleton-transport.md
- git show a7b6585:plans/ARCHITECTURE.md

## Excerpted design slice

### From plan § Design / Connection lifecycle state machine
> Each PeerLink runs this state machine. Inbound and outbound (dialed) connections share the state machine; the role field distinguishes them.
>
>    [Init]
>       |  TCP connected (dialed or accepted)
>       v
>    [HelloPending]
>       |  send Hello, await peer Hello with timeout 5s
>       |--peer Hello version mismatch -----> [Closed: HandshakeRejected]
>       |--peer Hello peer_node_id == self_node_id -> [Closed: SelfLoop]
>       |--peer Hello duplicates an existing PeerLink for that NodeId
>       |     and we are the higher NodeId in the ordered pair -----> [Closed: LosingTiebreak]
>       |--timeout (no Hello in 5s) -------------------------------> [Closed: HelloTimeout]
>       v  Hello OK
>    [Healthy] -- (covered by sibling task: heartbeat + unhealthy)
>
> State transitions and reason codes are emitted as tracing events at INFO so that the multinode-demo plan can grep them.

### From plan § Design / Hello handshake (exact sequence)
> 1. TCP connection established (dialer or listener side).
> 2. Local side serializes HelloPayload { proto_version: PROTO_VERSION, node_id: <our NodeId>, listen_addr: <our advertised addr>, capabilities: 0 }, wraps in Frame { kind: Hello, payload }, writes it.
> 3. Local side reads exactly one frame with a 5 s timeout. If the frame is not Hello, close with Error { code: ProtocolViolation }.
> 4. Validate proto_version == PROTO_VERSION. On mismatch, send Error { code: ProtoVersionMismatch } and close.
> 5. Validate peer_node_id != self_node_id. On match, log "self-loop detected" and close.
> 6. Tiebreak: if a PeerLink already exists for that NodeId and we are the higher of the two NodeIds on this connection, close (we lose; the lower-NodeId-initiated connection wins).
> 7. Promote to Healthy. Record peer_node_id -> PeerLink in the PeerMesh table. Start the heartbeat ticker.

### From plan § Requirements
> FR-6: Two peers exchange Hello and refuse the connection if proto_version differs.
> FR-7: When two peers race-connect each other, exactly one connection survives (the one initiated by the lower NodeId).

### From plan § Design / Public APIs (excerpt)
> Each PeerLink owns the codec, runs the Hello handshake, drives Heartbeat (sibling task), forwards inbound frames to the multiplexer.
> A backpressure note (open question): tokio::sync::mpsc with bound 1024 frames per peer; oversend triggers a link reset.

## Inputs
- classic-proto: Frame, FrameKind, encode_frame/decode_frame, CodecError, NodeId, PROTO_VERSION, HelloPayload, ErrorPayload, ErrorCode, Connection trait (from sibling tasks classic-53m, classic-4ef)
- tokio (TcpStream, time::timeout, sync::mpsc), tracing

## Outputs
Files under /home/horde/src/classic/crates/classic-node/:
- src/link.rs — PeerLink struct, role enum (Dialer | Listener), lifecycle FSM, Hello handshake
- src/lib.rs — pub mod link;

Public surface (sketch):
```rust
pub enum PeerRole { Dialer, Listener }

pub enum PeerState {
    Connecting,
    HelloPending,
    Healthy,
    Unhealthy,                 // populated by sibling heartbeat task
    Closed(CloseReason),
}

pub enum CloseReason {
    HandshakeRejected,         // ProtoVersionMismatch / ProtocolViolation
    SelfLoop,
    LosingTiebreak,
    HelloTimeout,
    PeerBye,
    PeerError,
    TransportLost,
}

pub struct PeerLink { /* fields: peer_id (set after Hello), state, role, send_tx, ... */ }

impl PeerLink {
    /// Run handshake; return the established link in Healthy or an error explaining the close reason.
    pub async fn handshake<S: AsyncRead + AsyncWrite + Unpin + Send>(
        stream: S,
        role: PeerRole,
        self_id: NodeId,
        listen_addr: String,
        existing: &dyn ExistingPeerLookup,
    ) -> Result<PeerLink, CloseReason>;
}

pub trait ExistingPeerLookup {
    fn has_link_for(&self, peer: NodeId) -> bool;
}
```

Notes:
- The send-loop uses a tokio::sync::mpsc::channel(1024) for the tx side.
- Tiebreak: pass through ExistingPeerLookup so this task does not depend on PeerMesh internals — sibling task classic-? wires PeerMesh to satisfy the trait.
- Each state transition emits tracing::info! event with fields: peer=node_id, role, from, to, reason.
- Hello timeout uses tokio::time::timeout(Duration::from_secs(5), ...).
- A non-Hello first frame triggers send of Error { ProtocolViolation } before close.

## Test plan
Unit tests over tokio::io::duplex pairs:
- cargo test -p classic-node link::tests::handshake_ok — two PeerLinks complete handshake and reach Healthy.
- cargo test -p classic-node link::tests::handshake_version_mismatch — peer with proto_version=2 is rejected with ProtoVersionMismatch (assert close reason and that an Error frame was sent).
- cargo test -p classic-node link::tests::self_loop — peer Hello carries our own NodeId; close reason SelfLoop.
- cargo test -p classic-node link::tests::tiebreak — given an existing-link lookup that returns true, the higher-NodeId side closes with LosingTiebreak; the lower-NodeId side keeps the connection.
- cargo test -p classic-node link::tests::hello_timeout — peer never sends Hello within 5 s (use tokio::time::pause + advance); close reason HelloTimeout.
- cargo test -p classic-node link::tests::non_hello_first_frame — peer sends a Heartbeat first; we send Error{ProtocolViolation} and close.

## Task-specific Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] PeerState and CloseReason enums match the plan's lifecycle FSM.
- [ ] handshake_ok test passes on a duplex pair.
- [ ] Version mismatch closes with HandshakeRejected and sends Error{ProtoVersionMismatch}.
- [ ] Self-loop detected and closed with SelfLoop.
- [ ] Tiebreak: higher-NodeId side closes with LosingTiebreak when ExistingPeerLookup says yes; lower-NodeId side wins.
- [ ] Hello timeout (5s) closes with HelloTimeout (verified with paused tokio time).
- [ ] Each FSM transition produces a tracing::info event with structured fields.

## Intra-epic deps
- Blocks-on: classic-53m (Frame + codec), classic-4ef (HelloPayload + Connection + PROTO_VERSION)
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] PeerState and CloseReason match plan FSM.
- [ ] handshake_ok over duplex pair reaches Healthy.
- [ ] Version mismatch sends Error{ProtoVersionMismatch} and closes HandshakeRejected.
- [ ] Self-loop closes with SelfLoop.
- [ ] Tiebreak: higher NodeId loses, lower NodeId wins.
- [ ] Hello timeout (5s) closes with HelloTimeout (paused-clock test).
- [ ] Every FSM transition emits a tracing::info structured event.

title: classic-place: evaluator (matches + score over NodeAd)
description:
Epic: classic-i0n (Feature: Placement predicates)
Plan: plans/03-placement-predicates.md @ commit a7b6585. Read with: git show a7b6585:plans/03-placement-predicates.md

## Reading first

1. Plan §Design "Values during eval" (Val enum).
2. Plan §Design "Interfaces" — `matches`, `score` signatures + field-skew semantics.
3. Plan §Requirements FR-3, FR-4, FR-7, FR-8, FR-10 (functional reqs the evaluator owns).

## Design slice (excerpted from plan §Design)

```rust
enum Val {
    Bool(bool),
    Int(i64),
    Float(f64),
    Str(String),
}
```

Numeric ops widen to Float if either operand is Float. Comparisons follow the same rule. There is no implicit string <-> number coercion.

Aggregates over collections (gpu, pci) introduce iteration variable binding that shadows outer fields. Empty collections: `any(...)`=false, `all(...)`=true, `count(...)`=0 (vacuous truth, FR-7).

`x in [a,b,c]` is sugar for `x == a || x == b || x == c`. Already lowered in AST as `In(x, [a,b,c])`. `x in []` is false (FR-8).

Field-skew tolerance (FR-10): a NodeAd missing a field referenced in a requirement causes that requirement to evaluate to `false` (no panic) and the eval as a whole short-circuits to false. For ranks, a missing field contributes the surrounding op's neutral element (0 for sum, 1 for product). `score` returns `f64::NEG_INFINITY` only if the rank can't be evaluated at all.

API (verbatim):
```rust
pub fn matches(req: &Requirement, ad: &NodeAd) -> bool;
pub fn score(rank: &Rank, ad: &NodeAd) -> f64;
```

Never panics. Allocations: `matches` allocates zero beyond temporary Vals on the eval stack (per FR-3).

## Inputs

- `crates/classic-place/src/ast.rs` (Expr enum).
- `crates/classic-place/src/typeck.rs` (Requirement / Rank wrappers).
- `classic-ad::NodeAd` schema (fields documented in plan §Data shapes; if classic-ad not yet implemented, define a minimal local shim of the NodeAd struct in `crates/classic-place/src/tests/fixtures.rs` per the fixture-builder task — this evaluator just needs to know the field names + types).

## Outputs

- New file `crates/classic-place/src/eval.rs`:
  - `enum Val { Bool(bool), Int(i64), Float(f64), Str(String) }` (private).
  - `pub fn matches(req: &Requirement, ad: &NodeAd) -> bool`.
  - `pub fn score(rank: &Rank, ad: &NodeAd) -> f64`.
  - Internal recursive walker over Expr that takes an environment threading the iteration-binding (None, or Some(GpuAd / PciAd) with name).
  - Field resolver that maps Field(["gpu", "vram_mb"]) (when not inside an aggregate) to the collection-only context, and Field(["gpu", "vram_mb"]) inside `any(gpu, ...)` to the bound element's vram_mb.
  - Numeric widening: Int op Int -> Int; Int op Float (or Float op Int) -> Float; Float op Float -> Float.
  - `In` evaluator: lower at eval time too — Bool of any equality match.
- Update lib.rs to declare `mod eval;` and re-export `matches`, `score`.

## Test plan

In-module `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` in eval.rs (table-driven where possible). Use fixture builders from the fixture task once available; for this task's local tests, hand-build minimal NodeAd values inline:

- Boolean ops: short-circuit for `||` and `&&`.
- Comparisons across all six operators with Int, Float, mixed (widening), Str.
- Numeric ops: `+ - * /` with int/float widening; `/ 0` returns Float NaN or rank-eval-fail (decide and document; plan does not pin div-by-zero behavior — pick: int / 0 -> rank evaluation fails -> NEG_INFINITY for surrounding rank; matches treats it as field-skew = false).
- Aggregates:
  - `any(gpu, gpu.vram_mb >= 80000)` true on H100, false on A100-40GB and CPU-only.
  - `all(gpu, gpu.in_use == false)` true on idle 8xH100, false on busy mix.
  - `count(gpu)` arity-1 returns vec length.
  - `count(gpu, !gpu.in_use)` arity-2 returns predicate count.
  - Empty collection: `any(gpu, ...)` -> false, `all(gpu, ...)` -> true, `count(gpu)` -> 0.
- `in` membership: `cpu.arch in [\"x86_64\", \"aarch64\"]` -> true on either fixture; `x in []` -> false.
- Field skew: synthetic NodeAd with empty gpu vec against `any(gpu, gpu.vram_mb >= 80000)` -> false, no panic.
- Rank evaluation: default rank `-load.cpu_pct - 1000.0 * (count(gpu) - count(gpu, gpu.in_use))` produces the documented score (e.g. -10 - 4*1000 = -4010 for 4-idle-GPU + 10% CPU node).
- Determinism / no allocation: smoke test with a tight loop running matches() 1000x against the same ad+req — no test-time leaks (visible via cargo test --release on a debug-allocator if desired; not required).

Run: `cargo test -p classic-place eval`.

## Acceptance criteria

- AC-E1: `matches` returns the predicate's truth value against any NodeAd; never panics.
- AC-E2: `score` returns f64; integer rank expressions cast to f64 at boundary.
- AC-E3: Empty-collection vacuous truth: any=false, all=true, count=0.
- AC-E4: `in` semantics: `x in []` is false; non-empty list is OR-of-equalities.
- AC-E5: Field skew: missing field in a Requirement -> false; missing field in Rank -> neutral element of surrounding op (or NEG_INFINITY if rank cannot evaluate at all).
- AC-E6: Numeric widening Int->Float when either side is Float in both arithmetic and comparison.
- AC-E7: Aggregate iteration binding correctly shadows outer field of same name.
- AC-E8: Default rank text evaluates to documented values on representative fixtures.
- AC-E9: No unsafe; std-only; no allocations beyond eval-stack temporaries (best-effort, asserted by inspection of eval.rs source).

## Intra-epic deps

- Depends on: parser (classic-sdi), type-check (classic-kwj).
- Blocks: place() API, place_str, fixtures, tests.
acceptance_criteria:
AC-E1: matches returns predicate truth value; never panics.
AC-E2: score returns f64; int rank expressions cast to f64 at boundary.
AC-E3: Empty-collection vacuous truth: any=false, all=true, count=0.
AC-E4: `in` semantics: `x in []` is false; otherwise OR-of-equalities.
AC-E5: Field skew: missing field -> false (matches) or neutral element / NEG_INFINITY (score).
AC-E6: Numeric widening Int->Float when either side is Float (arithmetic + comparison).
AC-E7: Aggregate iteration binding shadows outer field of same name.
AC-E8: Default rank text evaluates to documented values on representative fixtures.
AC-E9: No unsafe; std-only.

title: classic-cap: cgroup-v2 hierarchy (classicd.slice + per-task .scope cpu/mem/pids)
description:
# Task 2 — cgroup-v2 hierarchy: classicd.slice + per-task `.scope` dirs (cpu/memory/pids)

Parent epic: classic-1j3 (Feature: Spawn pipeline). Plan: plans/04-spawn-pipeline.md @ commit a7b6585. Read with: git show a7b6585:plans/04-spawn-pipeline.md.

## Reading first

Read plans/04-spawn-pipeline.md "Cgroup setup" subsection in full, plus plans/ARCHITECTURE.md "Linux runtime assumptions" (kernel >= 6.1 unified hierarchy mandate). Skim Linux Documentation/admin-guide/cgroup-v2.rst.

## Scope

Build the cgroup-v2 sysfs setup/teardown helpers in `crates/classic-cap/src/cgroup.rs`. cpu/memory/pids controllers only (devices controller is BPF, lands in Task 3). NO spawn-side use yet (Task 4).

In scope:
- One-time daemon-startup helper: ensure `/sys/fs/cgroup/classicd.slice` exists, write `+cpu +memory +pids` to both `cgroup.subtree_control` files.
- Per-task helpers: create `/sys/fs/cgroup/classicd.slice/task-<MboxId>.scope/`; write `max` to `memory.max`, `max` to `cpu.max`, `1024` to `pids.max`; write a helper PID into `cgroup.procs`; teardown rmdir the scope.
- A `Sysroot`-style trait (or path-prefix injection) so unit tests can swap a tempdir fixture for `/`.
- Surface a `cgroup_fd(scope_path) -> RawFd` open of the directory for Task 3 to attach the BPF program to.

Out of scope:
- BPF device controller (Task 3).
- CPU/memory limits derived from the predicate (post-v1; v1 writes `max`).
- spawn-side wiring (Task 4).

## Design slice

Per-task scope path: `/sys/fs/cgroup/classicd.slice/task-<MboxId>.scope/`.

Sysfs writes (exact sequence required):

```bash
# One-time at daemon startup:
mkdir -p /sys/fs/cgroup/classicd.slice
echo "+cpu +memory +pids" > /sys/fs/cgroup/cgroup.subtree_control
echo "+cpu +memory +pids" > /sys/fs/cgroup/classicd.slice/cgroup.subtree_control

# Per task, before fork:
SCOPE=/sys/fs/cgroup/classicd.slice/task-${MBOX}.scope
mkdir "$SCOPE"
echo "max"  > "$SCOPE/memory.max"
echo "max"  > "$SCOPE/cpu.max"
echo 1024   > "$SCOPE/pids.max"
echo $HELPER_PID > "$SCOPE/cgroup.procs"
```

CPU/memory limits are `max` in v1 — predicate-driven quotas are a follow-up; the hierarchy is in place so they can be added without re-architecting.

## Inputs

- `crates/classic-cap/src/lib.rs` (created in Task 1) — extend with cgroup module.
- `classic-proto::MboxId` — used for scope dir naming.
- plans/04-spawn-pipeline.md — design source.

## Outputs

Files to create/modify:
- `crates/classic-cap/src/cgroup.rs` (NEW): `pub fn ensure_slice(root: &Sysroot)`, `pub fn create_scope(root: &Sysroot, mbox: MboxId, helper_pid: i32) -> Result<ScopeHandle>`, `pub fn open_scope_fd(scope: &ScopeHandle) -> RawFd`, `Drop` on ScopeHandle removes the directory.
- `crates/classic-cap/src/lib.rs` (MOD): `pub mod cgroup;` and re-exports.
- `Sysroot` abstraction in either `cgroup.rs` or a small `sysroot.rs` module.
- Unit tests under `crates/classic-cap/tests/` using a tempdir-rooted Sysroot.

## Test plan

`cargo test -p classic-cap` (no root needed if Sysroot is tempdir-rooted):
- `ensure_slice` against tempdir creates `classicd.slice/` and writes the exact two `+cpu +memory +pids` strings; assert byte-equal contents.
- `create_scope(mbox=42, pid=$$)` produces directory `task-42.scope` with files `memory.max == "max\n"`, `cpu.max == "max\n"`, `pids.max == "1024\n"`, `cgroup.procs` containing `$$`.
- `ScopeHandle` Drop removes the directory.
- Failure injection: inject EPERM/ENOSPC on mkdir; assert error is propagated with `errno` accessible (callers will translate to `SpawnDeny{CgroupSetupFailed, detail:<errno>}` later).
- `open_scope_fd` returns a directory FD on a real `/sys/fs/cgroup` ONLY if running under root (gated `#[ignore]` or root-detection); otherwise opens the tempdir scope and asserts `O_DIRECTORY`.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] AC-T2.1: `cargo test -p classic-cap` green; cgroup tests use a tempdir Sysroot and require neither root nor a real cgroup-v2 mount.
- [ ] AC-T2.2: `ensure_slice` writes EXACTLY the two byte-strings shown in the design slice (no trailing junk; LF only).
- [ ] AC-T2.3: `create_scope(mbox)` produces a directory named `task-<mbox>.scope` with `memory.max=max`, `cpu.max=max`, `pids.max=1024`, `cgroup.procs=<helper_pid>`.
- [ ] AC-T2.4: `ScopeHandle` Drop removes the scope directory; the handle can also be torn down explicitly via a `teardown()` method that returns errors.
- [ ] AC-T2.5: Errors from sysfs writes carry `std::io::Error` (errno-bearing) and are typed so callers can map to `DenyReason::CgroupSetupFailed`.
- [ ] AC-T2.6: `open_scope_fd` returns a `RawFd` for the scope directory suitable for BPF attach in Task 3.

## Intra-epic dependencies

- Parent: classic-1j3.
- Prereqs (within this epic): Task 1 (classic-vmr) — extends the same crate.
- Blocks: Task 3 (BPF program — needs `open_scope_fd`), Task 4 (spawn core — needs scope create/teardown).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] AC-T2.1: `cargo test -p classic-cap` green; cgroup tests use a tempdir Sysroot and require neither root nor a real cgroup-v2 mount.
- [ ] AC-T2.2: `ensure_slice` writes EXACTLY the two byte-strings shown in the design slice (no trailing junk; LF only).
- [ ] AC-T2.3: `create_scope(mbox)` produces a directory named `task-<mbox>.scope` with `memory.max=max`, `cpu.max=max`, `pids.max=1024`, `cgroup.procs=<helper_pid>`.
- [ ] AC-T2.4: `ScopeHandle` Drop removes the scope directory; explicit `teardown()` method also exists and returns errors.
- [ ] AC-T2.5: Errors from sysfs writes carry `std::io::Error` (errno-bearing) and are typed so callers can map to `DenyReason::CgroupSetupFailed`.
- [ ] AC-T2.6: `open_scope_fd` returns a `RawFd` for the scope directory suitable for BPF attach in Task 3.

title: classic-cli: 'classic nodes'/'classic ad' subcommand + classic-node bring-up wiring
description:
Part of epic classic-ygs. Plan: plans/02-node-ad-hw-discovery.md @ a7b6585.

## Reading first
- git show a7b6585:plans/02-node-ad-hw-discovery.md (sections: Testing plan — End-to-end, Acceptance criteria AC-3/AC-4/AC-5/AC-6, File / crate layout)
- plans/ARCHITECTURE.md (crate dep direction; classic-cli -> classic-node; classic-node depends on classic-ad)

## Design slice (verbatim)
classic-node:
  src/main.rs  # MODIFIED — call classic_ad::start() during bring-up

End-to-end manual smoke tests (run on three Ubuntu 24.04 hosts):

  cargo run -p classic-node -- --listen 0.0.0.0:7777 --peers host1:7777,host2:7777,host3:7777
  cargo run -p classic-cli -- ad list --json | jq '.[].hostname'   # expect 3 lines within 15 s
  cargo run -p classic-cli -- ad show host3 | jq '.gpus | length'  # 0 on CPU host, ≥1 elsewhere

(classic-cli ad subcommands are part of plan 04's CLI but stubs that just dump AdStore::all_ads() are added by this plan to support smoke testing.)

This task implements:
1. classic-node bring-up: in main.rs, after classic-proto's ConnectionRegistry is up, call classic_ad::start(self_node_id, registry, AdConfig::default()) and stash the returned AdStore in the daemon's shared state for later plans (place/spawn). Expose the AdStore via a simple in-process control endpoint (admin socket / IPC) that classic-cli can query.
2. classic-cli stubs: 'classic ad list' and 'classic ad show <host>' subcommands that connect to the daemon's control endpoint and dump AdStore::all_ads() / peer(<id>) as JSON or a short text table.
3. The CLI must NOT reach into AdStore directly — it queries the daemon over the control endpoint that classic-node exposes.

## Inputs
- crates/classic-ad/src/lib.rs::start() and AdStore (from prior tasks)
- crates/classic-node/src/main.rs (existing — extend bring-up sequence)
- crates/classic-cli/src/main.rs (existing — extend with subcommand)
- classic-node's local control IPC mechanism (UDS socket or pre-existing admin endpoint from plan 01)

## Outputs
- crates/classic-node/src/main.rs (modified — wire classic_ad::start, expose AdStore via control endpoint)
- crates/classic-cli/src/{main.rs, commands/ad.rs} (modified/new — 'ad list' and 'ad show' subcommands)
- Optionally a tiny shared protocol struct for the control endpoint request/response (lives wherever the existing control protocol lives — DO NOT add new wire-format frames to classic-proto's 0x0100-0x01FF range; that range is for cluster gossip)

## Test plan
- Unit: classic-cli 'ad list --json' against a fake control-endpoint server that returns a fixture all_ads() — assert JSON shape, exit code 0.
- Manual smoke (documented for human runners): run three classicd daemons on three hosts; within 15 s, classic ad list shows three hostnames on each (AC-3).
- Manual smoke: rmmod nvidia on one host before starting daemon; classic ad show <that host> | jq '.gpus | length' returns 0; classicd log contains exactly one warning with 'NVML' (AC-4).
- Manual smoke: kill one daemon; survivors' classic ad list drops the dead host within peer_grace + gossip_period ≈ 100s (AC-5). Restart it; reappears within gossip_period (10s) with strictly greater boot_time (AC-6).

## Acceptance
- [ ] classic-node main.rs calls classic_ad::start() during bring-up and keeps the AdStore alive for the daemon's lifetime.
- [ ] classic-cli has 'ad list' and 'ad show <host>' subcommands that query the daemon's control endpoint and print JSON or a text table.
- [ ] Manual three-node smoke test confirms AC-3 (3 ads within 15s) and AC-5/AC-6 (eviction + restart visibility).
- [ ] No new frame kinds added in the 0x0100-0x01FF range beyond NodeAd/AdGossip/AdRequest. The CLI<->daemon control channel is local-only (UDS) and not part of cluster gossip.
acceptance_criteria:
classic-node main.rs calls classic_ad::start() during bring-up and keeps the AdStore alive for the daemon's lifetime.
classic-cli has 'ad list' and 'ad show <host>' subcommands that query the daemon's control endpoint and print JSON or a text table.
Manual three-node smoke test confirms AC-3 (3 ads within 15s) and AC-5/AC-6 (eviction + restart visibility).
No new frame kinds added in the 0x0100-0x01FF range beyond NodeAd/AdGossip/AdRequest.

title: Demo programs: cuda_hello.cu, pci_lspci_loop.c, per-program Makefiles
description:
Part of epic classic-o4c (Multi-node demo + integration tests).

Plan: plans/08-multinode-demo.md @ commit a7b6585. Read with:
  git show a7b6585:plans/08-multinode-demo.md
Foundations: git show a7b6585:plans/ARCHITECTURE.md

## Reading first
- git show a7b6585:plans/08-multinode-demo.md §Design / Demo programs and §Testing plan / Scenario 1, Scenario 4

## Excerpted design slice

### From plan §Design / Demo programs
- cuda_hello.cu — single CUDA kernel that prints `hello from gpu <ord> on node <hostname>` from device 0 and exits 0. CI replaces it with a host-only stub printing the same line when CLASSIC_NVML_STUB=1 is set.
- pci_lspci_loop.c — opens the namespace path passed on argv (e.g. /cluster/B/dev/pci/), reads each entry, prints `pci: <bdf> <vendor>:<device>`, sleeps 100 ms, repeats 10x. Pure libc.

### From plan §Testing plan / Scenario 1
Expected stdout: `hello from gpu 0 on node-b`.
Expected stderr: empty (modulo [classic]-tagged log lines, filtered).

### From plan §Testing plan / Scenario 4
Expected stdout: at least one line per PCI device on node-b matching:
  ^pci: [0-9a-f]{4}:[0-9a-f]{2}:[0-9a-f]{2}\.[0-9] [0-9a-f]{4}:[0-9a-f]{4}$
repeated 10x.

## Inputs
- examples/multinode/ directory layout (created by classic-lqx)
- nvcc available at build time (real-hardware path); fallback host-only build under CLASSIC_NVML_STUB=1
- Standard libc + headers

## Outputs
Files to create:
- examples/multinode/programs/cuda_hello/Makefile — two build modes: default uses nvcc to produce ./cuda_hello (a CUDA executable); when CLASSIC_NVML_STUB=1 is set in the environment, builds a host-only stub via cc that prints the same line and exits 0. clean target removes the binary.
- examples/multinode/programs/cuda_hello/cuda_hello.cu — single CUDA kernel that prints `hello from gpu <ord> on node <hostname>` from device 0 then exits 0. Must use cudaGetDevice (or equivalent) for <ord> and gethostname for <hostname>.
- examples/multinode/programs/pci_lspci_loop/Makefile — cc -Wall -Wextra -O2 -o pci_lspci_loop pci_lspci_loop.c. clean target removes the binary.
- examples/multinode/programs/pci_lspci_loop/pci_lspci_loop.c — argv[1] is namespace path. opendir, readdir, parse PCI entry to extract BDF (bus:dev.func) and read vendor/device files (or compatible) to format `pci: <bdf> <vendor>:<device>`. Sleep 100 ms between iterations. Repeat 10x. Use only libc.

## Test plan
- Build cuda_hello in stub mode: `CLASSIC_NVML_STUB=1 make -C examples/multinode/programs/cuda_hello`. Run produced binary; assert stdout matches `^hello from gpu 0 on node \S+$`. Exit 0.
- Build cuda_hello in real CUDA mode (skipped on hosts without nvcc): `make -C examples/multinode/programs/cuda_hello`. Run on a GPU host; assert stdout matches `^hello from gpu 0 on node \S+$`.
- Build pci_lspci_loop: `make -C examples/multinode/programs/pci_lspci_loop`. Run `./pci_lspci_loop /sys/bus/pci/devices/`. Assert stdout has 10x N lines (where N = device count) each matching `^pci: [0-9a-f]{4}:[0-9a-f]{2}:[0-9a-f]{2}\.[0-9] [0-9a-f]{4}:[0-9a-f]{4}$`. Total runtime > 900 ms (10 iterations x 100 ms sleep).
- Lint: shellcheck-clean Makefiles (we want no shell errors); -Wall -Wextra -Werror compiles clean.

## Task-specific Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] examples/multinode/programs/cuda_hello/{Makefile,cuda_hello.cu} exists.
- [ ] examples/multinode/programs/pci_lspci_loop/{Makefile,pci_lspci_loop.c} exists.
- [ ] cuda_hello stub mode (CLASSIC_NVML_STUB=1) builds via cc and prints the documented line.
- [ ] cuda_hello real CUDA mode builds via nvcc on a host with CUDA toolkit installed.
- [ ] pci_lspci_loop builds with cc -Wall -Wextra -Werror with no warnings.
- [ ] pci_lspci_loop output, when given /sys/bus/pci/devices/, matches the regex from plan §Testing plan / Scenario 4 (10x device count).
- [ ] Each program's Makefile has working clean target.
- [ ] No new dependency on libraries beyond libc and the CUDA toolkit (the latter only for non-stub mode).

## Non-obvious context
- Stub-mode cuda_hello is required because CI runs in Compose containers without GPUs. The LD_PRELOAD nvml-stub (downstream task classic-XXX) does NOT replace cuda_hello — cuda_hello must self-stub via CLASSIC_NVML_STUB=1.
- pci_lspci_loop is invoked over a 9P mount in scenario 4: the namespace path will look like /cluster/B/dev/pci/. The program must NOT assume /sys/bus/pci layout — it just enumerates directory entries and reads vendor/device files at relative paths. Plan 06 owns the 9P file layout exposed under /dev/pci/.
- Regex constraint: BDF format is exactly XXXX:XX:XX.X (domain:bus:device.function), and vendor:device is exactly XXXX:XXXX (4 hex digits each).

## Intra-epic deps
- Depends on classic-lqx (directory layout established before programs land in it).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] examples/multinode/programs/cuda_hello/{Makefile,cuda_hello.cu} and pci_lspci_loop/{Makefile,pci_lspci_loop.c} exist.
- [ ] cuda_hello stub mode (CLASSIC_NVML_STUB=1) builds via cc and prints `hello from gpu 0 on node <hostname>`.
- [ ] cuda_hello real-CUDA mode builds via nvcc on a host with CUDA toolkit.
- [ ] pci_lspci_loop builds with cc -Wall -Wextra -Werror clean.
- [ ] pci_lspci_loop output matches the BDF regex from plan §Testing plan / Scenario 4, 10x per device.
- [ ] Both Makefiles have working clean targets and use no libraries beyond libc + CUDA toolkit.

title: classic-node: TOML config loader + NodeId generation/persistence
description:
Part of epic classic-ivc. Plan: plans/01-skeleton-transport.md @ a7b6585.

## Reading first
- git show a7b6585:plans/01-skeleton-transport.md
- git show a7b6585:plans/ARCHITECTURE.md

## Excerpted design slice

### From plan § Design / TOML config schema
> Config file path resolution, in order:
> 1. --config <path> CLI flag (highest priority).
> 2. /etc/classicd/config.toml.
> 3. $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/classicd/config.toml (fallback for non-root dev runs).
>
> ```toml
> [node]
> listen_addr = "0.0.0.0:7421"
> state_dir   = "/var/lib/classicd"
> peers       = ["10.0.0.2:7421", "10.0.0.3:7421"]
>
> [log]
> level = "info"
> ```
>
> ```rust
> #[derive(Deserialize)]
> pub struct Config { pub node: NodeConfig, #[serde(default)] pub log: LogConfig }
> #[derive(Deserialize)]
> pub struct NodeConfig { pub listen_addr: String, pub state_dir: PathBuf, #[serde(default)] pub peers: Vec<String> }
> #[derive(Deserialize, Default)]
> pub struct LogConfig { #[serde(default)] pub level: Option<String> }
> ```
>
> Config is loaded once at startup; SIGHUP-triggered reload is **not** in scope. A config error is fatal and aborts startup with a clear log message.

### From plan § Design / NodeId generation and persistence
> - On startup, classic-node reads state_dir/node_id.
> - File present: parse 16 raw bytes, construct NodeId. A short or malformed file is fatal — bail with a clear error.
> - File absent: call getrandom::getrandom(&mut [u8; 16]), write the 16 bytes to state_dir/node_id with mode 0600, then construct NodeId. Use an atomic write (write + rename) to avoid half-written files on crash.
> - The state_dir itself is created with 0700 if missing.

### From plan § Requirements
> FR-1: classicd parses a TOML config from --config or the default paths and fails fast on malformed input.
> FR-2: On first start, classicd generates a fresh NodeId and persists it to state_dir/node_id with mode 0600.
> FR-3: On subsequent starts, classicd reads the persisted NodeId verbatim — the same identity must survive a restart.

### From plan § CLI surface (classicd) — exit codes
> 1   config error
> 2   state-dir error

## Inputs
- classic-proto::NodeId from task classic-53m
- toml crate (deserialization), serde, getrandom, thiserror, std::fs, std::os::unix::fs::PermissionsExt

## Outputs
Files under /home/horde/src/classic/crates/classic-node/:
- src/config.rs — Config, NodeConfig, LogConfig structs; pub fn load_config(cli_path: Option<PathBuf>) -> Result<Config, ConfigError>; ConfigError type
- src/node_id.rs — pub fn ensure_node_id(state_dir: &Path) -> Result<NodeId, NodeIdError>; NodeIdError type
- src/lib.rs — pub mod config; pub mod node_id;
- crates/classic-node/Cargo.toml — add deps: classic-proto, toml, serde, getrandom, thiserror, dirs (for XDG_CONFIG_HOME resolution)

Behavior contracts:
- load_config tries --config first; otherwise /etc/classicd/config.toml; otherwise XDG_CONFIG_HOME/classicd/config.toml. First existing wins.
- Missing file when CLI path given => fatal config error (do NOT fall back).
- Malformed TOML, missing required keys (listen_addr, state_dir) => fatal config error.
- ensure_node_id creates state_dir mode 0700 if missing; reads or generates 16 bytes; writes new IDs atomically (tmp file + rename) at mode 0600.
- Short/garbage node_id file => return error (translate to exit 2 in main).

## Test plan
- cargo test -p classic-node config::tests::roundtrip — parse the example TOML and assert fields.
- cargo test -p classic-node config::tests::missing_required — TOML with no [node] section fails clearly.
- cargo test -p classic-node config::tests::cli_overrides — --config wins over default paths (use a tempdir).
- cargo test -p classic-node node_id::tests::generate_and_persist — first call to ensure_node_id creates a 16-byte file mode 0600; second call returns the same NodeId.
- cargo test -p classic-node node_id::tests::malformed_fatal — write a 5-byte file at state_dir/node_id; ensure_node_id returns an error.
- cargo test -p classic-node node_id::tests::state_dir_permissions — when state_dir does not exist, ensure_node_id creates it with mode 0700.

## Task-specific Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Config struct deserializes the example TOML successfully.
- [ ] load_config respects CLI > /etc > XDG fallback order.
- [ ] Missing required keys produce a fatal ConfigError; CLI explicitly missing also fatal.
- [ ] First-start path: state_dir created mode 0700, node_id file created mode 0600 with 16 random bytes via getrandom.
- [ ] Restart path: NodeId is byte-identical across two ensure_node_id calls.
- [ ] Short/malformed node_id file returns an error (caller maps to exit code 2).
- [ ] cargo test -p classic-node config::tests::* node_id::tests::* green.

## Intra-epic deps
- Blocks-on: classic-fxu (workspace), classic-53m (NodeId type)
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Config struct round-trips example TOML.
- [ ] CLI > /etc > XDG path resolution works.
- [ ] Missing required keys = fatal error.
- [ ] First-start creates state_dir mode 0700 and node_id mode 0600 with 16 random bytes.
- [ ] Restart returns byte-identical NodeId.
- [ ] Short/malformed node_id file is fatal.
- [ ] config::tests + node_id::tests green.

title: classic-ad: Gossip RX + TX (10s ticker, connection-up hook, peer drop)
description:
Part of epic classic-ygs. Plan: plans/02-node-ad-hw-discovery.md @ a7b6585.

## Reading first
- git show a7b6585:plans/02-node-ad-hw-discovery.md (sections: Architecture — Gossip RX/TX, Sequence — bring-up + gossip, Sequence — peer drop, FR-7/FR-8/FR-9)
- plans/ARCHITECTURE.md (transport, ConnectionRegistry)

## Design slice (verbatim)
Gossip RX is a frame-handler trait impl registered with classic-proto's mux. On each NodeAd or AdGossip frame it updates the corresponding peer slot in AdStore (LWW on generation).

Gossip TX runs a 10 s ticker and a hook on connection-up events. It reads the own-node ad from AdStore and broadcasts via classic-proto to all currently connected peers.

Sequence — bring-up + gossip:
 0ms   start; generate NodeId; run discovery probes (≤200 ms)
 250ms publish self_ad to AdStore
 300ms TCP connect → B (classic-proto)
 310ms send Hello; 320ms recv Hello
 320ms B accepts connect; sends NodeAd[gen=N]
 350ms recv NodeAd → AdStore.upsert(B); send NodeAd[gen=1]
 360ms B recv NodeAd → AdStore.upsert(A)
 10s   tick → broadcast NodeAd to B
 60s   slow probe re-enum; generation += 1; publish; 70s gossip tick broadcasts new ad

Sequence — peer drop:
 t      detect connection-down to B; AdStore.mark_stale(B, ttl=90s)
 t+1s   gossip tick still has B in all_ads()
 t+90s  AdStore.evict(B) if no fresh ad seen; watch() emits Removed(B)

FR-7: Daemon broadcasts its full ad on every connection-up event and every 10 s.
FR-9: On a peer connection-down event, the corresponding ad is marked stale and removed after peer_grace (default 90 s) unless a fresh ad arrives.

Receivers MUST accept both 0x0100 (NodeAd) and 0x0101 (AdGossip::Full(...)) frame kinds carrying the same payload. AdRequest (0x0102) handler: respond by unicast-sending current self_ad to the requester.

Open question §3 (resolved): gossip TX iterates the live connection set only; it does NOT send to peers we just disconnected from. peer_grace is purely a receiver-side TTL.

## Inputs
- crates/classic-ad/src/store.rs (AdStore::{self_ad, upsert, mark_stale})
- crates/classic-ad/src/frames.rs (encode/decode for kinds 0x0100-0x0102)
- classic_proto::ConnectionRegistry (from plan 01) — exposes connection-up/down events + send-to-peer + handler registration
- crates/classic-ad/src/lib.rs (start() signature already established)

## Outputs
- crates/classic-ad/src/gossip.rs (new — RX handler impl + TX broadcaster task)
- crates/classic-ad/src/lib.rs (extend — start() wires Discovery + AdStore + Gossip RX/TX into the ConnectionRegistry)

## Test plan
- Integration: tests/gossip_pair.rs — spin up two AdStores wired to an in-memory bidi Connection from classic-proto (no real TCP). Push an own-ad on each side; assert each side sees the other's ad within 1 gossip tick.
- Integration: tests/gossip_lww.rs — same harness; send three ads with generations 1, 3, 2 in order; assert final stored generation is 3.
- Integration: tests/gossip_eviction.rs — drop one side of the connection; assert peer() returns Some for peer_grace - 1 and None after; watch() emits Removed.
- Unit: connection-up event triggers an immediate broadcast of self_ad (FR-7).
- Unit: 10 s ticker triggers a broadcast (FR-7) — use a tokio::time::pause/advance harness.
- Unit: AdRequest handler responds by unicasting self_ad to the requester.

## Acceptance
- [ ] Daemon broadcasts full self_ad on every connection-up event and every gossip_period (FR-7).
- [ ] Receivers accept both 0x0100 and 0x0101 frame kinds carrying NodeAd payloads.
- [ ] AdRequest (0x0102) handler responds with current self_ad unicast.
- [ ] On connection-down, AdStore.mark_stale(peer, peer_grace) is invoked (FR-9).
- [ ] gossip_pair, gossip_lww, gossip_eviction integration tests green.
- [ ] start() in lib.rs wires Discovery, AdStore, Gossip RX, Gossip TX into a ConnectionRegistry; clean shutdown on AdStore drop.
acceptance_criteria:
Daemon broadcasts full self_ad on every connection-up event and every gossip_period.
Receivers accept both 0x0100 and 0x0101 frame kinds carrying NodeAd payloads.
AdRequest (0x0102) handler responds with current self_ad unicast.
On connection-down, AdStore.mark_stale(peer, peer_grace) is invoked.
gossip_pair, gossip_lww, gossip_eviction integration tests green.
start() wires Discovery + AdStore + Gossip RX/TX into a ConnectionRegistry with clean shutdown on AdStore drop.

title: classic-cap: DeviceCap + CapBroker scaffold (in-memory accounting)
description:
# Task 1 — `classic-cap` crate scaffold: `DeviceCap` + `CapBroker` (in-memory, no enforcement)

Parent epic: classic-1j3 (Feature: Spawn pipeline). Plan: plans/04-spawn-pipeline.md @ commit a7b6585. Read with: git show a7b6585:plans/04-spawn-pipeline.md.

Foundations: plans/ARCHITECTURE.md (identity types: NodeId, MboxId, NetId from classic-proto; trust model; Linux 6.1+).

## Reading first

Before writing code: read plans/04-spawn-pipeline.md in full (esp. "DeviceCap and CapBroker" subsection of Design), then plans/ARCHITECTURE.md. Skim crates/classic-proto for the existing identity types you'll consume (MboxId).

## Scope

Stand up the new `crates/classic-cap` crate with the public API for device capability tokens and the in-memory broker. Pure accounting: NO cgroup writes, NO BPF — those land in tasks 2 and 3. The broker is the single source of truth for "who currently holds what device"; later tasks attach the BPF allowlist sync to its acquire/release events.

In scope:
- `crates/classic-cap/` Cargo.toml + src/lib.rs + src/broker.rs scaffolding
- Public types: `DeviceKind` (GpuMinor(u32) | PciSlot(BdfAddr)), `BdfAddr { domain: u16, bus: u8, device: u8, function: u8 }`, `DeviceCap`, `CapBroker`, `AcquireError`.
- `CapBroker::new()`, `acquire(holder, &[DeviceKind], exclusive) -> Result<Vec<DeviceCap>, AcquireError>` (atomic all-or-nothing), `release_all(holder)`, `snapshot() -> Vec<CapSnapshot>` (diagnostics).
- `DeviceCap` Drop calls back into broker (RAII single-cap release). NO public constructor — `acquire` is the only way to obtain one.
- `nvidia.rs` enumeration helper (list `/dev/nvidia*` minors) — pure read.

Out of scope (other tasks):
- cgroup-v2 sysfs setup (Task 2).
- BPF program / device-controller enforcement (Task 3).
- Spawn-side use (Task 4).

## Design slice

```rust
pub enum DeviceKind {
    /// /dev/nvidia<N>. BPF program (Task 3) allows (c, 195, N) + ctl
    /// (195, NVIDIA_CTL_MINOR=255) and (195, 254) iff any GpuMinor held.
    GpuMinor(u32),
    /// Non-NVIDIA passthrough. Resolved to char (major,minor) via
    /// /sys/bus/pci/devices/<bdf>/uevent.
    PciSlot(BdfAddr),
}
pub struct BdfAddr { pub domain: u16, pub bus: u8, pub device: u8, pub function: u8 }

pub struct DeviceCap {
    pub kind: DeviceKind,
    pub exclusive: bool,
    pub holder: MboxId,
    _release: BrokerHandle,           // Drop calls broker.release_internal
}

pub struct CapBroker { /* Mutex<CapBrokerInner> */ }

impl CapBroker {
    pub fn new() -> Self;
    /// Atomically acquire all `kinds` for `holder` — all-or-nothing.
    /// `exclusive=true` refuses if any kind is held (shared or exclusive).
    /// `exclusive=false` co-holds with other shared holders only.
    pub fn acquire(&self, holder: MboxId, kinds: &[DeviceKind], exclusive: bool)
        -> Result<Vec<DeviceCap>, AcquireError>;
    /// Force-release every cap held by `holder`. Called on child exit or
    /// originator disconnect.
    pub fn release_all(&self, holder: MboxId);
    pub fn snapshot(&self) -> Vec<CapSnapshot>;
}

pub enum AcquireError {
    Taken { kind: DeviceKind },
    SharedConflict { kind: DeviceKind, n: usize },
}
```

## Inputs

- `crates/classic-proto` — import `MboxId`.
- `plans/04-spawn-pipeline.md` — design source (subsection "DeviceCap and CapBroker").
- `plans/ARCHITECTURE.md` — for crate dependency direction (`classic-cap` depends on stdlib + cgroup + nvml only).

## Outputs

Files to create:
- `crates/classic-cap/Cargo.toml` (workspace member; deps: `classic-proto`, std-only for now).
- `crates/classic-cap/src/lib.rs` — re-exports + module decls; doc comment summarizing the contract; `pub use broker::*; pub use nvidia::list_nvidia_minors;`.
- `crates/classic-cap/src/broker.rs` — types + Mutex-protected inner state + acquire/release/snapshot logic + BrokerHandle (Arc-of-broker handle that release_internal).
- `crates/classic-cap/src/nvidia.rs` — `pub fn list_nvidia_minors() -> Vec<u32>` (parses `/dev/nvidia<N>`; returns empty Vec if directory missing — no panic).

Update root `Cargo.toml` workspace member list if not already present.

## Test plan

`cargo test -p classic-cap` (Linux x86_64, no root, no GPU, no BPF):
- `acquire(holder=A, [GpuMinor(0)], exclusive=true)` -> Ok; second `acquire(holder=B, [GpuMinor(0)], exclusive=true)` -> `Taken { GpuMinor(0) }`.
- `acquire(A, [GpuMinor(0)], shared=false)` then `acquire(B, [GpuMinor(0)], shared=false)` both Ok.
- After A's caps drop (Vec dropped), `acquire(C, [GpuMinor(0)], exclusive=true)` Ok.
- `release_all(A)` releases both an exclusive and a shared GpuMinor cap held by A in one call.
- All-or-nothing: `acquire(A, [GpuMinor(0), GpuMinor(1)], exclusive=true)` when GpuMinor(1) is taken -> Err and GpuMinor(0) NOT held by A afterward.
- Cross-thread stress: 100 spawn-blocking threads contending on `[GpuMinor(0..7)], exclusive=true` -> exactly 8 holders observed at any time via snapshot, no deadlock.
- `nvidia::list_nvidia_minors()` returns `Vec` (may be empty); no panic when `/dev` lacks nvidia nodes.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] AC-T1.1: `cargo build -p classic-cap` and `cargo test -p classic-cap` succeed on Linux x86_64 without root, NVIDIA driver, or BPF support.
- [ ] AC-T1.2: `DeviceCap` has no public constructor — caps cannot be forged outside the crate (negative compile test or doc test illustrating this).
- [ ] AC-T1.3: `acquire` is atomic: a multi-kind call that fails partway leaves no caps held by the requested `holder`.
- [ ] AC-T1.4: `Drop` of a single `DeviceCap` releases just that one; subsequent `snapshot()` does not list it.
- [ ] AC-T1.5: `release_all(holder)` removes every cap belonging to `holder`, even ones still owned by their `DeviceCap` value (the value's Drop becomes a no-op).
- [ ] AC-T1.6: Exclusive vs shared semantics match the table in the design slice (covered by unit tests above).
- [ ] AC-T1.7: All public types derive what's natural (`Debug`, `Clone` where Copy-able) and the API is doc-commented with one-line summaries linking to plans/04-spawn-pipeline.md.

## Intra-epic dependencies

- Parent: classic-1j3.
- Blocks: Task 2 (cgroup hierarchy — needs broker hooks for snapshot), Task 3 (BPF loader — subscribes to acquire/release), Task 4 (spawn core — uses CapBroker).
- Prereqs: none within this epic (depends only on plan 01's classic-proto crate).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] AC-T1.1: `cargo build -p classic-cap` and `cargo test -p classic-cap` succeed on Linux x86_64 without root, NVIDIA driver, or BPF support.
- [ ] AC-T1.2: `DeviceCap` has no public constructor — caps cannot be forged outside the crate.
- [ ] AC-T1.3: `acquire` is atomic: a multi-kind call that fails partway leaves no caps held by the requested holder.
- [ ] AC-T1.4: `Drop` of a single `DeviceCap` releases just that one cap; subsequent `snapshot()` does not list it.
- [ ] AC-T1.5: `release_all(holder)` removes every cap belonging to `holder`, even ones still owned by a `DeviceCap` value.
- [ ] AC-T1.6: Exclusive vs shared semantics match the design slice (covered by unit tests).
- [ ] AC-T1.7: Public types derive `Debug`/`Clone` where appropriate and are doc-commented linking to plans/04-spawn-pipeline.md.

title: classic-place: type checker + Requirement/Rank distinction
description:
Epic: classic-i0n (Feature: Placement predicates)
Plan: plans/03-placement-predicates.md @ commit a7b6585. Read with: git show a7b6585:plans/03-placement-predicates.md

## Reading first

1. Plan §Design "Data shapes" (the type-rules table for field paths).
2. Plan §Design "AST" (Requirement / Rank wrapper structs).
3. Plan §Error-message examples — type-error rendering (`type error: cannot compare string and int`, `requirement must be a boolean expression`, `unknown field \`warp_count\` on GpuAd`).

## Design slice (excerpted from plan §Design)

Type rules for field paths (table reproduced):
| Field path                   | DSL type        |
|------------------------------|-----------------|
| cpu.cores, cpu.threads       | int             |
| cpu.arch, cpu.model          | string          |
| mem.total_mb, mem.free_mb    | int             |
| load.cpu_pct, etc.           | float           |
| gen                          | int             |
| gpu                          | collection ([GpuAd]) |
| gpu.vram_mb                  | int (per element, only inside any/all/count) |
| gpu.in_use                   | bool (per element) |
| gpu.vendor, gpu.device       | int             |
| pci.vendor, pci.device       | int (per element) |

Integers fit in i64. Floats are f64. Numeric comparisons coerce int -> float when one side is float. No implicit string<->number coercion.

Requirement vs Rank wrappers (verbatim from plan):
```rust
pub struct Requirement(pub Expr);  // type-checked: returns bool
pub struct Rank(pub Expr);         // type-checked: returns numeric (int|float)
```

Type errors at parse time (the type-checker runs after parse, errors flow through ParseError with kind=Type):
- Top-level Requirement non-bool rejected.
- Top-level Rank non-numeric rejected.
- Aggregate body must be bool: `any(gpu, gpu.vram_mb)` (non-bool body) rejected.
- `in [...]` list members must all share LHS type.
- Comparing string and int rejected (`cpu.arch >= 5`).
- Unknown field on a known root rejected with the "known: ..." hint listing the actual fields.
- `count` returns int. `any`/`all` return bool.

Aggregates introduce iteration variable binding that shadows outer fields of the same name; type-checker tracks the inner-binding types: inside `any(gpu, ...)` / `all(gpu, ...)` / `count(gpu, ...)`, `gpu` resolves to a single GpuAd element so `gpu.vram_mb` is int (not collection).

Collection root names recognized: `gpu`, `pci`. `labels` access syntax deferred (open question 1) — bracketed indexing not in v1 grammar.

## Inputs

- `crates/classic-place/src/ast.rs` (Expr enum from parser task).
- `crates/classic-place/src/parse.rs` (`parse_expr` returning Expr).
- `crates/classic-place/src/error.rs` (ParseError + ParseErrorKind::Type).

## Outputs

- New file `crates/classic-place/src/typeck.rs`:
  - Internal type lattice enum: `Ty { Bool, Int, Float, Str, GpuColl, PciColl, GpuElem, PciElem, List(Box<Ty>) }` (or equivalent).
  - `fn check_req(e: &Expr) -> Result<Ty, ParseError>` — verifies top is Bool.
  - `fn check_rank(e: &Expr) -> Result<Ty, ParseError>` — verifies top is Int or Float.
  - Field resolver that knows NodeAd shape (cpu, mem, load, gpu, pci, gen, hostname, os) and per-element shapes (GpuAd: index, vendor, device, model, vram_mb, vram_free_mb, sm_count, in_use, mig; PciAd: bdf, vendor, device, class).
- Update lib.rs:
  - Add `pub fn parse_req(src: &str) -> Result<Requirement, ParseError>` — lex -> parse -> typeck (top=Bool) -> Requirement.
  - Add `pub fn parse_rank(src: &str) -> Result<Rank, ParseError>` — lex -> parse -> typeck (top=Int|Float) -> Rank.
  - Define `pub struct Requirement(Expr)` and `pub struct Rank(Expr)` (private fields per plan API).

## Test plan

In-module tests under typeck.rs and integration tests under `crates/classic-place/tests/grammar.rs` (NEW or extended):

- Numeric coercion: `load.cpu_pct < 50` accepted (int coerces to float); `cpu.cores < 50.0` accepted; `cpu.arch >= 5` rejected (Type error).
- Top-level Requirement non-bool rejected: `parse_req("cpu.cores")` -> ParseError matching `requirement must be a boolean expression, got int`.
- Top-level Rank non-numeric rejected: `parse_rank("cpu.arch")` -> ParseError.
- Aggregate body type: `any(gpu, gpu.vram_mb)` -> Type error (body not bool).
- Aggregate binding shadow: inside `any(gpu, gpu.vram_mb >= 80000)`, `gpu` is GpuElem so gpu.vram_mb is int (not collection).
- Unknown field: `any(gpu, gpu.warp_count >= 4)` -> Type error with "known: ..." hint listing GpuAd field names.
- `in` list type mismatch: `cpu.cores in ["x86_64"]` rejected.
- All six example predicates from plan §Examples type-check successfully.
- Default rank text type-checks as Float.

Run: `cargo test -p classic-place typeck` and `cargo test -p classic-place --test grammar`.

## Acceptance criteria

- AC-T1: `parse_req` returns Requirement only when top-level type is Bool; otherwise ParseError kind=Type.
- AC-T2: `parse_rank` returns Rank only when top-level type is Int or Float; otherwise ParseError kind=Type.
- AC-T3: Field resolver knows the full NodeAd / GpuAd / PciAd field set (per plan §Data shapes table).
- AC-T4: Aggregate iteration variable shadows outer fields; per-element types resolved correctly inside the body.
- AC-T5: All six example predicates and the default rank type-check successfully.
- AC-T6: Type errors carry a clear message including "known: ..." hint for unknown fields.
- AC-T7: int->float coercion applies in numeric comparisons; string-vs-number rejected.
- AC-T8: No unsafe; std-only.

## Intra-epic deps

- Depends on: parser task (classic-sdi).
- Blocks: evaluator, place(), tests.
acceptance_criteria:
AC-T1: parse_req requires top-level Bool; otherwise ParseError kind=Type.
AC-T2: parse_rank requires top-level Int or Float; otherwise ParseError kind=Type.
AC-T3: Field resolver covers full NodeAd/GpuAd/PciAd schemas per plan §Data shapes table.
AC-T4: Aggregate iteration variable shadows outer fields; per-element types correct inside body.
AC-T5: All six example predicates and the default rank type-check.
AC-T6: Unknown-field errors include "known: ..." hint listing actual fields.
AC-T7: int->float coercion in numeric comparisons; string-vs-number rejected.
AC-T8: No unsafe; std-only.

title: classic-ad: Frame kinds 0x0100-0x0102 + classic-proto FrameKind extension
description:
Part of epic classic-ygs. Plan: plans/02-node-ad-hw-discovery.md @ a7b6585.

## Reading first
- git show a7b6585:plans/02-node-ad-hw-discovery.md (sections: Frame-kind allocation, Data shapes, FR-11/FR-12)
- plans/ARCHITECTURE.md (Frame format, Frame-kind allocation table, Encoding)

## Design slice (verbatim)
Frame-kind allocation (within the 0x0100–0x01FF range):

| Code     | Name        | Payload type    | Direction   |
|----------|-------------|-----------------|-------------|
| 0x0100   | NodeAd      | NodeAd          | broadcast   |
| 0x0101   | AdGossip    | AdGossip        | broadcast   |
| 0x0102   | AdRequest   | AdRequest       | unicast     |
| 0x0103 – 0x01FF | reserved | — | — |

NodeAd and AdGossip::Full(NodeAd) carry the same payload; the two frame kinds exist so future deltas can ride AdGossip without breaking the simpler NodeAd decoder. v1 emitters MAY use either — receivers MUST accept both.

FR-11: Frame kinds 0x0100–0x0102 are registered with classic-proto's FrameKind enum. Decoders reject unknown kinds in this range with Error::UnknownFrameKind.
FR-12: All wire payloads encode and decode via bincode v2 (fixed-int, little-endian) without allocations beyond the payload itself.

ARCHITECTURE.md frame format reminder:
+----------+----------+----------------------+
|  length  |   kind   |       payload        |
|  u32 LE  |  u16 LE  |   length - 2 bytes   |
+----------+----------+----------------------+

## Inputs
- crates/classic-proto/src/lib.rs (existing FrameKind enum from plan 01)
- crates/classic-ad/src/schema.rs (NodeAd, AdGossip, AdRequest)

## Outputs
- crates/classic-proto/src/lib.rs (modified — extend FrameKind enum with NodeAd=0x0100, AdGossip=0x0101, AdRequest=0x0102; constants only, no payload types live in classic-proto)
- crates/classic-ad/src/frames.rs (new — frame-kind constants, encode/decode glue mapping payload structs to/from the FrameKind discriminant; rejects 0x0103-0x01FF as UnknownFrameKind)

## Test plan
- Unit: encode NodeAd, AdGossip::Full(NodeAd), AdGossip::Delta{...}, AdRequest — assert outer frame layout matches the [length:u32, kind:u16, payload] format and bincode-decodes back.
- Unit: decoder presented with an unknown kind in 0x0103-0x01FF returns Error::UnknownFrameKind (FR-11).
- Unit: receivers accept both 0x0100 (NodeAd) and 0x0101 (AdGossip::Full) carrying the same payload.
- AC-8: classic-proto::FrameKind includes NodeAd=0x0100, AdGossip=0x0101, AdRequest=0x0102.

## Acceptance
- [ ] classic-proto::FrameKind enum extended with three variants matching the codes above; cargo build of classic-proto green.
- [ ] crates/classic-ad/src/frames.rs encodes and decodes all three frame kinds.
- [ ] Decoder rejects kinds in 0x0103-0x01FF with Error::UnknownFrameKind.
- [ ] No payload allocations beyond the payload itself (FR-12).
- [ ] cargo deny check passes (AC-8).
acceptance_criteria:
classic-proto::FrameKind extended with NodeAd=0x0100, AdGossip=0x0101, AdRequest=0x0102.
crates/classic-ad/src/frames.rs encodes and decodes all three frame kinds.
Decoder rejects kinds in 0x0103-0x01FF with Error::UnknownFrameKind.
No payload allocations beyond the payload itself.
cargo deny check passes.

title: classic-proto: FrameMux + FrameHandler trait (range-keyed dispatch)
description:
Part of epic classic-ivc. Plan: plans/01-skeleton-transport.md @ a7b6585.

## Reading first
- git show a7b6585:plans/01-skeleton-transport.md
- git show a7b6585:plans/ARCHITECTURE.md

## Excerpted design slice

### From plan § Design / Frame multiplexer
> The mux holds a [Option<Arc<dyn FrameHandler>>; 8] indexed by the high byte of FrameKind (so frame 0x0123 indexes slot 1, 0x0234 indexes slot 2, etc.). 8 slots covers 0x0000–0x07FF, the entire allocated range plus one reserved slot.
>
> ```rust
> pub trait FrameHandler: Send + Sync + 'static {
>     /// Called once per inbound frame whose kind falls in this handler's range.
>     /// Implementors must not block; offload to their own task if needed.
>     fn on_frame(&self, peer: NodeId, frame: Frame);
> }
>
> pub struct FrameMux {
>     handlers: [ArcSwapOption<dyn FrameHandler>; 8],
> }
>
> impl FrameMux {
>     pub fn new() -> Self { /* ... */ }
>     pub fn register(&self, range_high_byte: u8, h: Arc<dyn FrameHandler>);
>     pub fn dispatch(&self, peer: NodeId, frame: Frame);
> }
> ```
>
> A frame whose high byte has no registered handler triggers a single warn-level log and is dropped. The connection stays open. We do not send Error for unknown frame kinds — that path is reserved for handshake-time protocol violations only, since later versions may add new kinds that older peers should ignore.

### From plan § Requirements
> FR-9: An inbound frame whose kind has no registered handler is logged and dropped without closing the connection.
> FR-13: The FrameMux accepts handler registration for any 0x0N.. range from 0x00 through 0x07.

## Inputs
- classic-proto::NodeId, classic-proto::Frame from sibling task classic-53m
- arc-swap crate (for ArcSwapOption; lock-free hot-path register/replace)
- tracing crate (for warn-on-unknown-kind log)

## Outputs
Files under /home/horde/src/classic/crates/classic-proto/:
- src/mux.rs — FrameHandler trait + FrameMux struct + impl
- src/lib.rs — pub mod mux; pub use mux::{FrameMux, FrameHandler};
- crates/classic-proto/Cargo.toml — add arc-swap, tracing

Public API:
```rust
pub trait FrameHandler: Send + Sync + 'static {
    fn on_frame(&self, peer: NodeId, frame: Frame);
}

pub struct FrameMux { /* private */ }

impl FrameMux {
    pub fn new() -> Self;
    pub fn register(&self, range_high_byte: u8, h: Arc<dyn FrameHandler>); // range 0..8 valid; panics or returns Err out of range
    pub fn dispatch(&self, peer: NodeId, frame: Frame);
}
```

Notes:
- High byte of frame.kind = (frame.kind >> 8) as u8.
- range_high_byte must be in 0..=7; document and assert/return-error on out-of-range.
- Dispatch must be lock-free on the hot path (ArcSwapOption load).
- A second register() to the same range replaces the previous handler (documented behaviour).

## Test plan
- cargo test -p classic-proto mux::tests::register_and_dispatch — register a handler at high byte 0x01, dispatch a frame with kind=0x0123, the handler observes it.
- cargo test -p classic-proto mux::tests::unknown_range_dropped — dispatch a frame with kind=0x0723 to an empty mux; no panic, no handler called, log emitted.
- cargo test -p classic-proto mux::tests::multiple_ranges — register handlers at 0x01, 0x02, 0x05; verify dispatch routes to each correctly.
- cargo test -p classic-proto mux::tests::replace_handler — register at 0x01 twice; only the second handler receives subsequent frames.
- Concurrency smoke test: spawn N tokio tasks dispatching to the mux while another task is replacing the handler; no panic, no UB.

## Task-specific Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] FrameHandler trait defined as in plan (Send + Sync + 'static, on_frame(&self, NodeId, Frame)).
- [ ] FrameMux::new(), register(), dispatch() compile and behave as documented.
- [ ] Register supports high bytes 0x00..=0x07 (8 slots).
- [ ] Unknown range produces a warn log and drops the frame (no panic, no error returned).
- [ ] Concurrent dispatch + register safe (ArcSwapOption-based).
- [ ] cargo test -p classic-proto mux::tests::* green.

## Intra-epic deps
- Blocks-on: classic-53m (needs Frame + NodeId)
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] FrameHandler trait per plan; FrameMux::{new,register,dispatch} compile and route by high byte.
- [ ] register supports high bytes 0x00..=0x07.
- [ ] Unknown range = warn log + drop, no panic, no error.
- [ ] Concurrent dispatch + register is safe.
- [ ] mux::tests::* green.

title: classic-ad: AdStore + watch() stream + LWW logic
description:
Part of epic classic-ygs. Plan: plans/02-node-ad-hw-discovery.md @ a7b6585.

## Reading first
- git show a7b6585:plans/02-node-ad-hw-discovery.md (sections: Architecture — AdStore, Public API, Sequence — peer drop, FR-8/FR-9/FR-10)
- plans/ARCHITECTURE.md

## Design slice (verbatim)
AdStore is the central Arc<RwLock>-backed shared map. The own-node slot is written only by Discovery; peer slots are written only by Gossip RX.

Public API of classic-ad (everything else pub(crate)):

#[derive(Clone)]
pub struct AdStore { /* Arc<Inner> */ }

impl AdStore {
    pub fn self_ad(&self) -> NodeAd;
    pub fn all_ads(&self) -> Vec<NodeAd>;
    pub fn peer(&self, id: NodeId) -> Option<NodeAd>;
    pub fn watch(&self) -> impl futures::Stream<Item = AdUpdate> + Send + 'static;
}

FR-8: Receivers store peer ads keyed by NodeId. Conflict resolution is LWW on (generation, boot_time): prefer higher generation; tie-break by higher boot_time.
FR-9: On a peer connection-down event, the corresponding ad is marked stale and removed after peer_grace (default 90 s) unless a fresh ad arrives.
FR-10: AdStore::watch() emits Inserted / Updated / Removed events; Updated MUST NOT fire when a re-broadcast carries an unchanged generation.

watch() Stream is buffered, lossy on lag (overflow drops oldest). Implementation hint: tokio::sync::broadcast wrapped to drop oldest on lag.

mark_stale(peer, ttl) starts a timer; if a fresh ad with newer (generation, boot_time) arrives before ttl, the timer is cancelled. evict(peer) removes from map and emits AdUpdate::Removed.

all_ads() ordering: own ad first, then peer ads in stable order (e.g. sorted by NodeId bytes). all_ads() returns in <100 µs for 256 nodes (NFR perf).

## Inputs
- crates/classic-ad/src/schema.rs (NodeAd, AdUpdate)
- classic-proto::NodeId
- tokio::sync::broadcast (or analogous) for watch()

## Outputs
- crates/classic-ad/src/store.rs (new — AdStore, Inner, mark_stale, evict, upsert, watch)
- crates/classic-ad/src/lib.rs (extend — re-export AdStore, AdUpdate)

## Test plan
- Unit: insert ad with generation 5; insert with generation 3 — discarded. Insert with generation 5, boot_time T+1 — accepted (restart) (FR-8).
- Unit: AdStore::watch() — subscribe; insert; assert Inserted. Re-insert same (node_id, generation) — assert no event. Insert higher generation — assert Updated (FR-10).
- Unit: mark_stale starts a timer; evict fires Removed via watch() after ttl elapses; cancellation prevents eviction when a fresh ad arrives first.
- Integration: tests/gossip_lww.rs — feed three ads with generations 1, 3, 2; final stored generation is 3.
- Integration: tests/gossip_eviction.rs — drop one side of an in-memory connection; peer() returns Some for peer_grace - 1 and None after; watch() emits Removed.
- Perf check: all_ads() with 256 entries returns in <100 µs (NFR).

## Acceptance
- [ ] AdStore::{self_ad, all_ads, peer, watch} match the public API verbatim.
- [ ] LWW on (generation, boot_time) per FR-8.
- [ ] watch() never fires Updated for unchanged generation (FR-10).
- [ ] mark_stale + evict honor peer_grace (FR-9).
- [ ] all_ads() returns own ad first, then peers in stable order.
- [ ] Lossy buffered watch() (broadcast or equivalent) does not block writers.
acceptance_criteria:
AdStore::{self_ad, all_ads, peer, watch} match the public API verbatim.
LWW on (generation, boot_time) per FR-8.
watch() never fires Updated for unchanged generation per FR-10.
mark_stale + evict honor peer_grace per FR-9.
all_ads() returns own ad first then peers in stable order.
Lossy buffered watch() does not block writers.

title: classic-place: recursive-descent parser + AST + parse errors
description:
Epic: classic-i0n (Feature: Placement predicates)
Plan: plans/03-placement-predicates.md @ commit a7b6585. Read with: git show a7b6585:plans/03-placement-predicates.md

## Reading first

1. Read the lexer task output (this task consumes its Token stream).
2. Plan §Design "Grammar", "AST", "Parser strategy" subsections.
3. Plan §Error-message examples (the parser owns most of these).

## Design slice (excerpted from plan §Design)

Grammar (PEG-style):
```
Expr        := OrExpr
OrExpr      := AndExpr ( "||" AndExpr )*
AndExpr     := NotExpr ( "&&" NotExpr )*
NotExpr     := "!" NotExpr | CmpExpr
CmpExpr     := AddExpr ( CmpOp AddExpr )?
             | AddExpr "in" ListLit
CmpOp       := "==" | "!=" | "<=" | ">=" | "<" | ">"
AddExpr     := MulExpr ( ("+" | "-") MulExpr )*
MulExpr     := UnaryExpr ( ("*" | "/") UnaryExpr )*
UnaryExpr   := "-" UnaryExpr | Primary
Primary     := Aggregate | "(" Expr ")" | ListLit | Literal | FieldPath
Aggregate   := AggOp "(" Ident ( "," Expr )? ")"
AggOp       := "any" | "all" | "count"
ListLit     := "[" ( Expr ( "," Expr )* )? "]"
FieldPath   := Ident ( "." Ident )*
```

Comparisons are non-associative: `a < b < c` is a parse error (per plan).
`in` is disambiguated by lookahead-1 after AddExpr.

AST (verbatim from plan):
```rust
pub enum Expr {
    Bool(bool), Int(i64), Float(f64), Str(String),
    List(Vec<Expr>),                   // only legal as RHS of `in`
    Field(Vec<String>),                // ["gpu", "vram_mb"]
    BinOp(BinOp, Box<Expr>, Box<Expr>),
    UnaryOp(UnaryOp, Box<Expr>),
    In(Box<Expr>, Vec<Expr>),          // already lowered list-membership
    Agg(AggOp, String, Option<Box<Expr>>),
}
pub enum BinOp { Or, And, Eq, Ne, Lt, Le, Gt, Ge, Add, Sub, Mul, Div }
pub enum UnaryOp { Not, Neg }
pub enum AggOp { Any, All, Count }
```

Parser strategy: hand-written recursive descent. Hand-tuned error messages — emit `expected \`,\` or \`)\` after aggregate body` not `expected one of: ...`. No backtracking; LL(1) modulo `in`.

Reserved keywords (any, all, count, in, true, false) cannot appear as field names — parser must reject `any.in == 3` per plan error example.

`x in [a,b,c]` lowers to `In(x, [a,b,c])` AST node directly (per plan: "already lowered list-membership").

## Inputs

- `crates/classic-place/src/lex.rs` (token stream + Pos).
- `crates/classic-place/src/error.rs` (ParseError type).

## Outputs

- New file `crates/classic-place/src/ast.rs` — Expr, BinOp, UnaryOp, AggOp enums (verbatim from plan); derive Debug, Clone.
- New file `crates/classic-place/src/parse.rs`:
  - `pub fn parse_expr(toks: &[Token]) -> Result<Expr, ParseError>` — internal, returns the AST without type-checking.
  - Recursive-descent functions: parse_or, parse_and, parse_not, parse_cmp, parse_add, parse_mul, parse_unary, parse_primary, parse_agg, parse_list, parse_field_path.
- Update `crates/classic-place/src/lib.rs` to declare `mod ast; mod parse;` and re-export AST types.
- Flesh out `crates/classic-place/src/error.rs` — Display impl that produces messages matching the format in plan §Error-message examples (the byte-identical AC-4 snapshots are in a later golden-test task; this task produces messages that satisfy the format `line N col M: <reason>` with optional caret-underline rendering).

## Test plan

In-module tests under parse.rs (`#[cfg(test)] mod tests`), table-driven where structure permits:

- Precedence:
  - `a || b && c` parses as `Or(a, And(b, c))`.
  - `!a == b` parses as `Eq(Not(a), b)`.
  - `-a + b` parses as `Add(Neg(a), b)`.
- Chained comparison rejected: `cpu.cores < 32 < 64` -> ParseError, kind=Syntax.
- Aggregates:
  - `count(gpu)` -> `Agg(Count, "gpu", None)`.
  - `count(gpu, gpu.in_use)` -> `Agg(Count, "gpu", Some(Field(...)))`.
  - `any(gpu, gpu.vram_mb >= 80000)`.
- `in` lowers: `x in [1,2,3]` -> `In(Field("x"), [Int(1), Int(2), Int(3)])`.
- Hex/decimal/float distinct: `0x10de` -> Int(0x10de); `50.0` -> Float(50.0); `50` -> Int(50).
- Reserved-keyword as field rejected: `any.in == 3` -> ParseError matching plan's `\`in\` is a reserved keyword` text.
- Trailing tokens after a complete expression -> ParseError.
- Unbalanced parens / brackets -> ParseError.

Run: `cargo test -p classic-place parse`.

## Acceptance criteria

- AC-P1: Every grammar production parses; AST shape matches plan §AST.
- AC-P2: Operator precedence matches plan (low->high: ||, &&, !, comparisons, +/-, */ /, unary -, primaries).
- AC-P3: Chained comparisons produce ParseError kind=Syntax.
- AC-P4: `in` is lowered to the In(Box<Expr>, Vec<Expr>) AST node at parse time.
- AC-P5: Reserved keywords cannot be field names; parser rejects with ParseError quoting the keyword.
- AC-P6: ParseError carries accurate line/col from the offending Token's Pos.
- AC-P7: No unsafe; std-only.

## Intra-epic deps

- Depends on: lexer task (classic-27u).
- Blocks: type-check, evaluator, all downstream.
acceptance_criteria:
AC-P1: Every grammar production parses; AST shape matches plan §AST verbatim.
AC-P2: Operator precedence matches plan (||, &&, !, cmp, +/-, * /, unary -, primaries) — table-driven precedence tests pass.
AC-P3: Chained comparisons (`a < b < c`) produce ParseError { kind: Syntax }.
AC-P4: `x in [..]` lowers to In(Box<Expr>, Vec<Expr>) AST node at parse time.
AC-P5: Reserved keywords (any/all/count/in/true/false) rejected as field names with descriptive ParseError.
