title: classic-proto: PROTO_VERSION + Hello/Heartbeat/Bye/Error payloads + Connection trait
description:
Part of epic classic-ivc. Plan: plans/01-skeleton-transport.md @ a7b6585.

## Reading first
- git show a7b6585:plans/01-skeleton-transport.md
- git show a7b6585:plans/ARCHITECTURE.md

## Excerpted design slice

### From plan § Design / Hello handshake (exact sequence)
> The Hello payload is bincode-v2 encoded:
> ```rust
> #[derive(Serialize, Deserialize)]
> pub struct HelloPayload {
>     pub proto_version: u32,
>     pub node_id: NodeId,
>     pub listen_addr: String, // "host:port" the peer can dial back on
>     pub capabilities: u64,   // reserved for v2; must be 0 in v1
> }
> ```

### From plan § Design / Heartbeat
> ```rust
> #[derive(Serialize, Deserialize)]
> pub struct HeartbeatPayload {
>     pub seq: u64,
>     pub send_time_ns: u64,
> }
> ```

### From plan § Design / Public APIs
```rust
pub const PROTO_VERSION: u32 = 1;

#[async_trait::async_trait]
pub trait Connection: Send + Sync {
    async fn send(&self, frame: Frame) -> Result<(), CodecError>;
    async fn recv(&mut self) -> Result<Frame, CodecError>;
    fn peer(&self) -> NodeId;
}

#[derive(Serialize, Deserialize, Debug)]
pub struct ErrorPayload {
    pub code: ErrorCode,
    pub message: String,
}

#[derive(Serialize, Deserialize, Debug)]
pub enum ErrorCode {
    ProtoVersionMismatch,
    ProtocolViolation,
    Internal,
}
```

### From ARCHITECTURE.md § Versioning
> classic-proto exposes pub const PROTO_VERSION: u32 = 1;
> Hello frame carries this version. Mismatched peers log + refuse the connection.

### From ARCHITECTURE.md § Frame-kind allocation
> Encoding: serde + bincode v2 (fixed-int, little-endian) for control frames.

Note: a Bye frame carries no payload (empty) per plan; but we still need a (possibly empty) ByePayload type for symmetry, or treat Bye as zero-length. Plan does not specify; pick zero-length payload (encode/decode no-op) and document.

## Inputs
- classic-proto::NodeId from sibling task classic-53m
- classic-proto::Frame, FrameKind, CodecError from sibling task classic-53m
- bincode v2 with the same legacy() config used by the codec
- async-trait crate

## Outputs
Files to create under /home/horde/src/classic/crates/classic-proto/:
- src/version.rs — pub const PROTO_VERSION: u32 = 1;
- src/proto.rs — HelloPayload, HeartbeatPayload, ErrorPayload, ErrorCode + encode/decode helpers (e.g. fn encode_payload<T: Serialize>(t: &T) -> Vec<u8> using bincode legacy() config; fn decode_payload<T: DeserializeOwned>(b: &[u8]) -> Result<T, CodecError>)
- src/conn.rs — Connection trait (async_trait)
- src/lib.rs — pub mod version; pub mod proto; pub mod conn; pub use version::PROTO_VERSION; pub use conn::Connection; pub use proto::*;
- crates/classic-proto/Cargo.toml — add async-trait dep (if not added by sibling)

## Test plan
- cargo test -p classic-proto proto::tests::hello_roundtrip — bincode encode -> decode of HelloPayload survives intact.
- cargo test -p classic-proto proto::tests::heartbeat_roundtrip — same for HeartbeatPayload.
- cargo test -p classic-proto proto::tests::error_roundtrip — same for ErrorPayload across all ErrorCode variants.
- Compile-only check: a dummy struct implementing Connection compiles and is object-safe (Box<dyn Connection>).
- PROTO_VERSION literal == 1 (compile-time assert via const).

## Task-specific Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] PROTO_VERSION = 1 is exported.
- [ ] HelloPayload, HeartbeatPayload, ErrorPayload, ErrorCode bincode-roundtrip via the same config as the frame codec.
- [ ] Connection trait is object-safe (Box<dyn Connection> compiles) and uses async_trait for send/recv.
- [ ] ErrorCode includes at minimum: ProtoVersionMismatch, ProtocolViolation, Internal.
- [ ] cargo test -p classic-proto green.

## Intra-epic deps
- Blocks-on: classic-53m (Frame + codec must exist for these payloads to wrap)
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] PROTO_VERSION = 1 exported as const.
- [ ] HelloPayload, HeartbeatPayload, ErrorPayload, ErrorCode bincode-roundtrip.
- [ ] Connection trait is object-safe with async send/recv via async_trait.
- [ ] ErrorCode has variants ProtoVersionMismatch, ProtocolViolation, Internal.
- [ ] cargo test -p classic-proto green.

title: Feature: Multi-node demo + integration tests
description:
Three-node demo with five reproducible scenarios; the proof v1 actually works end-to-end. This is a thin shell over the binaries delivered by plans 01-07 — no new crates, no new wire frames, no new CLI flags. Five scenarios under examples/multinode/ exercise the whole stack: HW-affinity placement (CUDA hello on GPU node), no-match failure, service registration + cross-node mailbox lookup, 9P remote bind, and PACK placement-group co-location. A new `cargo xtask demo-smoke` runs the no-real-hardware scenarios in CI via Docker Compose with NVML stubbed; scenarios 4 and 5 require hardware and are gated behind `make all-real-hardware`. Closes the loop with a human sign-off checklist that demonstrates real GPU hardware.

Plan: plans/08-multinode-demo.md @ commit a7b6585. Read with: git show a7b6585:plans/08-multinode-demo.md

Foundations: plans/ARCHITECTURE.md (frame ranges, identity types, crate layout, port 7421 baseline). Read with: git show a7b6585:plans/ARCHITECTURE.md

Workflow rules: AGENTS.md 'Feature Development Workflow' section.

Scope: examples/multinode/ tree (README, Makefile, three node configs, two demo programs, five scenario scripts, Compose-based smoke harness with NVML stub) plus xtask glue. No new crates. No new FrameKind values. No new CLI flags. If a scenario surfaces a missing feature, file a follow-up bead and mark the scenario xfail — do not retrofit here.

Out of scope: any new feature work, performance benchmarking, soak/chaos tests, authentication, multi-tenant isolation, cross-architecture testing.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] AC-1: examples/multinode/ exists with the file layout from plan §Design.
- [ ] AC-2: Each config/*.toml validates (`classicd --check-config` exits 0).
- [ ] AC-3: `make setup` exits 0 on a healthy cluster, non-0 with a clear remediation hint when any precondition is missing (peer down, daemon down, NVML missing).
- [ ] AC-4: `make scenario-1` … `make scenario-5` each run their script with the right CLASSIC_PEER env var and print OK on success.
- [ ] AC-5: All five scenarios pass on real hardware on the documented topology (signed off via the human checklist).
- [ ] AC-6: `cargo xtask demo-smoke` passes scenarios 1, 2, 3 in CI in <= 90 s wall time.
- [ ] AC-7: examples/multinode/README.md walks a fresh developer from `git clone` to a green `make scenario-1` with no undocumented steps (validated by a colleague new to the project).
- [ ] AC-8: README's troubleshooting section names at least: peer unreachable, NVML missing, GPU passthrough not configured, libnvidia-ml ABI mismatch, port 7421 firewalled, hostname not resolvable from peers.
- [ ] AC-9: No new crates added (`cargo metadata` workspace-member diff is empty).
- [ ] AC-10: No new FrameKind values allocated.
- [ ] Definition of multi-node demo success — human sign-off checklist (recorded in the PR description, on real hardware): `make setup` returned 0 listing all three peers reachable; scenario-1 OK with stdout containing 'hello from gpu 0 on node-b'; scenario-2 OK with CLI exit non-zero and `classic ps --all` showing no record on any node; scenario-3 OK with coordinator receiving exactly one hello from a NetId whose node component matches node-c's NodeId; scenario-4 OK with pci_lspci_loop BDF set matching `lspci -n` on node-b; scenario-5 OK with `classic ps --json` showing both members placed_node = node-b and >= 500 ms time overlap; `cargo xtask demo-smoke` passes locally; `make clean` returns the tree to no leftover Compose volumes or built binaries.

title: classic-ad: Discovery scheduler (1s fast tick + 60s slow tick + generation)
description:
Part of epic classic-ygs. Plan: plans/02-node-ad-hw-discovery.md @ a7b6585.

## Reading first
- git show a7b6585:plans/02-node-ad-hw-discovery.md (sections: Architecture — Discovery, Sequence — bring-up + gossip, FR-5/FR-6, Public API)
- plans/ARCHITECTURE.md

## Design slice (verbatim)
Discovery owns probes; it writes only the *own-node* slot of AdStore. It runs two tickers (1 s and 60 s) on a single tokio task.

Periodic refresh:
- Fast tick (1 s): LoadSample, per-GPU vram_free_mb, per-GPU utilization_pct, MemAvailable, task_count.
- Slow tick (60 s) and once at startup: full hardware re-enumeration (PCI list, GPU list, NUMA, CPU info).

FR-1: On startup, AdStore::self_ad() returns a NodeAd populated with hardware data within 500 ms of start() returning.
FR-5: LoadSample and dynamic GPU fields refresh on a 1 s cadence (configurable via AdConfig).
FR-6: Static fields (PCI, NUMA, GPU list, CPU info) refresh on a 60 s cadence and at startup. A static field that changes increments generation.

AdConfig {
    pub fast_tick: Duration,    // Default 1 s. Smallest accepted: 250 ms.
    pub slow_tick: Duration,    // Default 60 s. Smallest accepted: 5 s.
    pub gossip_period: Duration, // Default 10 s.
    pub peer_grace: Duration,    // Default 90 s.
    pub task_count_fn: Option<Arc<dyn Fn() -> u32 + Send + Sync>>,
}

Open question §2: task_count value is supplied by plan 04 via AdConfig::task_count_fn. v1 plan 02 reports 0 until plan 04 wires the callback.

Generation semantics: increment on every refresh that changes ANY field. boot_time is daemon CLOCK_REALTIME at startup, captured once at start().

## Inputs
- crates/classic-ad/src/schema.rs
- crates/classic-ad/src/discovery/* (probes from prior tasks)
- AdStore from the store task — own-node slot writer

## Outputs
- crates/classic-ad/src/discovery/mod.rs (extend — Discovery struct + spawn_loop tokio task)
- crates/classic-ad/src/lib.rs (extend — AdConfig struct definition)

## Test plan
- Unit test: invoke a single startup pass with a tempdir Sysroot + GpuProbe stub, assert AdStore::self_ad() populated within 500 ms.
- Unit test: simulate two slow ticks where the PCI fixture changes between ticks; assert generation increments on the change tick and stays equal on the no-change tick (FR-6 semantics).
- Unit test: simulate a fast tick after a load fixture change; assert LoadSample updated and generation incremented.
- Unit test: AdConfig validation — fast_tick < 250 ms is rejected; slow_tick < 5 s is rejected.
- Unit test: task_count_fn callback is invoked each fast tick; absent callback returns 0.

## Acceptance
- [ ] start() returns within 500 ms of being called and AdStore::self_ad() reflects discovered hardware (FR-1).
- [ ] Fast tick (1 s default) refreshes LoadSample + dynamic GPU fields + MemAvailable + task_count (FR-5).
- [ ] Slow tick (60 s default) and startup re-enumerate static hardware (FR-6).
- [ ] generation increments only when any field changes between ticks (FR-10 prerequisite).
- [ ] AdConfig validates min fast_tick=250ms, min slow_tick=5s.
- [ ] task_count_fn callback is invoked and value placed in LoadSample.task_count.
acceptance_criteria:
start() returns within 500 ms and self_ad() reflects discovered hardware.
Fast tick (1s default) refreshes LoadSample + dynamic GPU fields + MemAvailable + task_count.
Slow tick (60s default) and startup re-enumerate static hardware.
generation increments only when any field changes between ticks.
AdConfig validates min fast_tick=250ms, min slow_tick=5s.
task_count_fn callback is invoked and value placed in LoadSample.task_count.

title: Feature: Spawn pipeline
description:
Plan 04 — the headline feature. End-to-end: `classic spawn ...` on a user's CLI to a process running on a remote node, with stdio streamed back and exit status propagated. First thing that makes Classic look like a single-system image. Brings up two new crates (classic-spawn, classic-cap), the `classic spawn` CLI subcommand, the 0x0300-0x03FF spawn frame range, cgroup-v2 hierarchy with cpu/memory/pids/devices(BPF) controllers, NVIDIA minor binding via BPF cgroup_device, RAII cap release on exit/disconnect, and ranked-candidate fallback on placement refusal. Out of scope: mailbox abstraction (plan 05), 9P namespace (plan 06), placement groups (plan 07), CRIU/migration, binary distribution, node-to-node auth, multi-tenant quotas, per-task UIDs.

Plan: plans/04-spawn-pipeline.md @ commit a7b6585. Read with: git show a7b6585:plans/04-spawn-pipeline.md

Foundations: plans/ARCHITECTURE.md (identity types NodeId/MboxId/NetId, frame format, frame ranges, encoding bincode v2, cgroup v2 mandate, kernel >= 6.1, trust model).

Owner crates: crates/classic-spawn (NEW orchestrator state machine on both sides), crates/classic-cap (NEW DeviceCap tokens + CapBroker + cgroup-v2 device-controller enforcement). Wires up classic-cli (`spawn` subcommand) and classic-node (registers spawn handlers on the control socket and peer mux).

Owns frame-kind range 0x0300-0x03FF: SpawnRequest=0x0300, SpawnAck=0x0301, SpawnDeny=0x0302, ChildStdio=0x0303, ChildExit=0x0304. 0x0305-0x03FF reserved (PTY resize, signal injection, ...).

Depends on plan 01 (transport, frame mux, identity types) and plan 03 (`place()` returning ranked candidate NodeId list).
design:
Three processes on the remote-spawn path: `classic` CLI (one-shot), local classicd (originator role), chosen peer's classicd (executor role; forks the user task into a per-task cgroup scope). If local node IS the chosen executor, originator and executor run in the same daemon — same state machines, no peer hop.

Transport: CLI <-> local daemon over Unix domain socket (/run/classicd/control.sock, fallback $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/classicd.sock) carrying the same Frame codec from classic-proto. Daemon <-> daemon: TCP frame mux from plan 01.

Why dedicated frames instead of going through the mailbox layer (plan 05)? Spawn happens BEFORE the task has a mailbox; bootstrapping a mailbox over the mailbox layer is circular. Spawn also has hard ordering (caps before fork; cleanup on exit) that reads cleanly as a small state machine. Plan 05 should be allowed to land AFTER plan 04, since spawn is the headline.

Originator state machine per outstanding spawn:
  Submitted -> Placing -> Trying(idx) -> Running(net_id)
                       \\            |
                        \\---------> Denied(reason)
Trying(idx) cycles through ranked candidate list; SpawnDeny -> Trying(idx+1) or, if exhausted, Denied(AllCandidatesRefused). SpawnAck -> Running; thereafter stdio/exit frames are relayed verbatim between the CLI socket and the chosen executor connection.

Frames live in crates/classic-proto/src/frames/spawn.rs, all bincode v2 fixed-int LE:
- SpawnRequest { req_id: u64, requires: String, rank: String, argv: Vec<String>, env: Vec<String>, exclusive_device: bool, stdin_kind: Option<StdinKind>, hop: u8 }; StdinKind = Inherit | File. hop > 2 dropped (loop guard).
- SpawnAck { req_id: u64, net_id: NetId }
- SpawnDeny { req_id: u64, reason: DenyReason, detail: String }; DenyReason = NoCandidates | AllCandidatesRefused | PredicateNotSatisfied | DeviceTaken | CgroupSetupFailed | ExecFailed | HopExceeded | Internal.
- ChildStdio { req_id: u64, stream: StdioStream (Stdin|Stdout|Stderr), data: Vec<u8> (empty == EOF) }
- ChildExit { req_id: u64, code: Option<i32>, signal: Option<i32> }

DeviceCap and CapBroker (in crates/classic-cap/src/lib.rs):
  DeviceKind = GpuMinor(u32) [-> /dev/nvidia<N>; BPF allows (c,195,N) + ctl 254/255 iff any GpuMinor held] | PciSlot(BdfAddr) [resolved via /sys/bus/pci/devices/<bdf>/uevent].
  DeviceCap { kind, exclusive, holder: MboxId, _release: BrokerHandle (Drop -> broker.release_internal) }. NO public constructor — caps cannot be forged.
  CapBroker::acquire(holder, &[DeviceKind], exclusive) -> Result<Vec<DeviceCap>, AcquireError> — atomic all-or-nothing; exclusive=true refuses if any kind is held; exclusive=false co-holds with shared only.
  AcquireError = Taken { kind } | SharedConflict { kind, n }.
  release_all(holder) — force-release every cap; called on child exit OR originator disconnect.

Cgroup setup (cgroup v2 unified hierarchy, kernel >= 6.1). Per-task path: /sys/fs/cgroup/classicd.slice/task-<MboxId>.scope/. One-time at daemon startup: mkdir classicd.slice, enable +cpu+memory+pids in subtree_control. Per task before fork: mkdir scope; write "max" to memory.max + cpu.max; 1024 to pids.max; helper_pid into cgroup.procs. CPU/memory limits are "max" in v1 — predicate-driven quotas are a follow-up.

Device controller is NOT a sysfs file in v2 — it's a BPF_CGROUP_DEVICE program attached to the scope's cgroup fd. classic-cap/src/bpf/devices.bpf.c compiled at crate build time via libbpf-cargo. Default-deny. Allow (c,1,*) standard /dev/null|zero|random|urandom|tty. Allow (c,195,254) and (c,195,255) iff any GpuMinor held. Allow (c,195,N) per GpuMinor(N). Allow PCI char nodes per PciSlot. Attach with bpf(BPF_PROG_ATTACH, BPF_CGROUP_DEVICE) to scope fd. Update BPF_MAP_TYPE_HASH allowlist on every cap acquire/release. NOT borrowing systemd/runc device_filter — classicd owns the hierarchy outright.

Crate/file layout:
  crates/classic-proto/src/frames/spawn.rs (NEW), classic-proto/src/lib.rs (MOD register 0x0300..=0x0304).
  crates/classic-cap/ (NEW): Cargo.toml + build.rs (libbpf-cargo); src/lib.rs, broker.rs, cgroup.rs, bpf/devices.bpf.c, bpf/loader.rs, nvidia.rs.
  crates/classic-spawn/ (NEW): src/lib.rs (originate/execute), originator.rs, executor.rs, stdio.rs, cgroup_scope.rs.
  crates/classic-cli/src/bin/classic.rs (MOD wire `spawn`), src/cmd/spawn.rs (NEW).
  crates/classic-node/src/lib.rs (MOD register spawn handlers).

CLI: classic spawn --requires "<predicate>" [--rank "<expr>"] [--exclusive-device] [--stdin <file>|-] [--env KEY=VAL]... -- <argv>...
- --requires required (empty forbidden; use "true").
- --stdin "-" inherits CLI stdin; otherwise streams a file.
- --env KEY=VAL repeatable; CLI's own env is NOT forwarded.
- "--" mandatory before argv.
- Exit code: child exit (0-255) on normal; 128+signum on signal death; 2 on any spawn failure (predicate mismatch, all candidates refused, exec failure); 1 on usage error.

Public API:
  classic_spawn::originate(&OriginatorCtx, SpawnRequest, ControlConn) -> Result<(), SpawnError>;
  classic_spawn::execute(&ExecutorCtx, SpawnRequest, PeerConn) -> Result<(), SpawnError>;
  OriginatorCtx { ad_cache: Arc<AdCache>, peers: Arc<PeerSet>, local_node: NodeId, local_executor: ExecutorCtx };
  ExecutorCtx { broker: Arc<CapBroker>, mbox_alloc: Arc<MboxAllocator>, local_node: NodeId }.

Failure handling (originator retries on PredicateNotSatisfied, DeviceTaken, CgroupSetupFailed, connect failures; never on ExecFailed or HopExceeded):
- place() empty -> SpawnDeny{NoCandidates} -> CLI exits 2.
- TCP connect fails -> skip candidate; all-fail -> AllCandidatesRefused.
- Stale ad on executor -> SpawnDeny{PredicateNotSatisfied}.
- Exclusive cap held -> SpawnDeny{DeviceTaken}.
- mkdir EPERM/ENOSPC -> SpawnDeny{CgroupSetupFailed, detail:<errno>}.
- BPF attach EINVAL -> SpawnDeny{CgroupSetupFailed}; caps released.
- ENOEXEC/ENOENT on exec -> SpawnDeny{ExecFailed}; caps released; NO retry.
- Child crash (segfault/SIGKILL) -> ChildExit{signal:Some(n), code:None}; caps released.
- Originator disconnect (Ctrl-C / socket loss) -> Executor release_all caps; SIGTERM child, SIGKILL after 10s.

Non-functional: e2e spawn latency (CLI start -> child's first stdout byte) <= 250ms on 2-node loopback with /bin/echo. Linux 6.1+, x86_64 (aarch64 must compile, not gated). NVML required only on NVIDIA hosts. classicd runs as root; children inherit daemon UID in v1; trusted cluster. <= 1024 concurrent tasks/executor; per-stream stdio buffer 1 MiB.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] AC-1: Frames 0x0300-0x0304 encode/decode under bincode v2; no frame outside this range is owned by spawn.
- [ ] AC-2: Single-node `classic spawn --requires "true" -- /bin/echo hi` prints `hi`, exits 0.
- [ ] AC-3: 2-node setup with predicate forcing remote placement -> child runs on remote (`hostname`).
- [ ] AC-4: 4 MiB random round-trip via `--stdin` + `/bin/cat` is byte-perfect.
- [ ] AC-5: Child exit code (0, non-zero, signal) propagates correctly.
- [ ] AC-6: Unsatisfiable predicate -> CLI exits 2 with `no node matches predicate`.
- [ ] AC-7: Concurrent `--exclusive-device` for the same device fails the second invocation with `DeviceTaken`.
- [ ] AC-8: SIGINT on the CLI kills the remote child within 1 s and releases all its caps.
- [ ] AC-9: `task-<MboxId>.scope` is created on spawn, contains the child's pid, and is removed after exit.
- [ ] AC-10 (`hw-gpu`): BPF denies `/dev/nvidia1` to a task holding only `GpuMinor(0)`.
- [ ] AC-11: All non-hw tests pass on Linux 6.1+ x86_64 in CI.

title: classic-ad: GPU probe via NVML with graceful degrade
description:
Part of epic classic-ygs. Plan: plans/02-node-ad-hw-discovery.md @ a7b6585.

## Reading first
- git show a7b6585:plans/02-node-ad-hw-discovery.md (sections: Interfaces — GpuProbe, Hardware-dependent tests, Open questions §4)
- plans/ARCHITECTURE.md (NVML availability)

## Design slice (verbatim)
NVML access is encapsulated:

pub(crate) struct GpuProbe { nvml: Option<nvml_wrapper::Nvml> }

impl GpuProbe {
    pub fn new() -> Self {
        match nvml_wrapper::Nvml::init() {
            Ok(n)  => Self { nvml: Some(n) },
            Err(e) => { tracing::warn!(?e, "NVML unavailable; reporting no GPUs"); Self { nvml: None } }
        }
    }
    pub fn enumerate(&self) -> Vec<GpuInfo>;
    pub fn refresh_dynamic(&self, out: &mut [GpuInfo]); // vram_free, util
}

NVML calls used (all via nvml-wrapper):
- Nvml::init() — once at process start
- Nvml::device_count()
- Nvml::device_by_index(i)
- Device::uuid(), Device::name(), Device::pci_info(), Device::memory_info(),
  Device::cuda_compute_capability(), Device::utilization_rates(),
  Device::nvlink_remote_pci_info(link) for link in 0..Device::nvlink_count() (skip inactive links)

No Cargo feature flag for nvml-wrapper — it links lazily via dlopen so the binary builds and runs on hosts without the driver (Open question §4).

NVLink peers: collect remote-end GPU UUIDs via nvlink_remote_pci_info(link) for link in 0..nvlink_count(); skip inactive links. Map remote PCI addresses back to local-known UUIDs where possible; otherwise store the PCI string. (v1: store remote GPU UUIDs reachable via active NVLinks per the schema; for non-local peers the PCI addr suffices as identity hint.)

## Inputs
- crates/classic-ad/src/schema.rs (GpuInfo struct)
- nvml-wrapper crate (Cargo dep)

## Outputs
- crates/classic-ad/src/discovery/gpu.rs (new — GpuProbe::{new, enumerate, refresh_dynamic})
- crates/classic-ad/Cargo.toml (modified — add nvml-wrapper, tracing)

## Test plan
- Unit test: GpuProbe::new() when NVML is absent. Use tracing-test to capture logs; assert enumerate() returns [] AND exactly one warning was logged containing 'NVML' (FR-2, AC-4).
- #[ignore]'d hardware test (// HW: nvidia): Real-NVML enumeration on H100 host: assert gpus[0].compute_capability == (9, 0); vram_total_mb ≈ 81920 ± 1024; nvlink_peers non-empty on a multi-GPU host.
- #[ignore]'d hardware test: vram_free_mb falls and rises around a cudaMalloc test stub.
- cargo test -p classic-ad green on a host with no NVIDIA driver installed (AC-1).

## Acceptance
- [ ] GpuProbe::new() when libnvidia-ml.so.1 is missing: returns a probe with nvml=None, logs exactly one warning containing 'NVML', does NOT panic.
- [ ] GpuProbe::enumerate() returns [] when nvml is None.
- [ ] GpuProbe::refresh_dynamic() updates vram_free_mb and utilization_pct in place when nvml is Some.
- [ ] No Cargo feature gate around nvml-wrapper.
- [ ] Hardware-dependent tests are #[ignore]'d with // HW: nvidia comment so CI skips them.
- [ ] cargo test -p classic-ad green on a no-driver host (AC-1).
acceptance_criteria:
GpuProbe::new() with missing libnvidia-ml.so.1 logs exactly one warning containing 'NVML', does not panic, and enumerate() returns [].
GpuProbe::refresh_dynamic() updates vram_free_mb and utilization_pct in place when NVML is available.
nvml-wrapper is NOT behind a Cargo feature gate.
Hardware tests are #[ignore]'d with '// HW: nvidia' marker.
cargo test -p classic-ad green on a no-driver host.

title: classic-proto: identity types + Frame + length-prefixed codec
description:
Part of epic classic-ivc. Plan: plans/01-skeleton-transport.md @ a7b6585.

## Reading first
- git show a7b6585:plans/01-skeleton-transport.md
- git show a7b6585:plans/ARCHITECTURE.md

## Excerpted design slice

### From ARCHITECTURE.md § Identity types
```rust
#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct NodeId(pub [u8; 16]);

#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct MboxId(pub u64);

#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct NetId { pub node: NodeId, pub mbox: MboxId, }
```

### From ARCHITECTURE.md § Wire transport (baseline)
> Frame format (length-prefixed, little-endian):
> +----------+----------+----------------------+
> |  length  |   kind   |       payload        |
> |  u32 LE  |  u16 LE  |   length - 2 bytes   |
> +----------+----------+----------------------+
> length is the byte count of kind + payload (so the on-wire size is 4 + length). Max frame size: 16 MiB.

### From plan § Design / Frame format
> Defined in ARCHITECTURE.md § "Wire transport (baseline)". Recap: length-prefixed, little-endian, length covers kind + payload, max frame size 16 MiB. Plan 01 enforces the 16 MiB cap on both encode and decode and returns a CodecError::FrameTooLarge on violation.

### From plan § Design / Public APIs (excerpt for this task)
```rust
#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
#[repr(u16)]
pub enum FrameKind {
    Hello     = 0x0001,
    Heartbeat = 0x0002,
    Bye       = 0x0003,
    Error     = 0x0004,
    // 0x0005..=0x00FF reserved for future proto-range frames
}

pub struct Frame {
    pub kind: u16,        // raw u16, not FrameKind, so unknown kinds round-trip
    pub payload: Bytes,
}

pub fn encode_frame<W: AsyncWrite + Unpin>(w: &mut W, f: &Frame) -> impl Future<Output = Result<(), CodecError>>;
pub fn decode_frame<R: AsyncRead + Unpin>(r: &mut R) -> impl Future<Output = Result<Frame, CodecError>>;

#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
pub enum CodecError {
    #[error("frame too large: {0} bytes")]
    FrameTooLarge(u32),
    #[error("io: {0}")] Io(#[from] std::io::Error),
    #[error("decode: {0}")] Decode(String),
}
```

### Encoding (ARCHITECTURE.md § Frame-kind allocation)
> Encoding: serde + bincode v2 (fixed-int, little-endian) for control frames.

Plan-1 § Open questions notes: use bincode::config::legacy() (fixed-int LE).

## Inputs
- /home/horde/src/classic/crates/classic-proto/Cargo.toml (added by T classic-fxu)
- bincode v2 docs (legacy() config), tokio::io traits, bytes::Bytes, thiserror, serde

## Outputs
Files to create/modify under /home/horde/src/classic/crates/classic-proto/:
- src/ids.rs — NodeId, MboxId, NetId per ARCHITECTURE.md (Display/Debug/FromStr for NodeId useful but not required here)
- src/frame.rs — FrameKind enum, Frame struct, encode_frame/decode_frame async free functions, MAX_FRAME_SIZE const = 16 * 1024 * 1024, CodecError
- src/lib.rs — pub mod ids; pub mod frame; pub use ...
- crates/classic-proto/Cargo.toml — add deps: serde (derive), bincode v2, bytes, tokio (io util), thiserror, async-trait (deferred — for sibling task)

Public signatures:
```rust
pub const MAX_FRAME_SIZE: u32 = 16 * 1024 * 1024;

pub async fn encode_frame<W: AsyncWrite + Unpin>(w: &mut W, f: &Frame) -> Result<(), CodecError>;
pub async fn decode_frame<R: AsyncRead + Unpin>(r: &mut R) -> Result<Frame, CodecError>;
```
- Decoder MUST reject length > MAX_FRAME_SIZE before allocating the payload buffer.
- Encoder MUST reject payloads where (2 + payload.len()) > MAX_FRAME_SIZE before writing.

## Test plan
- cargo test -p classic-proto frame::tests::roundtrip — encode then decode of frames with random payloads.
- cargo test -p classic-proto frame::tests::oversize — encoding 17 MiB payload returns FrameTooLarge; decoding a length prefix of 17 MiB returns FrameTooLarge without allocating that much memory.
- cargo test -p classic-proto frame::tests::unknown_kind_roundtrips — kind=0x0FFF survives encode/decode (Frame.kind is raw u16).
- proptest seeded random payloads up to 4 KiB recommended.

## Task-specific Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] NodeId, MboxId, NetId compile and derive serde + Hash + Eq per ARCHITECTURE.md.
- [ ] FrameKind has exactly the four variants (Hello=0x0001, Heartbeat=0x0002, Bye=0x0003, Error=0x0004) at the right discriminants.
- [ ] encode_frame / decode_frame round-trip arbitrary payloads on a tokio::io::duplex.
- [ ] Encoder errors with FrameTooLarge when payload exceeds 16 MiB.
- [ ] Decoder errors with FrameTooLarge for length prefix > 16 MiB without allocating the payload.
- [ ] Frame.kind is u16 (not FrameKind) so unknown kinds round-trip.

## Intra-epic deps
- Blocks-on: classic-fxu (workspace must exist)
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] NodeId, MboxId, NetId compile and derive serde + Hash + Eq.
- [ ] FrameKind has Hello=0x0001, Heartbeat=0x0002, Bye=0x0003, Error=0x0004.
- [ ] encode_frame / decode_frame round-trip arbitrary payloads.
- [ ] Encoder errors with FrameTooLarge for >16 MiB payload.
- [ ] Decoder errors with FrameTooLarge for length prefix >16 MiB without allocating.
- [ ] Frame.kind is raw u16 so unknown kinds round-trip.

title: classic-place: lexer (tokens, position tracking, keywords)
description:
Epic: classic-i0n (Feature: Placement predicates)
Plan: plans/03-placement-predicates.md @ commit a7b6585. Read with: git show a7b6585:plans/03-placement-predicates.md
Foundations: plans/ARCHITECTURE.md @ a7b6585. Read with: git show a7b6585:plans/ARCHITECTURE.md

## Reading first

Before starting, read in this order:
1. The full plan: `git show a7b6585:plans/03-placement-predicates.md` (~800 lines)
2. ARCHITECTURE.md for crate layout
3. Plan §Design "Grammar" subsection (token alphabet)
4. Plan §Design "Parser strategy" subsection (lexer/parser split)

## Design slice (excerpted from plan §Design)

PEG-style grammar, with implicit whitespace skipping between tokens. Lexer is one-pass scanner producing Vec<Token> with attached positions.

Tokens to produce (from grammar productions Literal/Ident/CmpOp/operators/punctuation):
- Operators: `||`, `&&`, `!`, `==`, `!=`, `<=`, `>=`, `<`, `>`, `+`, `-`, `*`, `/`
- Punctuation: `(`, `)`, `[`, `]`, `,`, `.`
- Literals:
  - Float := [0-9]+ "." [0-9]+ ( ("e"|"E") ("+"|"-")? [0-9]+ )?
  - HexInt := "0x" [0-9A-Fa-f]+
  - Int := [0-9]+
  - String := '"' ( [^"\\] | "\\" ["\\nrt] )* '"'
  - Bool := "true" | "false"
- Identifier: [A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*
- Reserved keywords (cannot appear as field names): any, all, count, in, true, false

Position tracking: lexer carries a Pos { line: u32, col: u32, byte: u32 } and every token records its start position.

Hard limits: parser rejects inputs over 64 KiB with a hard error (defense against pathological predicates) — enforce at lexer boundary by short-circuiting before tokenizing.

## Inputs

- Crate created: `crates/classic-place/Cargo.toml` (NEW). Dependencies: workspace-shared `serde` (lex itself needs nothing beyond std, but the crate Cargo.toml is shared).
- Identity types from classic-proto (NodeId etc.) — not needed by lexer directly.
- AC-6 forbids new external deps; lexer must be std-only.

## Outputs

- New file `crates/classic-place/src/lex.rs`:
  - `pub struct Pos { pub line: u32, pub col: u32, pub byte: u32 }`
  - `pub enum TokKind { ... }` covering every token in the grammar including keywords as distinct kinds (KwAny, KwAll, KwCount, KwIn, KwTrue, KwFalse) so the parser does not re-match strings.
  - `pub struct Token { pub kind: TokKind, pub pos: Pos, pub text: ... }` (text either the source slice or owned String for literals).
  - `pub fn lex(src: &str) -> Result<Vec<Token>, ParseError>` (returns Err with ParseErrorKind::Lex on bad input).
- Update `crates/classic-place/src/lib.rs` (NEW) to declare `mod lex;`.
- New file `crates/classic-place/src/error.rs` (skeleton — just enough to compile lex.rs; full Display impls land in the error-task). Stub:
  ```
  pub struct ParseError { pub msg: String, pub line: u32, pub col: u32, pub expected: Vec<&'static str>, pub kind: ParseErrorKind }
  pub enum ParseErrorKind { Lex, Syntax, Type }
  ```

## Test plan

In-module tests under `crates/classic-place/src/lex.rs` (`#[cfg(test)] mod tests`):

- Every token kind: assert each operator, each punctuation, each literal kind, each keyword and a plain identifier produce the expected TokKind.
- Position tracking: feed multi-line input (e.g. `"a\n  && b"`); assert the && token has line=2, col=3.
- Bad escapes in strings: `"\\q"` -> ParseError with Lex kind.
- Unterminated string: `"H100` (no closing quote) -> ParseError, line/col point to opening quote per error-example in plan.
- Malformed hex: `0xZZ` -> ParseError.
- Trailing decimal point: `3.` -> ParseError (not a Float per grammar; not an Int followed by Dot per same reason).
- Source >64 KiB -> ParseError (Lex).
- Keyword vs identifier: `any` -> KwAny, `anyway` -> Ident.

Run: `cargo test -p classic-place lex`.

## Acceptance criteria

- AC-L1: All token kinds in plan grammar produce a distinct TokKind; reserved keywords are tokenized as their dedicated kinds, never as Ident.
- AC-L2: Each Token carries Pos { line, col, byte } where line/col are 1-indexed and accurate across newlines.
- AC-L3: Bad-input tests above each produce a ParseError with kind=Lex.
- AC-L4: Inputs >64 KiB rejected before tokenizing.
- AC-L5: No unsafe. Std-only.

## Intra-epic deps

- Blocks: parser task, all downstream tasks.
- Depends on: nothing within this epic.
acceptance_criteria:
AC-L1: All token kinds in plan grammar produce a distinct TokKind; reserved keywords tokenize as their dedicated kinds.
AC-L2: Each Token carries Pos { line, col, byte }; line/col 1-indexed, accurate across newlines.
AC-L3: Bad escapes, unterminated strings, malformed hex, trailing decimal point each produce ParseError { kind: Lex } with correct line/col.
AC-L4: Inputs >64 KiB rejected at lexer boundary.
AC-L5: No unsafe; std-only (no new external deps).

title: classic-ad: PCI + NUMA + CPU + Mem + Load probes via Sysroot trait
description:
Part of epic classic-ygs. Plan: plans/02-node-ad-hw-discovery.md @ a7b6585.

## Reading first
- git show a7b6585:plans/02-node-ad-hw-discovery.md (sections: Data shapes, Interfaces — Sysroot trait, Testing plan — Unit)
- plans/ARCHITECTURE.md

## Design slice (verbatim)
The discovery probes accept an injectable &dyn Sysroot so unit tests can swap a tempdir fixture for /. A trivial default impl reads from /.

pub trait Sysroot: Send + Sync {
    fn read(&self, rel: &Path) -> std::io::Result<Vec<u8>>;
    fn read_link(&self, rel: &Path) -> std::io::Result<PathBuf>;
    fn read_dir(&self, rel: &Path) -> std::io::Result<Vec<PathBuf>>;
}

Sysfs paths used:
| Probe        | Path                                                     |
| PCI device   | /sys/bus/pci/devices/<addr>/{vendor,device,class,numa_node} |
| IOMMU group  | /sys/bus/pci/devices/<addr>/iommu_group (symlink basename) |
| NUMA list    | /sys/devices/system/node/node*/cpulist                   |
| NUMA mem     | /sys/devices/system/node/node*/meminfo (line MemTotal)   |
| CPU info     | /proc/cpuinfo, /proc/stat                                |
| Mem info     | /proc/meminfo                                            |
| Load         | /proc/loadavg                                            |

A small dependency for PCI parsing is acceptable but the spec is simple enough to do directly. Recommended: implement inline (≤120 LOC) using std::fs. Avoid pciutils-rs and similar.

CpuInfo: cores_online from sysconf(_SC_NPROCESSORS_ONLN); cores_physical deduplicated by (physical id, core id) from /proc/cpuinfo; sockets deduplicated by physical id; model/vendor from CPU 0; arch via std::env::consts::ARCH; mhz from CPU 0 'cpu MHz' rounded.

MemInfo: MemTotal + MemAvailable from /proc/meminfo, in MiB. Pre-3.14 kernels missing MemAvailable: fall back to MemFree + Buffers + Cached.

LoadSample: loadavg_{1m,5m,15m} from /proc/loadavg scaled x100 (1.27 -> 127); cpu_pct computed from /proc/stat deltas across the fast-tick interval (0..=100*cores_online); mem_pct from used/total*100; task_count comes from AdConfig::task_count_fn callback (default 0).

PciDevice fields: addr 'DDDD:BB:DD.F', vendor/device/class as parsed hex, numa_node i32 (-1 if absent), iommu_group via symlink basename of /sys/bus/pci/devices/<addr>/iommu_group (Option<u32>; None if IOMMU disabled). Order results lexically by addr (FR-3).

NumaNode: enumerate every nodeN dir under /sys/devices/system/node/; parse cpulist formats like '0-7,16-23' into Vec<u32>; assign each online CPU to exactly one NUMA node (FR-4). mem_total_mb from per-node meminfo.

## Inputs
- crates/classic-ad/src/schema.rs (from previous task — sub-structs to populate)

## Outputs
- crates/classic-ad/src/discovery/mod.rs (new — Sysroot trait + RealSysroot default impl)
- crates/classic-ad/src/discovery/cpu.rs (new — CpuInfo probe + cpu_pct delta state)
- crates/classic-ad/src/discovery/mem.rs (new — MemInfo probe)
- crates/classic-ad/src/discovery/load.rs (new — LoadSample probe)
- crates/classic-ad/src/discovery/pci.rs (new — PciDevice enumeration ≤120 LOC inline)
- crates/classic-ad/src/discovery/numa.rs (new — NumaNode enumeration + cpulist parser)

## Test plan
- Unit tests in each probe module use a TempdirSysroot fixture seeded with realistic /proc and /sys files.
- cpu.rs: parse fixture /proc/cpuinfo for Xeon 8480+ and Ryzen 9 7950X. Assert socket / core / online-core counts.
- mem.rs: fixture /proc/meminfo with various MemAvailable cases (including missing field — fall back path).
- load.rs: fixture /proc/loadavg ('0.42 0.81 1.10 2/734 1234'); fixture pair of /proc/stat snapshots 1 s apart, assert cpu_pct delta.
- pci.rs: tempdir simulating /sys/bus/pci/devices/0000:1b:00.0/{vendor,device,class,numa_node} plus an iommu_group symlink to .../iommu_groups/17. Assert exact PciDevice. Assert lexical addr ordering.
- numa.rs: tempdir with two nodeN dirs, varied cpulist formats ('0-7,16-23').

## Acceptance
- [ ] Sysroot trait + RealSysroot default impl present and used by all probes.
- [ ] All probe unit tests green via tempdir fixtures.
- [ ] PCI probe enumerates entries lexically by addr (FR-3).
- [ ] NUMA probe assigns each online CPU to exactly one node (FR-4).
- [ ] cpu_pct delta math correct across two /proc/stat fixtures.
- [ ] task_count populated from AdConfig::task_count_fn callback (defaults to 0).
acceptance_criteria:
Sysroot trait + RealSysroot default impl present and used by all probes.
All probe unit tests green via tempdir fixtures.
PCI probe enumerates entries lexically by addr.
NUMA probe assigns each online CPU to exactly one node.
cpu_pct delta math correct across two /proc/stat fixtures.
task_count populated from AdConfig::task_count_fn callback (defaults to 0).

title: Feature: Placement predicates
description:
Hand-rolled expression DSL + matcher + ranker for hardware-aware task placement in classic-place.

Users declare hardware requirements per process as predicate strings (e.g. `any(gpu, gpu.vram_mb >= 80000 && !gpu.in_use)`); the cluster filters NodeAds by the predicate, ranks survivors with a numeric expression, and returns ordered candidates. This is the API behind the headline SSI feature.

Plan: plans/03-placement-predicates.md @ commit a7b6585. Read with: git show a7b6585:plans/03-placement-predicates.md

Foundations: plans/ARCHITECTURE.md (frame ranges, identity types, crate layout). Read with: git show a7b6585:plans/ARCHITECTURE.md

Workflow rules: AGENTS.md "Feature Development Workflow" section.

Scope: crates/classic-place leaf crate; depends only on classic-ad (NodeAd/NodeId) and classic-proto (frames). Owns FrameKind range 0x0500-0x05FF.

Out of scope: placement groups (plan 07), spawn pipeline (plan 04), hardware discovery (plan 02), remote-placement RPC behavior (v2), label access syntax (deferred), live re-placement.
design:
Hand-rolled DSL chosen over CEL/Rhai/Kubernetes-style label selectors (Alts A/B/C rejected: maintenance liability of moving CEL spec; Turing-completeness liability of Rhai; lack of composability for ranges/existentials with label selectors).

Grammar: PEG-style spec, recursive-descent parser (~400 lines hand-written Rust). Operator precedence encoded in OrExpr -> AndExpr -> NotExpr -> CmpExpr -> AddExpr -> MulExpr -> UnaryExpr -> Primary chain. LL(1) modulo `in` (one-token lookahead).

Two top-level expression kinds: Requirement (boolean predicate, filters) and Rank (numeric, orders). AST is a typed Expr enum with BinOp/UnaryOp/AggOp variants. Eval Val enum is Bool/Int/Float/Str.

Aggregates any/all/count over collections (gpu, pci) introduce iteration variable binding that shadows outer field names. Empty-collection vacuous-truth: any=false, all=true, count=0. `x in [a,b,c]` lowers to multi-eq AST.

Safe-eval guarantees: no I/O, no shell, no FS, no time/RNG, no recursion in the language, no closures, no UDFs, no string-as-code. Eval cost O(ast_size * max_collection_size). Predicate text capped at 64 KiB.

Field-skew tolerance: missing fields evaluate to false (boolean) or numeric neutral element (rank); never panic. Tie-breaking by NodeId byte ascending.

Wire frames in 0x0500-0x05FF: Place_Request=0x0501, Place_Response=0x0502, Place_Error=0x0503. v1 uses local in-process plumbing through these frames; v2 swaps carrier without re-allocating kinds.

Public API: parse_req, parse_rank, matches, score, place, place_str, default_rank. Default rank: `-load.cpu_pct - 1000.0 * (count(gpu) - count(gpu, gpu.in_use))`.

Crate layout: src/{lib.rs, lex.rs, parse.rs, ast.rs, typeck.rs, eval.rs, error.rs, frame.rs, tests/fixtures.rs} + tests/{grammar.rs, eval.rs, golden_errors.rs}. classic-proto/src/frame.rs MODIFIED to register Place_Request/Response/Error in FrameKind enum.
acceptance_criteria:
AC-1: parse_req accepts all six example predicates in plan §Examples and parse_rank accepts the default rank expression.
AC-2: For each (predicate, fixture) pair in the eval table, observed matches/score result equals the table's expected value.
AC-3: place(...) on the 5-fixture cluster picks the documented best node for each example predicate under the default rank.
AC-4: Every error-message example renders byte-identically to the pinned snapshot.
AC-5: Frame kinds 0x0501, 0x0502, 0x0503 are registered in classic-proto::FrameKind; bincode round-trip tests pass for each payload struct.
AC-6: No `unsafe` in classic-place. cargo deny (or equivalent) reports zero new external dependencies introduced beyond serde, bincode, and the workspace-shared crates.
AC-7: cargo test -p classic-place is green on Linux x86_64.
AC-8: cargo bench (or single #[test] perf_smoke() gated under --release) shows place(...) under 5 ms for 100 ads x 8 GPUs x 30-AST-node predicate.
AC-9: Documentation: every public item in classic-place has a doc comment; cargo doc -p classic-place builds with no warnings.

title: classic-ad: NodeAd schema + sub-structs + bincode v2 codec
description:
Part of epic classic-ygs. Plan: plans/02-node-ad-hw-discovery.md @ a7b6585.

## Reading first
- git show a7b6585:plans/02-node-ad-hw-discovery.md (sections: Data shapes, File / crate layout)
- plans/ARCHITECTURE.md (identity types, frame encoding)

## Design slice (verbatim)
All structs derive Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize, bincode::Encode, bincode::Decode. NodeAd itself also derives PartialEq for tests; the load-bearing equality is node_id + generation. bincode v2 with the fixed-int, little-endian configuration matching the rest of classic-proto. Strings use String (length-prefixed UTF-8). Optional fields use Option. No untagged unions on the wire.

NodeAd { node_id: NodeId, hostname: String, proto_version: u32, generation: u64, boot_time: u64, cpu: CpuInfo, mem: MemInfo, gpus: Vec<GpuInfo>, pci: Vec<PciDevice>, numa: Vec<NumaNode>, load: LoadSample }
CpuInfo { cores_online: u32, cores_physical: u32, sockets: u32, model: String, vendor: String, arch: String, mhz: u32 }
MemInfo { total_mb: u64, available_mb: u64 }
GpuInfo { index: u32, uuid: String, name: String, pci_vendor: u16, pci_device: u16, pci_addr: String, vram_total_mb: u64, vram_free_mb: u64, compute_capability: (u32, u32), nvlink_peers: Vec<String>, utilization_pct: u32 }
PciDevice { addr: String, vendor: u16, device: u16, class: u32, numa_node: i32, iommu_group: Option<u32> }
NumaNode { id: u32, cpus: Vec<u32>, mem_total_mb: u64 }
LoadSample { loadavg_1m: u32, loadavg_5m: u32, loadavg_15m: u32, cpu_pct: u32, mem_pct: u32, task_count: u32 }

AdGossip enum { Full(NodeAd), Delta { node_id, generation } } — v1 always sends Full; receivers must accept both kinds.
AdRequest { from: NodeId }.
AdUpdate enum { Inserted(NodeAd), Updated(NodeAd), Removed(NodeId) } — local-only, not on wire.

## Inputs
- classic-proto::NodeId, classic-proto::PROTO_VERSION (from plan 01)
- bincode v2, serde — match config used by classic-proto

## Outputs
- crates/classic-ad/Cargo.toml (new — depends on classic-proto, bincode v2, serde, tracing)
- crates/classic-ad/src/lib.rs (new — re-exports)
- crates/classic-ad/src/schema.rs (new — all structs above + derives)

## Test plan
- crates/classic-ad/tests/schema_roundtrip.rs: random-fill NodeAd via proptest, bincode encode_to_vec → decode_from_slice, assert equality.
- Verify encoded NodeAd ≤ 8 KiB for a host with 16 GPUs + 256 PCI devices (bound check).
- Round-trip the canonical example ad from the design doc; assert byte-equal.
- cargo test -p classic-ad --test schema_roundtrip green on Linux x86_64.
acceptance_criteria:
- All structs in schema.rs derive Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize, bincode::Encode, bincode::Decode (NodeAd also PartialEq).
- bincode encode/decode is round-trip stable on a proptest-generated NodeAd.
- The canonical example ad from the design doc round-trips byte-equal.
- Encoded size of a max-realistic ad (16 GPUs + 256 PCI devs) is ≤ 8 KiB.
- AdGossip enum variants Full and Delta both encode/decode.
- cargo build -p classic-ad and cargo test -p classic-ad pass without NVIDIA driver.

title: Feature: Node ad + hardware discovery
description:
Build the classic-ad crate: define the NodeAd schema, implement hardware discovery probes (CPU, mem, GPU via NVML, PCI, NUMA, load), gossip ads across the mesh, and expose an in-process AdStore API for downstream plans (placement / spawn).

Plan: plans/02-node-ad-hw-discovery.md @ commit a7b6585. Read with: git show a7b6585:plans/02-node-ad-hw-discovery.md

Foundations: plans/ARCHITECTURE.md (identity types NodeId, frame format, frame ranges, encoding bincode v2, transport).

Owner crate: crates/classic-ad. Owns frame-kind range 0x0100-0x01FF.
Consumers: classic-place (read-only), classic-node (runs the gossip task).
Depends on plan 01 (skeleton + transport): classic-proto, ConnectionRegistry, FrameKind enum.
design:
Three subsystems sharing an Arc<RwLock>-backed AdStore:
- Discovery: own-node only writer; fast tick (1s) refreshes LoadSample and dynamic GPU fields; slow tick (60s) re-enumerates static hardware; generation increments on any change.
- Gossip RX: registered with classic-proto frame mux; handles frame kinds 0x0100 (NodeAd), 0x0101 (AdGossip), 0x0102 (AdRequest); LWW on (generation, boot_time).
- Gossip TX: 10s ticker plus connection-up hook; broadcasts full self_ad to all live peers.

Schema: NodeAd { node_id, hostname, proto_version, generation, boot_time, cpu, mem, gpus, pci, numa, load } with sub-structs CpuInfo, MemInfo, GpuInfo, PciDevice, NumaNode, LoadSample. All derive Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize, bincode::Encode, bincode::Decode. Bincode v2 fixed-int little-endian on the wire.

GPU probe via nvml-wrapper crate; missing libnvidia-ml.so.1 -> empty gpus list + one warning, no panic.
Sysfs paths: /sys/bus/pci/devices/* (PCI), /sys/devices/system/node/* (NUMA), /proc/{cpuinfo,meminfo,loadavg,stat} (CPU/mem/load).
Sysroot trait abstracts the filesystem so unit tests can swap a tempdir fixture for /.

Public API: classic_ad::start(self_node_id, proto_registry, AdConfig) -> AdStore; AdStore::{self_ad, all_ads, peer, watch}; AdConfig { fast_tick, slow_tick, gossip_period, peer_grace, task_count_fn }.

Eventually consistent — no quorum, no Lamport clocks, no anti-entropy beyond periodic re-broadcast. Single-writer-per-key (each NodeAd owned by the node it describes) makes LWW sufficient.

Out of scope: predicates (plan 03), spawn/placement (plan 04), persistence to disk, ad signing, hot-plug below 60s granularity.
acceptance_criteria:
AC-1: cargo test -p classic-ad green on a host with no NVIDIA driver installed.
AC-2: cargo test -p classic-ad green with NVIDIA driver + GPU; hardware-gated tests pass with --ignored.
AC-3: 3-node cluster — every node's AdStore::all_ads() returns 3 ads within 15s of mesh formation.
AC-4: Node with libnvidia-ml.so.1 removed starts cleanly, logs exactly one warning containing 'NVML', reports gpus: [].
AC-5: Killing one daemon causes its ad to disappear from survivors' AdStore within peer_grace + gossip_period (default 100s).
AC-6: Restarting that daemon causes its ad to reappear within gossip_period (10s) with boot_time strictly greater than before.
AC-7: bincode::encode_to_vec(NodeAd, ...) round-trips byte-equal for the example ad in the design doc.
AC-8: classic-proto::FrameKind includes NodeAd=0x0100, AdGossip=0x0101, AdRequest=0x0102 and cargo deny check passes.
AC-9: All unit + integration tests in the testing plan pass on Linux x86_64.

title: Feature: Skeleton + transport
description:
Plan 01 — the foundation every other plan depends on. Establishes the Cargo workspace (root manifest + 9 crate stubs), the wire transport between classicd daemons (length-prefixed framed TCP, bincode v2 control frames), the Hello/Heartbeat/Bye/Error proto-range frames, and a frame multiplexer that later subsystems hang their handlers off. Ships the classicd binary that loads TOML config, persists a NodeId, dials and accepts peers, drives the lifecycle FSM, and reconnects with exponential backoff. Out of scope: hardware ads, placement, mailboxes, 9P, TLS, dynamic discovery, QUIC.

Plan: plans/01-skeleton-transport.md @ commit a7b6585. Read the committed version with: git show a7b6585:plans/01-skeleton-transport.md
design:
Workspace: Cargo virtual manifest at root; 9 crates per ARCHITECTURE.md (classic-proto, classic-ad, classic-place, classic-mbox, classic-fs, classic-cap, classic-spawn, classic-node, classic-cli). Stubs other than classic-proto and classic-node may be empty.

Wire format (per ARCHITECTURE.md "Wire transport"): length-prefixed LE: u32 length covers kind+payload, u16 LE FrameKind, payload. 16 MiB cap enforced both directions, returns CodecError::FrameTooLarge.

FrameKind range 0x0000-0x00FF: Hello=0x0001, Heartbeat=0x0002, Bye=0x0003, Error=0x0004. PROTO_VERSION=1. Frame.kind is raw u16 so unknown kinds round-trip.

Encoding: serde + bincode v2 fixed-int LE (legacy config) for control payloads.

Transport model: one TCP connection per ordered peer pair; lower-NodeId initiates and wins tiebreak. PeerLink actor per established conn drives Hello+Heartbeat. PeerMesh supervisor spawns one dialer task per configured peer + one listener; reconnect backoff 250ms->30s, ±20% jitter.

Lifecycle FSM: Init -> HelloPending (5s timeout) -> Healthy -> Unhealthy (3 missed = 15s) -> Closed. Closure only on Bye/transport error/handshake reject; Unhealthy keeps conn open and recovers on any inbound frame.

FrameMux: [Option<Arc<dyn FrameHandler>>;8] indexed by high byte. Plan 01 registers proto-range handler. Unknown range = warn+drop (do NOT send Error). Error reserved for handshake-time protocol violations.

Config: TOML at --config | /etc/classicd/config.toml | $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/classicd/config.toml. NodeId: 16 random bytes via getrandom on first start, persisted at state_dir/node_id mode 0600 (atomic write+rename); state_dir is mode 0700.

Logging: tracing-subscriber, RUST_LOG override [log].level. Shutdown: SIGTERM/SIGINT -> stop dialers/listener -> Bye on all healthy peers (1s grace) -> exit 0.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] AC-1: cargo build --workspace succeeds; cargo test --workspace passes on Linux x86_64.
- [ ] AC-2: All 9 crates from ARCHITECTURE.md § Repository layout exist as compiling stubs in the workspace.
- [ ] AC-3: Two classicd instances dialed at each other reach Healthy within 1 s on loopback and exchange Heartbeats indefinitely.
- [ ] AC-4: A classicd started with a peer pointing at a non-existent address keeps retrying with exponential backoff and logs each retry.
- [ ] AC-5: A classicd whose peer is killed observes the unhealthy transition within 15 s of the last heartbeat.
- [ ] AC-6: A classicd whose peer was killed and restarted reconnects automatically without operator intervention.
- [ ] AC-7: Two classicd instances with mismatched PROTO_VERSION log ProtoVersionMismatch and refuse to peer.
- [ ] AC-8: state_dir/node_id is created with mode 0600 and survives a daemon restart (same NodeId reported in logs).
- [ ] AC-9: RUST_LOG=debug classicd emits structured events for every state transition in the lifecycle FSM.
- [ ] AC-10: SIGTERM produces a clean shutdown with Bye sent to every healthy peer; exit code is 0.
- [ ] AC-11: A handler registered against frame range 0x01 (simulated by a test handler) receives a frame kind = 0x0123 injected on a PeerLink; an unhandled kind = 0x0723 is logged and dropped without closing the connection.
- [ ] AC-12: Frame larger than 16 MiB returns FrameTooLarge on encode and decode without OOM or panic.

title: classic-fs: real FUSE bridge (hw-fuse feature) — fuser mount + umount2 + stale reap
description: Follow-up to classic-ebs. The Namespace::mount_fuse / unmount surface lands today as no-op stubs (mountpoint created/removed without an actual mount). The real FUSE bridge needs: fuser crate dep behind a hw-fuse Cargo feature; mount(2) call with mount options ro=yes, allow_other=no, fsname=classic, subtype=classic; umount2(MNT_DETACH) on unmount; stale-mount reap on daemon startup walking /run/classic/ns/ and umount2(MNT_FORCE|MNT_DETACH). Requires root + CAP_SYS_ADMIN at runtime, so tests gate on a CLASSIC_FUSE_TESTS=1 env var or --ignored.

title: docs: top-level README.md for the project
description:
## Why

The repo currently has no top-level README. Newcomers (and the user
themselves on a fresh checkout) land at https://github.com/lesserevil/classic
with no orientation.

## What

Create `README.md` at the repo root covering:

1. **Name + tagline** — Classic = CLustered Agentic Single System Image
   Cluster (recursive). One-line pitch: "Single-system-image cluster
   runtime for Linux — declare hardware requirements per process, the
   cluster places the process on a node that has matching hardware
   available."
2. **Status** — v1 in development; link to the epic plans in
   `plans/01-skeleton-transport.md` through `plans/09-qemu-e2e.md`.
3. **Architecture link** — `plans/ARCHITECTURE.md` is the cross-cutting
   source of truth.
4. **Building** — `cargo build --workspace`; binaries are `classicd`
   (the daemon) and `classic` (the CLI).
5. **Quick demo** — point at `examples/multinode/` once it exists
   (currently a forward reference).
6. **Predicate language** — link to `plans/dsl-reference.md`.
7. **Project conventions** — link to `AGENTS.md` and `CLAUDE.md` for
   contributor / agent workflow.
8. **License** — MIT OR Apache-2.0 (matches workspace.package.license).

## Acceptance

- [ ] `README.md` exists at repo root and renders cleanly on GitHub.
- [ ] All cross-references (ARCHITECTURE.md, plans/, dsl-reference.md,
      AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md) resolve.
- [ ] No build steps fail under the documented `cargo build --workspace`
      instruction.

## Notes

- Do NOT block on examples/multinode existing — phrase that section as
  "Coming with plan 08 (classic-o4c)".
- Mention the recursive acronym in the tagline; don't expand it
  repeatedly in the body.

title: qemu-xtask: CI workflow + README + leak-free + acceptance verification
description:
Part of epic classic-sub. Plan: plans/09-qemu-e2e.md @ commit bcd0410.

## Reading first
`git show bcd0410:plans/09-qemu-e2e.md` (sections: CI integration, Acceptance criteria, FR-9, FR-10)

## Plan slice (excerpted)
> CI integration: opt-in only.
> - .github/workflows/qemu-e2e.yml triggered by workflow_dispatch (manual button) and PR label 'qemu-e2e'
> - NOT triggered by cargo test, cargo xtask test, cargo xtask demo-smoke, push to main, or any default-on path
> - On tagged release commits, runs automatically as a release gate
>
> FR-9: failing case dumps VM serial log + agent log + runner log + last 200 lines of stdout/stderr
>       to target/qemu/results/<case>/<kernel>/
> FR-10: no QEMU process leaked across runs (cleanup on SIGINT, panic, normal exit)

## Inputs
- T1-T5 (full xtask functional)
- Existing CI structure (whatever .github/workflows/ already contains from earlier epics; this task adds one new file)

## Outputs
- .github/workflows/qemu-e2e.yml — triggers: workflow_dispatch + PR label 'qemu-e2e' + tag push
  - Smoke job: single-cell canary (bpf-device-cgroup on 6.6) — ~2 min
  - Full job: complete matrix — ~20 min
- xtasks/qemu/README.md — full content per AC-10 (developer can add a case without modifying Rust source)
- Cleanup harness (signal handlers, RAII pattern for QEMU child procs) — wired into runner.rs from T1
- Negative-case verification: run a deliberately-failing case (assert.exit_code=0 against /bin/false) and confirm artifact bundle (AC-8)
- AC-3 verification: a host-side check (perhaps a simple grep against ps output during cargo test) confirming no qemu-system-x86_64 spawned

## Test plan
- e2e: workflow_dispatch trigger from a branch; full matrix runs to green
- e2e: open a PR without 'qemu-e2e' label, push commits — workflow does NOT run (AC-4)
- e2e: add label, workflow runs
- Integration: deliberate-fail case produces artifact bundle (AC-8)
- Integration: SIGINT mid-run leaves no qemu processes (AC-9)
- Integration: cargo test does not invoke qemu (AC-3)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] qemu-e2e.yml exists, only triggers on workflow_dispatch / qemu-e2e label / tag push (verify by inspecting workflow event filters)
- [ ] Workflow's smoke job passes within ~2 minutes
- [ ] Full matrix completes within 20 minutes (NFR target)
- [ ] README walks through adding a case TOML without Rust changes
- [ ] Deliberate-fail case produces target/qemu/results/<case>/<kernel>/ bundle with serial-log + agent-log + runner-log + tail of stdout/stderr
- [ ] No leaked qemu-system-x86_64 after success, Ctrl-C, or panic (verified)
- [ ] cargo test / cargo xtask test / cargo xtask demo-smoke do NOT spawn qemu-system-x86_64 (verified)

## Intra-epic deps
- T1, T2, T3, T4, T5 (everything)
acceptance_criteria: qemu-e2e.yml triggers only on workflow_dispatch+label+tag; smoke <=2min; full matrix <=20min; README sufficient for adding a case; failure-bundle produced; no leaks; default cargo paths do not invoke qemu

title: qemu-xtask: plan 08 scenarios 1/2/3 ported as QEMU cases
description:

title: qemu-xtask: multi-VM topology + socket-mesh networking
description:
Part of epic classic-sub. Plan: plans/09-qemu-e2e.md @ commit bcd0410.

`git show bcd0410:plans/09-qemu-e2e.md` (sections: Boot model networking, In-scope multi-VM cases, FR-6, Open question 4)

## Plan slice (excerpted)
> Multi-VM cases:
> - 3-VM topology mirroring plan 08's three nodes (CPU dev box + GPU host + CPU worker)
> - networked via QEMU socket transport (no host bridge needed)
>
> Networking:
> - Single-VM cases: user-mode SLIRP, no host privileges
> - Multi-VM cases: per-VM -netdev socket,... mesh, plus a small in-host bridge process if needed for >2 VMs
> - No tun/tap, no root

title: qemu-xtask: single-VM topology + first BPF + FUSE/v9fs cases
description:

title: Integration tests: pack/spread happy paths, atomic failure, coord-crash TTL recovery
description:
Part of epic classic-2ki. Plan: plans/07-placement-groups.md @ a7b6585.

## Reading first
- git show a7b6585:plans/07-placement-groups.md  §Testing plan, §Failure-mode analysis, §Acceptance criteria
- git show a7b6585:plans/04-spawn-pipeline.md  (single-spawn pipeline; in-process daemons used in integration tests)

## Excerpted design slice

### From plan §Testing plan / Integration (single host, in-process daemons)
> - it_pack_2_members_one_node — 1 in-proc daemon, 2-member PACK; both spawn.
> - it_spread_3_members_3_nodes — 3 in-proc daemons; 1 member per daemon.
> - it_atomic_failure_no_leaked_caps — 2 daemons; second configured to deny
>   GroupReserve. Submit fails: assert zero spawns and daemon-1's cap pool
>   back to original.
> - it_coord_crash_ttl_recovers — submit, kill coord between Ack and Commit,
>   wait reserve_ttl + 1 s: assert no held reservations, no spawns.

### From plan §Acceptance criteria covered by these tests
> AC-1: A 2-member PACK group with any(gpu) lands both members on the
> same node, observable via classic ps showing identical node_id.
> AC-2: A 3-member SPREAD group lands one member on each of three
> distinct nodes; node_ids are pairwise unique.
> AC-3: When phase 1 fails on any node, zero members of the group
> are spawned anywhere in the cluster.
> AC-4: When the coordinator is kill -9'd after phase-1 acks but
> before phase-2 commit, all daemons release their held caps within
> reserve_ttl + 2 s.

### From plan §Hardware-dependent
> Placement-algorithm tests are pure (synthetic ads). Multi-node e2e exercises
> plan 04's NVML cap path under reservation: real GPUs required. Mark e2e
> #[ignore], gate behind CLASSIC_E2E_GPU=1 in CI.

## Inputs
- All prior epic-2ki tasks complete: classic-place group types + PACK + SPREAD; classic-spawn group_proto + coord + reservation; classic-cli submit subcommand.
- Plan 04's in-process daemon harness for integration tests.

## Outputs
Files to create:
- /home/horde/src/classic/crates/classic-spawn/tests/it_groups.rs — integration tests using in-process daemons:
  - it_pack_2_members_one_node — start 1 in-proc daemon with 2 GPUs; submit a 2-member PACK group; assert both members spawn on the same daemon's NodeId; covers AC-1.
  - it_spread_3_members_3_nodes — start 3 in-proc daemons; submit a 3-member SPREAD group; assert each member lands on a distinct daemon NodeId; covers AC-2.
  - it_atomic_failure_no_leaked_caps — start 2 daemons; configure daemon-2 to GroupReserveDeny on receipt; submit a 2-member SPREAD group; assert submit_group returns ReserveDenied; assert zero spawned children on either daemon; assert daemon-1's free cap pool equals its initial pool (no leak); covers AC-3.
  - it_coord_crash_ttl_recovers — submit a 2-member group, capture phase-1 ack on both daemons, then drop the coord task before sending GroupCommit; wait reserve_ttl + 1 s; assert no live reservations on either daemon and no children spawned anywhere; covers AC-4.
- Optional /home/horde/src/classic/crates/classic-spawn/tests/e2e_groups.rs — multi-node e2e marked #[ignore], gated behind CLASSIC_E2E_GPU=1 env var.

Test infrastructure:
- Reuse plan 04's in-process daemon test harness (same as single-spawn integration tests).
- Synthetic NodeAd fixtures for placement-algorithm-only tests live in classic-place/tests; this task focuses on integration spanning both crates.
- Use a controllable test clock (or tokio::time::pause) for the TTL-recovery test so we don't sleep wall-clock 10 s.

## Test plan
- cargo test -p classic-spawn --test it_groups (the four integration tests above pass on Linux x86_64 without GPUs).
- cargo test --workspace (full suite stays green; no regressions to single-spawn tests).
- CLASSIC_E2E_GPU=1 cargo test -p classic-spawn -- --ignored (e2e tests, manually verified on a 2-host cluster).

## Task-specific Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] it_pack_2_members_one_node passes; covers AC-1.
- [ ] it_spread_3_members_3_nodes passes; covers AC-2.
- [ ] it_atomic_failure_no_leaked_caps passes; covers AC-3 (zero spawns on phase-1 failure + cap pool unchanged).
- [ ] it_coord_crash_ttl_recovers passes; covers AC-4 (TTL releases caps within reserve_ttl + 2 s after coord drop).
- [ ] cargo test --workspace green on Linux x86_64 (no GPU required).
- [ ] No new clippy warnings introduced (AC-8 from epic).
- [ ] cargo doc -p classic-place && cargo doc -p classic-spawn && cargo doc -p classic-cli build clean (AC-8 from epic).

## Intra-epic deps
- Depends on the CLI submit task (and transitively on every algorithm/orchestrator/reservation task in this epic).

title: classic-spawn: per-node reservation table + TTL sweeper + plan-04 spawn integration
description:
Part of epic classic-2ki. Plan: plans/07-placement-groups.md @ a7b6585.

## Reading first
- git show a7b6585:plans/07-placement-groups.md  §Per-node reservation table, §Failure-mode analysis (F3, F6, F7)
- git show a7b6585:plans/04-spawn-pipeline.md  (single-spawn local exec path; cap/cgroup machinery; ChildExit semantics)

## Excerpted design slice

### From plan §Per-node reservation table (classicd)
> struct Reservation {
>     group_id: GroupId,
>     members: Vec<ReservedMemberSlot>,    // device caps + slot accounting held
>     deadline: Instant,                   // now + reserve_ttl
>     state: ReservationState,             // Held | Committing | Released
> }
>
> - GroupReserve: revalidate every member's Requirement against current
>   state, attempt to reserve caps + slots. On success push into a
>   HashMap<GroupId, Reservation> and reply Ack. On failure (cap
>   exhausted, predicate no longer satisfied, slot limit) reply Deny with
>   reason.
> - GroupCommit: locate by GroupId, mark Committing, drive plan 04's
>   local-spawn path per member using the *already-reserved* caps. Reply
>   GroupSpawned per member as exec completes; drop the reservation entry
>   when all done.
> - GroupAbort: locate, release caps + slots, reply AbortAck. Idempotent
>   (release of Released is a no-op).
> - **TTL sweeper thread** wakes every 1 s; any reservation past deadline is
>   auto-released as if GroupAbort had arrived. This is the leak-prevention
>   net.

### From plan §Failure-mode analysis (excerpts)
> F3 — Coord crashes between GroupReserve send and ack/timeout. Each
> daemon's reservation simply ages out via the TTL sweeper after
> reserve_ttl_ms (~12 s including slack). **No leak.** This is the
> load-bearing reason every reservation has a TTL.
>
> F6 — Reservation expires *exactly* as GroupCommit arrives. Resolved at
> the daemon under a mutex: if state is Released, reply
> GroupCommitFailed { reason: "ttl expired" }; coord treats this like F4.
>
> F7 — Duplicate GroupReserve for the same GroupId on the same node.
> Should not happen (coord groups members by node), but if a buggy coord sends
> two: the second is rejected with Deny { reason: "duplicate group_id" }.

### From plan §Functional requirements
> FR-5: TTL sweeper releases any reservation older than reserve_ttl_ms
> even if coord never sends abort/commit.
> FR-6: GroupAbort is idempotent.

## Inputs
- Plan-04 single-spawn local exec path (cap reserve, cgroup setup, exec) — already exists in classic-spawn from another epic; this task calls into it from the commit handler with already-reserved caps.
- /home/horde/src/classic/crates/classic-spawn/src/group_proto.rs (from the coord task — frame payload structs).
- Plan 03 Requirement::matches for revalidation against current node ad.

## Outputs
Files to create:
- /home/horde/src/classic/crates/classic-spawn/src/reservation.rs — pub struct ReservationTable wrapping HashMap<GroupId, Reservation> behind Mutex; methods: reserve(GroupReserveFrame, &CurrentAd) -> Result<Vec<(label, token)>, DenyReason>, commit(GroupId, tokens) -> CommitFlow, abort(GroupId), tick_ttl(now: Instant) -> Vec<GroupId> (returns released groups for logging). Plus pub fn spawn_ttl_sweeper(table: Arc<ReservationTable>) returning a JoinHandle that wakes every 1 s and calls tick_ttl.
- ReservedMemberSlot struct describing a single held member's cap+slot snapshot (sufficient to drive plan-04 local-spawn during commit without re-resolving against the ad).

Files to modify:
- /home/horde/src/classic/crates/classic-spawn/src/lib.rs — re-export ReservationTable + spawn_ttl_sweeper for use by classic-node when wiring frame handlers.
- Frame handler dispatch for GroupReserve/GroupCommit/GroupAbort: revalidate Requirement on Reserve; on Commit transition Held -> Committing under mutex (rejects if Released or Committing); for each member drive plan-04's local-spawn path using the ReservedMemberSlot caps and stream GroupSpawned per member; drop entry when all members done. Abort releases caps+slots and replies AbortAck idempotently.
- F6 race: state check inside mutex; if Released, reply GroupCommitFailed { reason: "ttl expired" }.
- F7 duplicate detection: reserve() rejects if HashMap already contains the GroupId.

TTL sweeper: tokio task (or std::thread per project convention — match plan 04's pattern) wakes every 1 s, walks the table under mutex, releases entries with deadline <= now.

## Test plan
- /home/horde/src/classic/crates/classic-spawn/tests/reservation.rs (with a fake clock helper or tokio-test pause):
  - ttl_sweep_releases_held_caps — install reservation, advance clock past deadline, run tick_ttl, assert released; re-reserving the same caps succeeds.
  - commit_after_ttl_returns_failed — install reservation, sweep first to Released, then commit() returns CommitFailed { reason: "ttl expired" }.
  - abort_idempotent — abort same GroupId twice, both succeed; second is a no-op.
  - duplicate_reserve_rejected — second reserve() for same GroupId returns Deny { reason: "duplicate group_id" }.
  - reserve_revalidates_against_current_ad — predicate that no longer matches current ad state returns Deny.
  - commit_drives_plan04_per_member — mock plan-04 spawn path; assert one invocation per member with the reserved caps; assert GroupSpawned reply per member with the resulting NetId; assert reservation entry dropped when all done.
- cargo test -p classic-spawn reservation

## Task-specific Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ReservationTable supports reserve / commit / abort / tick_ttl behind a single mutex (no torn state).
- [ ] TTL sweeper auto-releases past-deadline entries even with no coord traffic (FR-5).
- [ ] GroupAbort is idempotent (FR-6).
- [ ] F6 race resolves correctly: commit-after-release returns CommitFailed { reason: "ttl expired" }.
- [ ] F7 race resolves correctly: duplicate reserve same GroupId returns Deny.
- [ ] Commit path drives plan-04's local-spawn per member using the already-reserved caps and emits GroupSpawned per member.
- [ ] All listed tests pass.

## Intra-epic deps
- Depends on the 2PC frames + coord task (frame payload definitions are shared between coord and node-side handlers).
- Plan 04's local-spawn path is owned by another epic; this task calls into it.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] ReservationTable reserve/commit/abort/tick_ttl single-mutex, no torn state.
- [ ] TTL sweeper auto-releases past-deadline entries (FR-5).
- [ ] GroupAbort idempotent (FR-6).
- [ ] F6 race: commit-after-release -> CommitFailed { reason: ttl expired }.
- [ ] F7: duplicate reserve same GroupId -> Deny.
- [ ] Commit drives plan-04 local-spawn per member + emits GroupSpawned.
- [ ] reservation tests pass.

title: Plan-05 integration tests: in-process roundtrip, two-node send, 3-node CRDT convergence, mbox-e2e harness
description:
End-to-end and convergence tests covering the full plan-05 surface: in-process roundtrip, two-node send, service declare/lookup across nodes, 3-node CRDT convergence, and the plan-05 acceptance script driver `mbox-e2e`. Existing per-task tests (in mbox/local_roundtrip/directory_crdt/gc) stay; this task adds the cross-cutting integration coverage and the AC harness.

## Plan slice (excerpted from plans/05-mailbox-service-directory.md @ commit a7b6585)

> ### End-to-end (two classicd processes on loopback)
> ```bash
> cargo build -p classic-node -p classic-cli
> ./target/debug/classicd --listen 127.0.0.1:7001 --peer 127.0.0.1:7002 &
> ./target/debug/classicd --listen 127.0.0.1:7002 --peer 127.0.0.1:7001 &
> ./target/debug/mbox-e2e declare 7001 coordinator
> ./target/debug/mbox-e2e wait-svc 7002 coordinator
> ./target/debug/mbox-e2e send 7002 coordinator "hello"
> ./target/debug/mbox-e2e expect 7001 coordinator "hello"
> ```
> mbox-e2e is a thin test binary added under `crates/classic-mbox/tests/bin/`.

> Acceptance criteria:
> - AC-2: two-node end-to-end script runs to completion, B's recv() returns b"hello" originally sent from A.
> - AC-3: When task A is killed (SIGKILL), node B observes coordinator disappear from service_lookup within 2 s.
> - AC-5: 3-node CRDT convergence test: three nodes each declare "coordinator" with different NetIds; all three converge to the identical 3-element service_lookup result.
> - AC-6: Every frame in 0x0200-0x02FF round-trips through classic-proto bincode (table-driven).

## Inputs

- Plan doc: `git show a7b6585:plans/05-mailbox-service-directory.md`
- All prereq tasks landed: primitives, local delivery, cross-node, directory, gossip, gc
- classicd binary from plan 01 (will need a tiny test-only RPC into the daemon to call mbox APIs from mbox-e2e — define a Unix-socket admin endpoint or extend an existing one). Coordinate with plan-01 epic if a hook is missing; if so, file a `discovered-from` issue rather than blocking on it.

## Outputs

- `crates/classic-mbox/tests/bin/mbox-e2e.rs` — test binary with subcommands `declare`, `wait-svc`, `send`, `expect`. Uses an admin RPC into classicd to invoke mbox APIs. (If admin RPC doesn't exist yet, build a minimal one inside classic-mbox / classic-node behind a `cfg(test_helper)` feature.)
