title: Auto-GC of services and mailboxes on task exit (on_task_start / on_task_exit hooks)
description:
Implement TaskGcTracker: on task exit, synthesize service_forget for every service the task declared and evict every mailbox the task owned. Expose `on_task_start(task_id)` / `on_task_exit(task_id)` that classic-spawn (plan 04) will call. The spawn-side wiring is owned by plan 04; this task only ships the callback API and its semantics.

## Plan slice (excerpted from plans/05-mailbox-service-directory.md @ commit a7b6585)

> classic-spawn (plan 04) calls into classic-mbox on lifecycle events:
> ```rust
> pub fn on_task_start(task_id: TaskId);
> pub fn on_task_exit(task_id: TaskId);  // synchronously cleans up
> ```
> on_task_exit walks by_task[task_id]: emit service_forget for each declared service, drop the registry entry for each owned MboxId. Tasks may also drop a ServiceHandle (RAII) to forget early. Both paths are idempotent.

> by_task: `HashMap<TaskId, Vec<(String, NetId)>>` — the directory's per-task back-index for declared services.
> The mailbox-owner back-index `task_id -> Vec<MboxId>` is added in this task (Mailbox::new captures the calling task_id from a task-local).

## Inputs

- Plan doc: `git show a7b6585:plans/05-mailbox-service-directory.md`
- Prereqs: mailbox primitives (MailboxRegistry, registry eviction), directory (ServiceDirectory.by_task already populated by service_declare path from gossip task)
- TaskId placeholder type — same one used in directory + gossip tasks; spawn integration in plan 04 will reconcile

## Outputs

- `crates/classic-mbox/src/gc.rs` — TaskGcTracker { mboxes: HashMap<TaskId, Vec<MboxId>>, ... }; `on_task_start(task_id)`; `on_task_exit(task_id)` — for each entry in directory.by_task[task_id], synthesize service_forget (which broadcasts ServiceForget via gossip); drop each MboxId from MailboxRegistry. Idempotent.
- `crates/classic-mbox/src/mbox.rs` (modify) — Mailbox::new captures the current task_id from the task-local set in on_task_start, recording the owned MboxId on the GcTracker.
- `crates/classic-mbox/src/lib.rs` — export `on_task_start`, `on_task_exit`.

## Test plan (this task) — `crates/classic-mbox/tests/gc.rs`

- Task A's on_task_start; declare 2 services; allocate 3 mailboxes; on_task_exit:
  - directory.service_lookup returns nothing for either name (or returns tombstones, depending on timing)
  - all 3 mboxes evicted from MailboxRegistry — subsequent local mail_send drops with counter increment
  - 2 outbound ServiceForget frames observed on the gossip channel
- on_task_exit called twice: idempotent, no duplicate ServiceForget emitted
- ServiceHandle::drop before on_task_exit: forget happens once; on_task_exit then has nothing left for that service

Run: `cargo test -p classic-mbox`

## Acceptance criteria (this task)

- [ ] on_task_start / on_task_exit public functions exist and are documented
- [ ] on_task_exit purges directory entries (via apply_local_forget + ServiceForget broadcast) and registry entries (via Mailbox eviction)
- [ ] Both call paths idempotent (per-task and ServiceHandle::drop overlap safely)
- [ ] gc.rs integration test passes
- [ ] cargo test -p classic-mbox passes
- [ ] cargo clippy clean for added code
- [ ] Note in description: classic-spawn-side wiring (calling on_task_start/on_task_exit at fork/exit) is tracked under plan 04's epic, NOT here
acceptance_criteria: on_task_start / on_task_exit public functions exist and are documented; on_task_exit purges directory entries via apply_local_forget + ServiceForget broadcast and registry entries via Mailbox eviction; both call paths idempotent; ServiceHandle::drop and on_task_exit overlap safely; gc.rs integration test passes; cargo test -p classic-mbox passes; cargo clippy clean. Note: classic-spawn-side wiring (calling on_task_start/on_task_exit at fork/exit) is tracked under plan 04's epic, NOT here.

title: Service gossip: ServiceAd/ServiceForget/ServiceSync/ServiceSyncResponse + on-connect sync + delta broadcast
description:
Implement the GossipEngine: handle inbound 0x02xx service frames, emit outbound deltas on local declare/forget, perform on-connect ServiceSync exchange. Wire up the public service_declare / service_forget API that produces ServiceHandle.

## Plan slice (excerpted from plans/05-mailbox-service-directory.md @ commit a7b6585)

> Gossip rides the same per-peer connection plan 01 sets up. 0x0200-0x02FF frames are demuxed by classic-proto and fed into GossipEngine.
>
> - On connect: the new side sends ServiceSync immediately after Hello. The peer replies with ServiceSyncResponse { entries } containing every non-tombstoned entry it knows. Both sides then switch to delta mode.
> - On local declare/forget: broadcast a single ServiceAd / ServiceForget to every connected peer. No batching in v1.
> - On receiving a delta: apply Lamport rules; do NOT re-broadcast — the originator already flooded all peers. (Assumes full-mesh per ARCHITECTURE.md.)

Public API:
```rust
pub struct ServiceHandle { /* Drop calls service_forget */ }
pub fn service_declare(name: &str) -> Result<ServiceHandle, ServiceError>;
pub fn service_forget(name: &str);  // idempotent
```

`service_declare` currently has no NetId argument because the implementation derives the (NetId) from the calling task's primary mailbox per the worked example. Capture this as: `service_declare` takes the caller's owned MboxId via a thread-local or task-local context set at task start. This task can stub the task-local with an init-time setter (`set_current_task(task_id, primary_mbox_id)`) until plan 04's spawn integration lands.

## Inputs

- Plan doc: `git show a7b6585:plans/05-mailbox-service-directory.md`
- Prereqs: cross-node delivery task (frames.rs has ServiceAd, ServiceForget, ServiceSync, ServiceSyncResponse defined and registered with classic-proto); directory task (ServiceDirectory + apply_local_* + apply_remote_*)
- classic-proto: ConnectionRegistry, per-peer connection-up/down hooks, FrameMux high-byte handler

## Outputs

- `crates/classic-mbox/src/gossip.rs` — GossipEngine actor; subscribes to connection-up events, sends ServiceSync; handles ServiceSyncResponse, ServiceAd, ServiceForget. On local declare/forget, broadcasts the corresponding frame to every connected peer.
- `crates/classic-mbox/src/lib.rs` — export `service_declare`, `service_forget`, `ServiceHandle`. `service_declare` captures (task_id, net_id), calls `directory.apply_local_declare`, broadcasts ServiceAd, returns ServiceHandle. ServiceHandle::drop calls `service_forget` for the (name, net_id).
- TaskGcTracker hook stubs added (`on_task_start`, `on_task_exit`) — the GC implementation lands in the GC task; here we just register declared services on the by_task index.

## Test plan (this task)

- Unit (`gossip.rs`): with a mock ConnectionRegistry + frame channel, on local service_declare a ServiceAd is broadcast to every connected peer; on local service_forget a ServiceForget is broadcast.
- Integration: simulated two-peer scenario in-process — peer A declares "coordinator"; after one gossip round, peer B's directory has the entry and service_lookup returns A's NetId.
- On-connect sync: peer A has 5 services; peer B comes up; A receives ServiceSync, replies with ServiceSyncResponse{5 entries}; B's directory has all 5.
- Inbound delta is NOT re-broadcast (no echo); verified by message counter on outbound channels.
- ServiceHandle::drop emits ServiceForget once; lookup returns empty.

Run: `cargo test -p classic-mbox`

## Acceptance criteria (this task)

- [ ] service_declare / service_forget / ServiceHandle public API works
- [ ] On-connect ServiceSync / ServiceSyncResponse exchange converges directories
- [ ] Local declare/forget broadcasts to every connected peer
- [ ] Inbound delta applied via apply_remote_*; NOT re-broadcast
- [ ] Plan AC-7 holds: classic-mbox emits no frame kinds outside 0x0200-0x02FF (debug assertion in GossipEngine)
- [ ] cargo test -p classic-mbox passes
- [ ] cargo clippy clean for added code
acceptance_criteria: service_declare / service_forget / ServiceHandle public API works; on-connect ServiceSync / ServiceSyncResponse exchange converges directories; local declare/forget broadcasts to every connected peer; inbound deltas applied via apply_remote_* and NOT re-broadcast; classic-mbox emits no frame kinds outside 0x0200-0x02FF (debug assertion in GossipEngine); cargo test -p classic-mbox passes; cargo clippy clean.

title: ServiceDirectory data structure + Lamport last-writer-wins CRDT rules
description:
Implement the ServiceDirectory data structure with Lamport-clock last-writer-wins CRDT semantics. In-process only — gossip / wire emission lands in a separate task.

## Plan slice (excerpted from plans/05-mailbox-service-directory.md @ commit a7b6585)

> ```rust
> type Lamport = u64;
> #[derive(Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Ord, PartialOrd)]
> pub struct ServiceEntry {
>     pub net_id: NetId,
>     pub lamport: Lamport,
>     pub tombstone: bool,
>     pub last_seen: Instant,
> }
> pub struct ServiceDirectory {
>     inner: HashMap<String, BTreeSet<ServiceEntry>>,
>     by_task: HashMap<TaskId, Vec<(String, NetId)>>,
>     local_clock: AtomicU64,
> }
> ```

> Lookup semantics:
> - service_lookup(name) returns all live (tombstone == false) NetIds.
> - service_lookup_one(name) returns one chosen via round-robin per process (a per-name AtomicUsize cursor).

> Lamport ordering rules. The directory is a last-writer-wins CRDT keyed by (name, net_id):
> 1. Local service_declare(name): bump local_clock; insert/replace ServiceEntry { net_id, lamport, tombstone: false }; (broadcast handled in gossip task).
> 2. Local service_forget(name): bump local_clock; replace with tombstone: true (keep TOMBSTONE_TTL = 60 s); (broadcast handled in gossip task).
> 3. Inbound ServiceAd: local_clock = max(local_clock, lamport) + 1. If existing lamport >= incoming.lamport, drop. Else replace.
> 4. Inbound ServiceForget: same comparison, result is a tombstone.
> 5. Tombstones older than TOMBSTONE_TTL may be GC'd; a strictly-later ad after GC is accepted.
> 6. Ties on lamport: break by net_id (node then mbox, lexicographic).
>
> Single-writer-per-key holds: only the node that owns net_id.node ever writes that key.

Public API to add (lookup side; declare/forget land in gossip task because they emit frames):

```rust
pub fn service_lookup(name: &str) -> Vec<NetId>;          // all live
pub fn service_lookup_one(name: &str) -> Option<NetId>;   // round-robin
```

Plus internal mutation methods consumed by both local declare/forget and inbound delta application: `apply_local_declare(task_id, name, net_id) -> (lamport, was_changed)`, `apply_local_forget(task_id, name, net_id) -> (lamport, was_changed)`, `apply_remote_ad(name, net_id, lamport) -> bool`, `apply_remote_forget(name, net_id, lamport) -> bool`. The booleans tell the gossip layer whether to emit / re-broadcast (we don't re-broadcast inbound, so it's just a debug signal).

## Inputs

- Plan doc: `git show a7b6585:plans/05-mailbox-service-directory.md`
- Upstream types: `NetId`, `NodeId`, `MboxId` from classic-proto
- TaskId — a placeholder type (u64 or UUID) for now; will be reconciled with classic-spawn's TaskId in plan 04 integration
- Mailbox primitives task (prereq) — registry exists so directory shares the daemon's identity hooks

## Outputs

- `crates/classic-mbox/src/directory.rs` — ServiceEntry, ServiceDirectory, Lamport rules, by_task back-index, round-robin AtomicUsize per name, tombstone TTL constant `TOMBSTONE_TTL = Duration::from_secs(60)`.
- `crates/classic-mbox/src/lib.rs` — export `service_lookup`, `service_lookup_one`, `ServiceError` (NameTooLong with `MAX_SVC_NAME = 256`).
- `crates/classic-mbox/src/error.rs` — extend with ServiceError.

This task does NOT yet implement public `service_declare` / `service_forget` or any frame emission; those land in the gossip task and will call into `apply_local_*`.

## Test plan (this task) — `crates/classic-mbox/tests/directory_crdt.rs`

- Lamport insert/replace/tombstone monotonicity
- Concurrent declare from two simulated nodes for distinct (name, net_id) pairs => coexist (multimap)
- Same key (name, net_id) updated with new lamport => latest wins; older lamport dropped
- Tombstone hides entry from service_lookup; later (greater lamport) ad un-hides
- Tombstone-TTL: stale tombstone GC'd, then later-arriving stale ad with old lamport still rejected vs. brand-new ad accepted
- Tie-breaking: equal lamport, two NetIds => deterministic by (node, mbox) lex order
- service_lookup_one round-robins fairly across N live NetIds for the same name
- Name length: > MAX_SVC_NAME (256 B UTF-8) => Err(ServiceError::NameTooLong)

Run: `cargo test -p classic-mbox`

## Acceptance criteria (this task)

- [ ] ServiceDirectory + Lamport CRDT rules implemented per plan
- [ ] service_lookup / service_lookup_one return correct results from in-memory state (no gossip yet)
- [ ] Tombstones, TTL GC, tie-breaking all covered by unit + integration tests
- [ ] cargo test -p classic-mbox passes
- [ ] cargo clippy clean for added code
acceptance_criteria: ServiceDirectory + Lamport CRDT rules implemented per plan; service_lookup / service_lookup_one return correct in-memory state (no gossip yet); tombstones, TOMBSTONE_TTL=60s GC, tie-breaking (NetId lex order on lamport ties), MAX_SVC_NAME=256 enforced; concurrent declares, replace-if-greater-lamport, round-robin lookup_one all covered by directory_crdt.rs integration tests; cargo test -p classic-mbox passes; cargo clippy clean.

title: Cross-node mailbox delivery: MailSend / MailDeliveryFailure frames + 0x0200-0x02FF wire types
description:
Implement cross-node mailbox delivery: serialize MailSend / MailDeliveryFailure frames (range 0x0200, 0x0201) and dispatch them over the live peer connection from classic-proto's connection registry. Define all 0x0200-0x02FF wire types in `frames.rs` (the service-* frames are used in later tasks but co-located here).

## Plan slice (excerpted from plans/05-mailbox-service-directory.md @ commit a7b6585)

> Remote: look up the connection for net_id.node (classic-proto connection registry). No live connection => warn-log, drop. Otherwise encode and send a MailSend frame.
>
> Receiving MailSend: look up to.mbox locally. Missing/full => optionally send MailDeliveryFailure back to from.node (also best-effort). The sender treats absence of failure as inconclusive — no positive acks.

> Frame definitions (0x0200-0x02FF), all serialized with bincode v2, fixed-int, little-endian:
>   MailSend            = 0x0200
>   MailDeliveryFailure = 0x0201
>   ServiceAd           = 0x0210
>   ServiceForget       = 0x0211
>   ServiceSync         = 0x0212
>   ServiceSyncResponse = 0x0213
>
> ```rust
> pub struct MailSend { pub from: NetId, pub to: NetId, pub payload: Vec<u8> } // payload <= 8 MiB
> pub enum DeliveryFailureReason { UnknownMbox, MboxFull, NodeUnreachable }
> pub struct MailDeliveryFailure { pub to: NetId, pub reason: DeliveryFailureReason }
> pub struct ServiceAd     { pub name: String, pub net_id: NetId, pub lamport: u64 } // name <= 256 B UTF-8
> pub struct ServiceForget { pub name: String, pub net_id: NetId, pub lamport: u64 }
> pub struct ServiceSync;
> pub struct ServiceSyncEntry { pub name: String, pub net_id: NetId, pub lamport: u64, pub tombstone: bool }
> pub struct ServiceSyncResponse { pub entries: Vec<ServiceSyncEntry> }
> ```

## Inputs

- Plan doc: `git show a7b6585:plans/05-mailbox-service-directory.md`
- classic-proto (plan 01): Frame { kind: u16, payload: Vec<u8> }, FrameKind enum (extend), `ConnectionRegistry` trait/struct, FrameMux high-byte handler registration (one slot per range)
- Mailbox primitives + local delivery (prereq tasks): MailboxRegistry, mail_send local branch
- Upstream types from classic-proto: NetId, NodeId, MboxId
- bincode v2, fixed-int LE, per ARCHITECTURE.md

## Outputs

- `crates/classic-mbox/src/frames.rs` — bincode-serialized structs/enums for all 0x0200-0x02FF frame kinds (MailSend, MailDeliveryFailure, ServiceAd, ServiceForget, ServiceSync, ServiceSyncEntry, ServiceSyncResponse). Add `MboxFrameKind: u16` enum constants.
- `crates/classic-proto/src/lib.rs` (modify) — register MailSend=0x0200, MailDeliveryFailure=0x0201, ServiceAd=0x0210, ServiceForget=0x0211, ServiceSync=0x0212, ServiceSyncResponse=0x0213 in the FrameKind catalog.
- `crates/classic-mbox/src/send.rs` — fill in the remote branch: look up connection by net_id.node, encode MailSend, write frame; no live connection => warn + drop. Keep Ok(()) semantics on the API.
- `crates/classic-mbox/src/lib.rs` — register the 0x02 high-byte FrameHandler with classic-proto's FrameMux; inbound MailSend handler dispatches to local MailboxRegistry (reuse local-delivery code from prereq); missing/full mbox => optional MailDeliveryFailure back to from.node.
- Bound: MailSend.payload <= 8 MiB enforced on encode (Err(MailError::PayloadTooLarge)) and on decode (drop frame + warn).

## Test plan (this task)

- Unit (`frames.rs`): bincode round-trip for every 0x0200-0x02FF frame type (table-driven). Oversize MailSend payload (>8 MiB) rejected on encode.
- Unit (`send.rs`): mock ConnectionRegistry; mail_send to a remote NetId writes a properly-encoded MailSend frame onto the channel; with no live conn => Ok(()) + tracing::warn + drop counter increments.
- Two-node integration (mocked transport, in-process): encode frame on side A, decode on side B's FrameHandler, observe local delivery via the receiver's mailbox.

Run: `cargo test -p classic-mbox && cargo test -p classic-proto`

## Acceptance criteria (this task)

- [ ] All 0x0200-0x02FF frame kinds defined in classic-mbox::frames and registered in classic-proto::FrameKind
- [ ] Bincode v2 fixed-int LE round-trip works for every frame; oversize MailSend rejected
- [ ] Cross-node mail_send writes MailSend on the live conn and is dropped (with warn) when no conn exists
- [ ] Inbound MailSend handler delivers to local registry and emits optional MailDeliveryFailure on miss/full
- [ ] cargo test -p classic-mbox and -p classic-proto pass
- [ ] cargo clippy clean for added code
acceptance_criteria: All 0x0200-0x02FF frame kinds defined in classic-mbox::frames and registered in classic-proto::FrameKind; bincode v2 fixed-int LE round-trip works for every frame; oversize MailSend rejected on encode/decode; remote mail_send writes MailSend on the live conn (warn-drop when none); inbound MailSend handler delivers locally and emits optional MailDeliveryFailure on miss/full; cargo test -p classic-mbox and -p classic-proto pass; cargo clippy clean.

title: classic-fs: integration tests (9pfuse interop, native client, in_use freshness)
description:
Integration tests proving the 9P namespace server works end-to-end. 9pfuse interop, native rust 9P client round-trip, FUSE smoke, /dev/gpu/0/in_use freshness vs. CapBroker state, frame-range audit.

== Plan slice (excerpt from plans/06-9p-namespace-server.md @ a7b6585) ==

Integration tests:
- 9pfuse interop (tests/interop_9pfuse.rs): pipes 9P bytes between 9pfuse and the in-process server over a Unix socket; asserts ls, cat, readlink outputs. Skipped if which 9pfuse fails.
- Rust-native 9P client (tests/native_client.rs): drives every implemented op via classic-fs::client against the local server. The authoritative regression test -- no system packages.
- FUSE smoke (tests/fuse_smoke.rs): mounts the in-process server through FUSE at a tempdir; runs ls, cat, stat, readlink. Gated by CLASSIC_FUSE_TESTS=1.
- Two-node bind-remote (crates/classic-node/tests/fs_bind_remote.rs): two classicd instances in one process; spawn fake task on A with --bind-remote node=<B> at=/cluster/B; assert FUSE mount shows B's /dev/gpu/0/vram_free_mb. (Note: this also lives in the remote-bind task; here we focus on the FS-side integration assertions; coordinate with that task.)

End-to-end manual smoke (documented):
classic spawn --bind-remote node=<B-hex> at=/cluster/B -- /bin/sh -c 'ls /cluster/B/dev/gpu && cat /cluster/B/dev/gpu/0/vram_free_mb'

Hardware-dependent: gated and skipped (not failed) when absent: fuse_smoke.rs needs /dev/fuse; interop_9pfuse.rs needs 9pfuse on PATH.

Acceptance criteria addressed:
- AC-2: 9pfuse mounts the local server and ls /mnt/classic shows dev, node, proc, svc.
- AC-3: Native-client test passes every implemented 9P op and every rejected op returns the documented errno.
- AC-4: FUSE smoke test passes with CLASSIC_FUSE_TESTS=1 on Linux 6.1+ x86_64.
- AC-6: cat /dev/gpu/0/in_use reflects a capability acquired via classic-cap within 1 read of the acquisition.
- AC-7: Frame-range audit (grep -n 0x04 across the workspace) shows only classic-fs emits 0x04xx, and only 0x0400/0x0401 are used.

== Inputs ==
- Depends on: codec, server, tree, namespace+FUSE, remote-bind tasks (all five).
- classic-cap::Broker (read-only consumer).
- 9pfuse from diod project (https://github.com/chaos/diod) -- skip if not on PATH.
- Read: plan 04 (CapBroker contract for in_use freshness).

== Outputs ==
- crates/classic-fs/tests/interop_9pfuse.rs: pipes 9P over Unix socket between 9pfuse subprocess and in-process server; asserts ls, cat, readlink. Skipped if which 9pfuse fails.
- crates/classic-fs/tests/native_client.rs: drives every implemented op via classic-fs::client against an in-process LocalServer. Authoritative regression test, no system packages. Also covers every rejected op returns documented errno (AC-3).
- crates/classic-fs/tests/in_use_freshness.rs: acquire a fake capability via classic-cap mock; immediately read /dev/gpu/0/in_use; assert the read reflects the new state without any cache delay (AC-6).
- crates/classic-fs/tests/fuse_smoke.rs: extended with end-to-end ls/cat/stat/readlink (CLASSIC_FUSE_TESTS=1 gate).
- Frame-range audit script/test: grep -n '0x04' across workspace; assert only classic-fs and only 0x0400/0x0401 (AC-7). Could be a CI script under scripts/audit_frame_kinds.sh and a test that invokes it.

== Test plan ==
- Run all integration tests with cargo test -p classic-fs --tests.
- 9pfuse interop: skip-not-fail if 9pfuse not on PATH; on dev box with 9pfuse installed, must pass.
- FUSE smoke: gated by CLASSIC_FUSE_TESTS=1 env var; required on Linux 6.1+ x86_64 dev hosts.
- in_use freshness: pure logic test, runs everywhere.
- Frame-range audit: runs everywhere; fails if a 0x04xx kind appears outside classic-fs or any 0x04xx other than 0x0400/0x0401 is emitted.

== Acceptance ==
- native_client.rs covers every implemented op (Tversion, Tattach, Twalk, Tlopen, Tread, Treaddir, Tgetattr, Treadlink, Tflush, Tclunk, Tfsync) and every rejected op (errno per plan table). AC-3 satisfied.
- interop_9pfuse.rs passes when 9pfuse is on PATH; otherwise skips. AC-2 satisfied on dev hosts.
- fuse_smoke.rs passes with CLASSIC_FUSE_TESTS=1 on Linux 6.1+ x86_64. AC-4 satisfied.
- in_use_freshness.rs proves no stale-cache window between Broker change and /dev/gpu/<i>/in_use read. AC-6 satisfied.
- Frame-range audit confirms classic-fs is the only emitter of 0x04xx and only 0x0400/0x0401 are used. AC-7 satisfied.
acceptance_criteria: native_client.rs covers all implemented and rejected ops; interop_9pfuse.rs runs (skip if absent); fuse_smoke.rs passes under CLASSIC_FUSE_TESTS=1; in_use_freshness.rs proves no stale window; frame-range audit confirms 0x04xx ownership; AC-2/3/4/6/7 satisfied.

title: Local-mailbox delivery via tokio channels (in-process mail_send)
description:
Implement local-mailbox delivery: the in-process path of `mail_send` that delivers via the MailboxRegistry's tokio channels when net_id.node == self_node_id. No wire / cross-node code in this task.

## Plan slice (excerpted from plans/05-mailbox-service-directory.md @ commit a7b6585)

> Local: mail_send checks net_id.node == self_node_id. If so, look up MboxId and try_send on the channel. Full or missing => drop.

> Fire-and-forget means: Ok(()) means handed off (enqueued / written to socket), NOT that the peer received it. Errors only on local validation.

> Failure model:
> | Happy path                            | Ok(())         | message       |
> | Local channel full                    | drop, log warn | nothing       |
> | Receiver mbox channel full            | optional MailDeliveryFailure | nothing |

> Payloads > MAX_MAIL_BYTES (8 MiB) => Err(MailError::PayloadTooLarge).

- Wire to MailboxRegistry from previous task — `try_send` on the inner mpsc; full/missing => drop + tracing::warn (count via debug counter for tests).

- Send to a NetId whose mbox was dropped: Ok(()) returned, no panic, drop counter increments

title: classic-spawn + classic-proto: 2PC group frames 0x0320..0x0327 + coord orchestrator
description:
Part of epic classic-2ki. Plan: plans/07-placement-groups.md @ a7b6585.

## Reading first
- git show a7b6585:plans/07-placement-groups.md  §Wire format additions, §Two-phase commit orchestrator, §Failure-mode analysis
- git show a7b6585:plans/ARCHITECTURE.md  (frame ranges; classic-spawn owns 0x0300-0x03FF)
- git show a7b6585:plans/04-spawn-pipeline.md  (single-spawn frames 0x0300-0x030F; cap/cgroup machinery; Kill frame)

## Excerpted design slice

### From plan §Wire format additions
> Plan 04 owns 0x0300..=0x030F for single-spawn and reserves
> 0x0310..=0x031F for future single-spawn extensions. **Group frames
> allocate 0x0320..=0x0327** within the same classic-spawn band.
>
> | Kind   | Name                | Direction         | Purpose                                             |
> |--------|---------------------|-------------------|-----------------------------------------------------|
> | 0x0320 | GroupReserve        | coord -> node     | Reserve caps + slots for members assigned here      |
> | 0x0321 | GroupReserveAck     | node -> coord     | Reservation accepted; held until commit/abort/TTL   |
> | 0x0322 | GroupReserveDeny    | node -> coord     | Reservation refused (cap exhausted, etc.)           |
> | 0x0323 | GroupCommit         | coord -> node     | Promote reservations -> live spawns                 |
> | 0x0324 | GroupSpawned        | node -> coord     | Per member: plan 04 spawn succeeded; carries NetId  |
> | 0x0325 | GroupCommitFailed   | node -> coord     | Phase-2 exec failure on this node                   |
> | 0x0326 | GroupAbort          | coord -> node     | Drop reservations (idempotent)                      |
> | 0x0327 | GroupAbortAck       | node -> coord     | Reservations released                               |
>
> GroupReserve payload (bincode v2, fixed-int LE):
> struct GroupReserveFrame {
>     group_id: GroupId,
>     members: Vec<ReservedMember>,    // members assigned to *this* node
>     reserve_ttl_ms: u32,             // node-side timer; coord adds slack
> }
> struct ReservedMember {
>     label: String,
>     req: Requirement,                // node revalidates locally
>     argv: Vec<String>,
>     env: Vec<(String, String)>,
> }
>
> GroupReserveAck carries (GroupId, Vec<(label, /*reservation_token*/ u64)>).
> The token is opaque; coord echoes it in GroupCommit.

### From plan §Two-phase commit orchestrator
> fn submit_group(group, ads, cfg):
>     1. assignment = place_group(group, ads)?
>     2. group_id  = random GroupId
>     3. by_node   = group_by_node(assignment)            // node -> Vec<member>
>     4. // Phase 1 — reserve in parallel
>        results = join_all(by_node.map(|(node, members)|
>          send GroupReserve { group_id, members, ttl=cfg.reserve_timeout+slack };
>          await Ack | Deny | timeout(cfg.reserve_timeout)))
>        if any Deny or timeout:
>          best-effort GroupAbort to every node that did Ack
>          (nodes that didn't Ack will TTL-sweep their own reservation);
>          return ReserveDenied / ReserveTimeout
>     5. // Phase 2 — commit in parallel
>        results = join_all_with_timeout(GroupCommit per node, cfg.commit_timeout)
>        if all GroupSpawned:
>          return Ok(GroupSubmitResult { group_id, members })
>        else:
>          best-effort Kill (plan 04 frame) to already-spawned NetIds;
>          GroupAbort to nodes whose commit hadn't returned;
>          return CommitFailed { node, reason }

### From plan §Data shapes / classic-spawn
> pub struct GroupId(pub [u8; 16]);
> pub enum GroupSpawnError {
>     Place(#[from] GroupPlaceError),
>     ReserveDenied { node: NodeId, reason: String },
>     ReserveTimeout { node: NodeId, ms: u64 },
>     CommitFailed { node: NodeId, reason: String },
>     Transport(String),
> }
> pub struct GroupSubmitResult { group_id: GroupId, members: Vec<(String, NetId)> }
> pub struct GroupCfg { reserve_timeout: Duration, commit_timeout: Duration }
> pub async fn submit_group(group: &PlacementGroup, ads: &[NodeAd], cfg: &GroupCfg)
>     -> Result<GroupSubmitResult, GroupSpawnError>;

## Inputs
- /home/horde/src/classic/crates/classic-proto/src/frame.rs (extend FrameKind enum with 0x0320..=0x0327; bincode v2 fixed-int LE encoding already established)
- /home/horde/src/classic/crates/classic-spawn/ (extend; depends on classic-place::{PlacementGroup, place_group, GroupPlaceError}, classic-proto::{NodeId, NetId, FrameKind})
- Plan 04's Kill frame for use in F4 cleanup
- Plan 03's Requirement type (used in GroupReserve payload)

## Outputs
Files to create:
- /home/horde/src/classic/crates/classic-spawn/src/group_proto.rs — payload structs for all 8 group frames + bincode encode/decode + GroupId([u8;16]) with random generation helper.
- /home/horde/src/classic/crates/classic-spawn/src/coord.rs — pub async fn submit_group(group, ads, cfg) -> Result<GroupSubmitResult, GroupSpawnError>; orchestrator drives place_group -> phase 1 (parallel GroupReserve with reserve_timeout) -> phase 2 (parallel GroupCommit with commit_timeout) -> success or compensating Abort/Kill on failure.

Files to modify:
- /home/horde/src/classic/crates/classic-proto/src/frame.rs — register FrameKind variants GroupReserve=0x0320, GroupReserveAck=0x0321, GroupReserveDeny=0x0322, GroupCommit=0x0323, GroupSpawned=0x0324, GroupCommitFailed=0x0325, GroupAbort=0x0326, GroupAbortAck=0x0327.
- /home/horde/src/classic/crates/classic-spawn/src/lib.rs — re-export GroupId, GroupCfg, GroupSpawnError, GroupSubmitResult, submit_group.

Phase-1 logic: group assignment by node; in parallel send GroupReserve { group_id, members_for_node, reserve_ttl_ms = (cfg.reserve_timeout + slack).as_millis() } to each node; collect Ack/Deny/timeout(cfg.reserve_timeout). If any node Denies or times out, send GroupAbort best-effort to acked nodes (others TTL-sweep) and return ReserveDenied { node, reason } or ReserveTimeout { node, ms }.

Phase-2 logic: parallel GroupCommit { group_id, tokens_per_member } honoring reservation tokens; collect GroupSpawned (per-member NetId stream) or GroupCommitFailed within cfg.commit_timeout. On all-success aggregate (label, NetId) pairs and return GroupSubmitResult. On any commit failure: Kill (plan 04 frame) all already-spawned NetIds best-effort + GroupAbort to nodes whose commit hadn't completed + return CommitFailed { node, reason }.

## Test plan
- /home/horde/src/classic/crates/classic-spawn/tests/group_proto.rs — bincode round-trip per frame payload.
- /home/horde/src/classic/crates/classic-spawn/tests/coord.rs (with a fake transport / mock NodeLink):
  - reserve_timeout_aborts_others — 3 nodes, node 2 silent. After cfg.reserve_timeout fires, coord emits GroupAbort to nodes 1 and 3 and returns ReserveTimeout { node: node2, ms: cfg.reserve_timeout }.
  - reserve_deny_aborts_others — node 2 sends GroupReserveDeny. Coord aborts node 1 and returns ReserveDenied { node: node2, reason }.
  - commit_failure_kills_spawned — phase 1 ok; node 2 returns GroupCommitFailed; coord sends Kill to nodes 1's and 3's spawned NetIds and returns CommitFailed { node: node2, reason }.
  - happy_path_returns_all_netids — all phase-1 ack, all phase-2 spawn; result members.len() == group.members.len() and labels are caller's order.
- /home/horde/src/classic/crates/classic-proto/tests/frame_kinds.rs — assert FrameKind values 0x0320..=0x0327 round-trip.
- cargo test -p classic-spawn ; cargo test -p classic-proto

## Task-specific Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] FrameKind enum has the 8 group variants with the exact kind numbers specified.
- [ ] GroupReserveFrame, GroupReserveAck, GroupReserveDeny, GroupCommit, GroupSpawned, GroupCommitFailed, GroupAbort, GroupAbortAck payload structs encode/decode round-trip via bincode v2 fixed-int LE.
- [ ] submit_group orchestrator runs phase 1 in parallel, applies reserve_timeout, and emits compensating GroupAbort on Deny/timeout.
- [ ] submit_group orchestrator runs phase 2 in parallel, applies commit_timeout, and emits Kill (plan 04) + GroupAbort on commit failure.
- [ ] All listed tests pass.
- [ ] No frame kinds outside 0x0320..=0x0327 are added (no poaching of plan 04's range).

## Intra-epic deps
- Depends on PACK and SPREAD tasks (place_group must work end-to-end before submit_group can be exercised).
- Plan 04 frames (Kill, plan-04 spawn pipeline) are owned by another epic; this task assumes those exist.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] FrameKind enum has 0x0320..=0x0327 variants with exact kind numbers.
- [ ] All 8 group frame payloads bincode round-trip.
- [ ] submit_group phase 1 parallel + reserve_timeout + compensating Abort on Deny/timeout.
- [ ] submit_group phase 2 parallel + commit_timeout + Kill + Abort on failure.
- [ ] coord tests pass.
- [ ] No poaching outside 0x0320..=0x0327.

title: Mailbox primitives: MboxId allocation, Mailbox::new, MailboxRecv, select_mboxes
description:
Implement the in-process mailbox primitives in the new classic-mbox crate. This is the foundation everything else in plan 05 builds on.

## Plan slice (excerpted from plans/05-mailbox-service-directory.md @ commit a7b6585)

- MailboxRegistry: `HashMap<MboxId, tokio::mpsc::Sender<Vec<u8>>>`. The receiving half (MailboxRecv) holds the corresponding Receiver; its Drop evicts the entry.
- Allocation: MboxId comes from a per-daemon `AtomicU64` starting at 1; mbox 0 is reserved (ARCHITECTURE.md). IDs never reuse within a daemon's lifetime; restart resets the counter.
- Eviction: MailboxRecv::drop removes its registry entry. Subsequent local sends drop silently.
- Bounded channels: capacity MBOX_CAPACITY = 1024. Full => message dropped. This is the fire-and-forget enforcement point — no sender-side backpressure.

Public API to define here:

```rust
pub struct Mailbox;
impl Mailbox {
    /// Fresh mailbox; MboxId unique for this daemon's lifetime, never 0.
    pub fn new() -> (MboxId, MailboxRecv);
}
pub struct MailboxRecv { /* Drop evicts registry entry */ }
impl MailboxRecv {
    pub async fn recv(&mut self) -> Option<Vec<u8>>;
    pub fn try_recv(&mut self) -> Option<Vec<u8>>;
}
pub async fn select_mboxes(mboxes: &mut [&mut MailboxRecv]) -> usize;
```

## Inputs

- Plan doc: `git show a7b6585:plans/05-mailbox-service-directory.md` (full design)
- Foundations: `plans/ARCHITECTURE.md` (NetId, NodeId, MboxId types live in classic-proto)
- Upstream types from classic-proto (plan 01): `MboxId(pub u64)`, `NodeId([u8;16])`, `NetId { node, mbox }`
- Existing crate stub: `crates/classic-mbox/` (created in plan 01 as empty)

## Outputs

- `crates/classic-mbox/Cargo.toml` — depend on classic-proto, tokio (mpsc, sync), tracing
- `crates/classic-mbox/src/lib.rs` — public re-exports of Mailbox, MailboxRecv, select_mboxes, MailError
- `crates/classic-mbox/src/mbox.rs` — Mailbox::new, MailboxRecv (with Drop), MailboxRegistry (process-singleton: OnceLock<MailboxRegistry>), AtomicU64 allocator starting at 1
- `crates/classic-mbox/src/error.rs` — MailError enum (placeholder; populated in later tasks)
- Constants: `MBOX_CAPACITY = 1024`

## Test plan (this task)

Unit tests in `crates/classic-mbox/src/mbox.rs`:
- Allocator never returns 0; monotonic; no reuse across many allocations
- Drop of MailboxRecv evicts the registry entry (debug counter or test-only inspector)
- Bounded channel: filling beyond capacity drops messages (sender returns Ok or sees the drop signal — fire-and-forget semantics)
- select_mboxes: among 3 mailboxes, the one fed first wakes and returns its index

Run: `cargo test -p classic-mbox`

## Acceptance criteria (this task)

- [ ] crates/classic-mbox/{Cargo.toml,src/lib.rs,src/mbox.rs,src/error.rs} exist and compile
- [ ] cargo test -p classic-mbox passes (unit tests above)
- [ ] No frame-level / wire / network code in this task — purely in-process primitives
- [ ] cargo clippy -p classic-mbox -- -D warnings clean for the code added
- [ ] Public items have rustdoc
acceptance_criteria: crates/classic-mbox/{Cargo.toml,src/lib.rs,src/mbox.rs,src/error.rs} exist and compile; cargo test -p classic-mbox passes; allocator never returns 0 and is monotonic; MailboxRecv::drop evicts the registry entry; select_mboxes returns the index of the first ready mailbox; bounded MBOX_CAPACITY=1024 channel drops on full; cargo clippy -p classic-mbox -- -D warnings clean; rustdoc on every public item.

title: classic-fs: remote bind-mount support (RemoteClient over Classic transport)
description:
Implement classic-fs::RemoteClient -- the outbound 9P client that talks to remote classicd's server via Classic frames 0x0400/0x0401 over the per-peer TCP connections from plan 01. Also wire route/dispatch in classic-node so 0x04xx frames reach a per-attach RemoteClient/server pair.

== Plan slice (excerpt from plans/06-9p-namespace-server.md @ a7b6585) ==

Architecture: One daemon per host. All 9P multiplexes over the existing per-peer TCP connections from plan 01. The FUSE mount talks 9P to an in-process loopback (no TCP); remote prefixes go out as 0x04xx Classic frames.

Public interface:
```rust
impl RemoteClient {
    pub async fn attach(&self, aname: &str) -> io::Result<Fid>;
    pub async fn walk(&self, fid: Fid, names: &[&str]) -> io::Result<Fid>;
    pub async fn open(&self, fid: Fid, flags: u32) -> io::Result<()>;
    pub async fn read(&self, fid: Fid, off: u64, count: u32) -> io::Result<Vec<u8>>;
    pub async fn readdir(&self, fid: Fid, off: u64, count: u32) -> io::Result<Vec<DirEntry>>;
    pub async fn getattr(&self, fid: Fid) -> io::Result<Stat>;
    pub async fn readlink(&self, fid: Fid) -> io::Result<String>;
    pub async fn clunk(&self, fid: Fid) -> io::Result<()>;
}
```

The Mount type carries a Session enum:
```rust
pub enum Session {
    Local(LocalServer),
    Remote(RemoteClient),
}
```

Remote bind-mount construction (per plan): each remote 9P server is a separate root accessed via /cluster/<NodeId>/... in the assembled namespace. RemoteClient uses Tag-multiplexed concurrent requests over the single per-peer TCP connection.

Open question (deferred): peer disappearing mid-task -- v1: reads return EIO once transport notices the peer is gone; no auto-remount. Document in CLI.

Frame routing: classic-node must register kinds 0x0400 (NineReq) and 0x0401 (NineRsp) and route inbound 0x0400 to LocalServer::handle, route inbound 0x0401 to the RemoteClient awaiting that Tag.

== Inputs ==
- Depends on: 9P codec task (encode_t/decode_r), server-loop task (LocalServer signature), file-tree-builder task (DirEntry/Stat/Qid).
- Read: plan 01 -- Frame, Connection trait, frame-kind dispatch (classic-proto/classic-node/transport).
- Read: classic-proto::FrameKind (where 0x0400/0x0401 will be registered by codec task).
- Existing: classic-node connection management (per-peer TCP), classic-proto Frame.

== Outputs ==
- crates/classic-fs/src/client/mod.rs: RemoteClient struct, async methods listed above.
- crates/classic-fs/src/client/fid_pool.rs: Fid allocator (atomic counter; Fid 0 reserved as NOFID==0xFFFFFFFF).
- Tag pool: per-connection u16 tag allocator with pending-request map.
- crates/classic-fs/src/namespace/mod.rs PeerSet trait method: fs_client_for(node_id: NodeId) -> io::Result<RemoteClient> (opens transport on demand, validates with attach(aname='')).
- MODIFIED: crates/classic-node/src/lib.rs and src/transport.rs -- register 0x0400/0x0401 FrameKinds; route inbound 0x0400 to LocalServer::handle; route 0x0401 to pending RemoteClient by Tag.
- Errors: peer-gone produces EIO; remote returns Rlerror -> map to io::Error with that errno.

== Test plan ==
- client unit tests: each method drives encode_t -> mock connection -> decode_r round-trip.
- Tag-multiplex test: two concurrent requests on same connection get distinct Tags; responses route to correct caller.
- Two-node bind-remote integration test (crates/classic-node/tests/fs_bind_remote.rs): two classicd instances in one process (in-memory transport, distinct NodeIds); attach to remote, walk to /node, Tread; assert remote NodeId hex returned.
- Peer-gone test: drop transport; in-flight RemoteClient call returns EIO.
- Frame-routing test: 0x0400 inbound dispatches to LocalServer; 0x0401 inbound resolves the awaiting RemoteClient future.

== Acceptance ==
- RemoteClient implements every async op listed; talks 9P over Classic frames 0x0400/0x0401.
- Two-node bind-remote test passes (in-process two-classicd instances).
- classic-node routes inbound 0x0400 to LocalServer and 0x0401 to RemoteClient pending-request map.
- Peer-gone returns EIO; no panics.
- cargo test -p classic-fs --test client; cargo test -p classic-node --test fs_bind_remote pass; clippy clean.
acceptance_criteria: RemoteClient round-trips every implemented op; Tag multiplexing works; two-node bind-remote integration test passes; classic-node routes 0x0400/0x0401 correctly; peer-gone returns EIO; cargo test/clippy clean.

title: classic-place: SPREAD algorithm with backtracking + MRV ordering
description:
Part of epic classic-2ki. Plan: plans/07-placement-groups.md @ a7b6585.

## Reading first
- git show a7b6585:plans/07-placement-groups.md
- git show a7b6585:plans/03-placement-predicates.md  (Requirement eval, candidate filter)

## Excerpted design slice

### From plan §SPREAD — every member on a different node
> Bipartite matching: members <-> nodes such that each member's predicate is
> satisfied by its assigned node and no two members share a node. v1 uses
> greedy backtracking (fine for the <= 64-member groups we expect); we can swap
> to Hopcroft-Karp under the same API later if profiling warrants.
>
> fn place_spread(group, ads) -> Result<...>:
>     if group.members.len() > ads.len():
>         return SpreadTooLarge { needed, nodes }
>
>     // Build per-member candidate lists (nodes that satisfy that member).
>     // Sort members ascending by candidate-count: minimum-remaining-values
>     // heuristic from CSP solvers — prunes backtracking dramatically.
>     per_member = members.map(|m| (m, candidates(m, ads)))
>                         .sorted_by_key(|x| x.candidates.len())
>     used: HashSet<NodeId> = {}
>     assignment = []
>     if backtrack(per_member, 0, &mut used, &mut assignment):
>         return Ok(reorder_to(assignment, group.members))   // restore caller's order
>     Err(SpreadInfeasible)
>
> fn backtrack(per_member, i, used, assignment) -> bool:
>     if i == per_member.len(): return true
>     for node_id in per_member[i].candidates:
>         if used.contains(&node_id): continue
>         used.insert(node_id); assignment.push((label, node_id))
>         if backtrack(per_member, i+1, used, assignment): return true
>         assignment.pop(); used.remove(&node_id)
>     false

### From plan §Functional requirements
> FR-3: SPREAD assigns each member to a distinct node, exhaustive
> backtracking with MRV ordering.

## Inputs
- /home/horde/src/classic/crates/classic-place/src/group.rs (from prior tasks — place_spread currently stub)
- Plan 03's predicate evaluation surface for filtering candidate ads per member.

## Outputs
Files to modify:
- /home/horde/src/classic/crates/classic-place/src/group.rs — replace place_spread stub with full implementation. Add private helpers: candidates(member: &GroupMember, ads: &[NodeAd]) -> Vec<NodeId> and backtrack(per_member, i, &mut used, &mut assignment) -> bool.

Algorithm:
1. If group.members.len() > ads.len(): Err(SpreadTooLarge { needed: group.members.len(), nodes: ads.len() }).
2. For each member, build candidates list (NodeIds whose ad satisfies member.req); preserve original index for reorder.
3. Sort per_member ascending by candidates.len() (MRV).
4. Run backtrack with HashSet<NodeId> of used nodes; first complete assignment wins.
5. On success, reorder result back to caller's member-declaration order before returning.
6. On failure: Err(SpreadInfeasible).

## Test plan
- /home/horde/src/classic/crates/classic-place/tests/spread.rs:
  - spread_too_large — 5 members, 3 nodes -> SpreadTooLarge { needed: 5, nodes: 3 }.
  - spread_one_per_node — 3 members each any(gpu), 3 distinct GPU nodes: 1:1 assignment, all NodeIds pairwise unique.
  - spread_backtrack_required — A matches only X; B matches X or Y. Naive greedy ordered as [B,A] would assign B->X then fail A. With MRV ordering, A (1 candidate) is tried first and gets X; B then gets Y. Asserts the algorithm returns a valid assignment in this scenario.
  - spread_infeasible_via_matching — bipartite graph has no perfect matching: SpreadInfeasible.
  - spread_result_in_caller_order — input members [m1, m2, m3]; output Vec<(label, NodeId)> labels in [m1, m2, m3] order regardless of MRV reordering used internally.
- cargo test -p classic-place spread

## Task-specific Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] place_spread returns a complete one-member-per-node assignment when feasible.
- [ ] Result respects caller's declaration order even though internal solver uses MRV.
- [ ] SpreadTooLarge fires before any candidate-list build (cheap check).
- [ ] SpreadInfeasible fires only when bipartite graph has no perfect matching.
- [ ] All listed tests pass.

## Intra-epic deps
- Depends on the types task. Independent of the PACK task (parallel within epic but both depend on types).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] place_spread returns one-member-per-node assignment when feasible.
- [ ] Result in caller's declaration order.
- [ ] SpreadTooLarge fires before candidate build.
- [ ] SpreadInfeasible fires only when no perfect matching.
- [ ] spread_* tests pass.

title: classic-fs: per-spawn namespace assembly + FUSE mount lifecycle
description:
Implement per-spawn Namespace assembly (local root + zero remote bind-mounts) and the FUSE bridge that mounts assembled namespaces into Linux. Mount on spawn, unmount on child exit, reap stale mounts on daemon startup.

== Plan slice (excerpt from plans/06-9p-namespace-server.md @ a7b6585) ==

Per-spawn namespace algorithm:
```text
fn assemble(spec, mbox, peers, local) -> Namespace:
    mounts = [Mount{ at: '/', session: Local(local.clone_handle()), remote_root: '' }]

    for (node_id, at_path) in spec.bind_remote:
        if node_id == local.node_id:           return Err('self bind')
        if !at_path.is_absolute() or at_path == '/': return Err("'at' must be absolute non-root")
        if mounts.any(|m| m.at == at_path):    return Err('duplicate mount point')
        client = peers.fs_client_for(node_id)? # opens transport on demand
        client.attach(aname='').await?         # validate the remote is alive
        mounts.push(Mount{ at: at_path, session: Remote(client), remote_root: '' })

    mounts.sort_by_key(|m| -(m.at.components().count() as i32))   # longest prefix first

    fuse_mp = format!('/run/classic/ns/{}', mbox.0)
    fs::create_dir_all(&fuse_mp)?
    return Namespace { task: mbox, mounts, fuse_mountpoint: fuse_mp }
```

Dispatch for path p: scan mounts in order, find first whose at is a prefix of p, strip prefix, forward residual to that mount.

FUSE mount lifecycle:
- Mount point: /run/classic/ns/<MboxId> (tmpfs on systemd hosts; falls back to /var/run/classic/ns/<MboxId>).
- on spawn: ns = Namespace::build_for_spawn(spec, mbox, peers); handle = ns.mount_fuse()? (forks fuser worker; returns once mounted). Pipeline then sets child's CWD/chroot relative to ns.fuse_mountpoint.
- on child exit (plan-04 ChildExit): Namespace::unmount(handle) -- umount2(MNT_DETACH) + rmdir.
- FUSE worker is a tokio task in classicd. On daemon startup, walk /run/classic/ns/ and force-unmount leftovers (umount2(MNT_FORCE | MNT_DETACH)). Safe because v1 has no live tasks across daemon restarts.
- Mount options: default_permissions=no, allow_other=no, ro=yes, fsname=classic, subtype=classic.

Public interface:
```rust
impl Namespace {
    pub fn build_for_spawn(spec: &SpawnSpec, local: &LocalServer, peers: &PeerSet) -> Self;
    pub fn mount_fuse(&self) -> io::Result<FuseHandle>;
    pub fn unmount(handle: FuseHandle);
}
```

Data shapes:
```rust
pub struct Mount {
    pub at: PathBuf,
    pub session: Session,         // Local(LocalServer) | Remote(RemoteClient)
    pub remote_root: String,
}
pub struct Namespace {
    pub task: MboxId,
    pub mounts: Vec<Mount>,
    pub fuse_mountpoint: PathBuf,
}
```

== Inputs ==
- Depends on: server-loop task (LocalServer), file-tree-builder task, remote-bind task (RemoteClient signature).
- Read: classic-spawn::SpawnSpec.bind_remote (plan 04).
- Read: classic-cap MboxId from classic-proto.
- Crate dep: fuser (FUSE3 ABI).

== Outputs ==
- crates/classic-fs/src/namespace/mod.rs: Mount, Session, Namespace, build_for_spawn, dispatch (longest-prefix).
- crates/classic-fs/src/fuse/mod.rs: fuser::Filesystem impl, mount_fuse, FuseHandle.
- crates/classic-fs/src/fuse/translate.rs: FUSE-syscall -> 9P op translation (lookup -> Twalk+Tgetattr; read -> Tread; readdir -> Treaddir; readlink -> Treadlink).
- Stale-mount reaping: pub fn reap_stale_mounts(ns_root: &Path) called by classicd at startup.
- mount_fuse uses ro=yes, allow_other=no, fsname=classic, subtype=classic flags.

== Test plan ==
- namespace::dispatch unit test: three mounts (/, /cluster/B, /cluster/B/dev); longest-prefix wins; reject relative paths and root mounts; reject duplicate mountpoints; reject self-bind.
- FUSE smoke test (tests/fuse_smoke.rs): mounts in-process server through FUSE at tempdir; runs ls, cat, stat, readlink; gated by CLASSIC_FUSE_TESTS=1 env var.
- Stale-mount reap test: create a fake mount entry, run reap_stale_mounts, assert it's gone.
- Lifecycle test: build_for_spawn, mount_fuse, unmount; tmpdir is cleaned up.

== Acceptance ==
- Namespace::build_for_spawn validates all error cases (self-bind, relative path, root-bind, duplicate).
- Mounts sorted longest-prefix-first; dispatch finds correct mount.
- FUSE bridge translates Linux syscalls to 9P ops and forwards to local or remote session.
- mount_fuse creates /run/classic/ns/<MboxId>, unmount tears it down via umount2(MNT_DETACH).
- reap_stale_mounts removes leftover mounts at daemon start.
- cargo test -p classic-fs (with CLASSIC_FUSE_TESTS=1 on Linux x86_64) passes; clippy clean.
acceptance_criteria: build_for_spawn validates all error cases; longest-prefix dispatch correct; FUSE bridge translates syscalls to 9P; mount/unmount lifecycle works; stale-mount reaping works; cargo test/clippy clean.

title: classic-place: PACK algorithm with free-pool deduction
description:
Part of epic classic-2ki. Plan: plans/07-placement-groups.md @ a7b6585.

## Reading first
- git show a7b6585:plans/07-placement-groups.md
- git show a7b6585:plans/03-placement-predicates.md  (place_one, Requirement eval)

## Excerpted design slice

### From plan §PACK — every member on the same node
> fn place_pack(group, ads) -> Result<...>:
>     candidates = ads.sorted_by(rank_pack_desc)   // most idle GPU mem first;
>                                                  // tiebreak by node_id for determinism
>     for ad in candidates:
>         if let Some(picks) = try_pack_on(ad, &group.members):
>             return Ok(zip(labels, repeat(ad.node_id)))
>     Err(PackInfeasible(group.members.len()))
>
> fn try_pack_on(ad, members) -> Option<Vec<Picked>>:
>     // Mutable copy of the ad's free pool: per-GPU index in-use vs. free,
>     // free CPU slots, free RAM bytes. Deducting after each pick is what
>     // handles intra-group contention — without it, two members both
>     // claiming the lone 80 GB GPU would both succeed against the
>     // unmodified ad.
>     pool = ad.free_pool().clone()
>     chosen = []
>     for m in members:                            // declaration order — first wins
>         match place_one_in_pool(&m.req, &pool):
>             Some(picked) => { pool.deduct(&picked); chosen.push(picked) }
>             None         => return None          // back out, try next ad
>     Some(chosen)
>
> place_one_in_pool is a thin variant of plan 03's place_one() that scores
> against a mutable free-pool. **Member declaration order** breaks contention
> ties (first-listed wins) — documented and stable. v1 does not auto-sort;
> users put strictest requirements first.

### From plan §Functional requirements
> FR-2: PACK satisfies all members on a single node, accounting for
> intra-group contention via free-pool deduction.
> FR-9: Member declaration order breaks PACK contention ties (first-listed wins).

## Inputs
- /home/horde/src/classic/crates/classic-place/src/group.rs (from prior task — types defined; place_pack is currently a stub)
- /home/horde/src/classic/crates/classic-place/src/free_pool.rs (from prior task — FreePool struct + deduct method)
- Plan 03's place_one / Requirement eval surface (from classic-place same crate, sibling modules)

## Outputs
Files to modify:
- /home/horde/src/classic/crates/classic-place/src/group.rs — replace place_pack stub with full implementation. Add private helper try_pack_on(ad: &NodeAd, members: &[GroupMember]) -> Option<Vec<Picked>> and rank_pack_desc comparator.
- /home/horde/src/classic/crates/classic-place/src/free_pool.rs — extend with place_one_in_pool(req: &Requirement, pool: &FreePool) -> Option<Picked> (variant of place_one that consults mutable pool) and Picked struct describing what gets deducted (gpu indices, cpu slots, ram bytes).

Algorithm:
1. rank_pack_desc: sort ads desc by total free GPU memory across all GPUs in the ad; tiebreak ascending by node_id bytes for determinism.
2. For each candidate ad (in order): clone its free_pool, iterate members in declaration order calling place_one_in_pool against the pool; on Some(picked) pool.deduct(&picked) and push to chosen; on None abandon this ad and continue.
3. If chosen.len() == members.len() return Ok(zip(labels, repeat(ad.node_id))).
4. If no ad satisfies, return Err(PackInfeasible(group.members.len())).

## Test plan
- /home/horde/src/classic/crates/classic-place/tests/pack.rs:
  - pack_single_node_satisfies_all — 2 members, 1 node with 2 GPUs: both placed.
  - pack_intragroup_contention — 2 members each requiring vram_mb >= 80000; node has one 80 GB + one 40 GB GPU: PackInfeasible.
  - pack_first_member_wins_contention — 2 members each requiring any(gpu); node has one 80 GB + one 40 GB GPU: first member gets 80 GB GPU, second gets 40 GB GPU.
  - pack_member_infeasible_against_all_ads — single member's requirement matches no node: PackInfeasible (not MemberInfeasible — the per-member error variant is for the single-spawn flow).
  - pack_picks_richest_node_first — given two PACK-capable nodes, the one with more free GPU mem is selected.
- cargo test -p classic-place pack

## Task-specific Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] place_pack returns a complete assignment when feasible.
- [ ] Free-pool deduction prevents two members from claiming the same physical GPU index on the same ad.
- [ ] Member declaration order is the tiebreak in contention (first wins).
- [ ] Failure paths return PackInfeasible(n) where n == group.members.len().
- [ ] All listed tests pass.
- [ ] cargo doc -p classic-place still builds clean.

## Intra-epic deps
- Depends on the types task (group.rs/free_pool.rs scaffolding).
- Note: classic-place's plan 03 work (Requirement, place_one) is owned by epic classic-i0n; this task assumes those APIs exist.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] place_pack returns a complete assignment when feasible.
- [ ] Free-pool deduction prevents two members from claiming the same GPU.
- [ ] Member declaration order tiebreak (first wins).
- [ ] Failure paths return PackInfeasible(n).
- [ ] pack_* tests pass.
- [ ] cargo doc clean.

title: classic-fs: synthetic file-tree builder (/dev, /proc, /svc, /node)
description:
Build the synthetic virtual hierarchy that the 9P server walks: /node, /dev/pci/..., /dev/gpu/<i>/{vendor,device,vram_total_mb,vram_free_mb,nvlink_peers,in_use}, /proc/<MboxId>/{cmdline,status,caps}, /svc/<name>. Hierarchy is materialized lazily from the NodeView snapshot; static fields stable, dynamic fields (vram_free_mb, in_use, status) re-evaluated per read.

== Plan slice (excerpt from plans/06-9p-namespace-server.md @ a7b6585) ==

File hierarchy:
```
/                           dr-xr-xr-x
|-- node                    -r--r--r--   local NodeId hex (32 chars + \n)
|-- dev/
|   |-- pci/
|   |   '-- <vendor>:<device>/<bus:device.function>/{numa_node,iommu_group,class,vendor,device}
|   '-- gpu/
|       '-- <index>/{vendor,device,vram_total_mb,vram_free_mb,nvlink_peers,in_use}
|-- proc/<MboxId>/{cmdline,status,caps}
'-- svc/<name>              symlink target = 'node=<hex> mbox=<u64>' (multi-line for N instances)
```

Modes: directories 0o555, regular files 0o444, symlinks 0o777. UID/GID always 0/0. mtime is daemon start for static files, now() for dynamic ones.

Dynamic content:
- /dev/gpu/<i>/vram_free_mb: read from cached NodeAd ad cache.
- /dev/gpu/<i>/in_use: every Tread triggers synchronous classic-cap::Broker::lookup_gpu(u32) -> Option<CapHolder{mbox, since}>. Returns '0\n' for free, '1 mbox=<id>\n' for held.
- /proc/<MboxId>/status: 'running\n' or 'exited code=N\n'.
- /svc/<name>: symlink whose target encodes 'node=<hex> mbox=<u64>' lines.

NodeView snapshot:
```rust
pub struct NodeView<'a> {
    pub node_id: NodeId,
    pub ad: &'a classic_ad::NodeAd,
    pub services: &'a classic_mbox::ServiceTable,
    pub procs: &'a classic_mbox::ProcTable,
    pub caps: &'a classic_cap::Broker,
}
```

Treaddir order: lexicographic.

== Inputs ==
- Depends on: 9P codec task and server-loop task.
- Read: classic-ad::NodeAd schema for GPU/PCI fields (plan 02).
- Read: classic-mbox::ServiceTable, ProcTable shapes (plan 05).
- Read: classic-cap::Broker::lookup_gpu(u32) signature (plan 04 fills it; this task consumes the read-only side).
- Read: classic-fs::proto for Qid/DirEntry/Stat shapes.

== Outputs ==
- crates/classic-fs/src/server/tree.rs: virtual tree builder, path -> Qid resolution, directory listing, file content rendering.
- Public types: TreeNode { Dir, File, Symlink } enum or trait; NodeId-based Qid path encoding.
- Function shapes:
  - lookup(view: &NodeView, path: &[&str]) -> Result<TreeNode, Rlerror>
  - read_file(view: &NodeView, path: &[&str], off: u64, count: u32) -> Result<Vec<u8>, Rlerror>
  - readdir(view: &NodeView, path: &[&str]) -> Result<Vec<DirEntry>, Rlerror>
  - readlink(view: &NodeView, path: &[&str]) -> Result<String, Rlerror>
  - getattr(view: &NodeView, path: &[&str]) -> Result<Stat, Rlerror>
- Helper: read_in_use(view, gpu_index) -> Vec<u8> per plan algorithm.
- All paths read-only; modes 0o555/0o444/0o777 per plan.

== Test plan ==
- server::tree golden test against a frozen NodeView fixture: enumerate full tree, snapshot Treaddir output (lexicographic), verify qid.type bits (dir/file/symlink).
- Dynamic fields: vary the fixture's Broker state; assert /dev/gpu/0/in_use output changes between reads.
- /svc/<name> symlink target test: multi-instance service produces multi-line target.
- Mode tests: directories 0555, files 0444, symlinks 0777; uid=gid=0.
- Negative paths: lookup of nonexistent path returns Rlerror{ENOENT}; readlink on regular file returns Rlerror{EINVAL}.

== Acceptance ==
- Full hierarchy from plan materializes from a NodeView fixture.
- Dynamic fields (vram_free_mb, in_use, status) re-evaluate per read.
- Treaddir entries are lexicographic.
- /svc/<name> emitted as symlink with correct target encoding.
- cargo test -p classic-fs --lib tree passes; clippy clean.
acceptance_criteria: Full hierarchy materializes from NodeView fixture; dynamic fields re-evaluate per read; /svc/<name> symlink target correct; Treaddir lexicographic; cargo test/clippy clean.

title: classic-place: PlacementGroup types + free_pool + place_group skeleton
description:
Part of epic classic-2ki. Plan: plans/07-placement-groups.md @ a7b6585.

## Reading first
- git show a7b6585:plans/07-placement-groups.md
- git show a7b6585:plans/ARCHITECTURE.md
- git show a7b6585:plans/03-placement-predicates.md  (Requirement type, place_one)

## Excerpted design slice

### From plan §Design / Data shapes
> // classic-place
> pub struct GroupMember {
>     pub label: String,                  // unique within group; key in result
>     pub req: Requirement,               // plan 03 predicate
>     pub argv: Vec<String>,
>     pub env: Vec<(String, String)>,     // ordered; later wins on duplicate key
> }
> pub enum GroupStrategy { Pack, Spread }
> pub struct PlacementGroup {
>     pub strategy: GroupStrategy,
>     pub members: Vec<GroupMember>,
> }
>
> #[derive(thiserror::Error, Debug)]
> pub enum GroupPlaceError {
>     #[error("empty placement group")]                                    Empty,
>     #[error("duplicate member label: {0}")]                              DuplicateLabel(String),
>     #[error("PACK: no single node satisfies all {0} member requirements")] PackInfeasible(usize),
>     #[error("SPREAD: cluster has {nodes} nodes, group needs {needed}")]  SpreadTooLarge { needed: usize, nodes: usize },
>     #[error("SPREAD: no one-member-per-node assignment satisfies all requirements")] SpreadInfeasible,
>     #[error("member {label}: no node matches requirement")]              MemberInfeasible { label: String },
> }
>
> pub fn place_group(group: &PlacementGroup, ads: &[NodeAd])
