title: classic-fs: server loop, fid table, request dispatch, errno mapping
description:
Build the local 9P server loop: connection handling, fid table, request dispatch to op handlers, errno mapping for rejected ops. The server is a state machine that consumes 9P T-messages and produces R-messages; never closes the connection except on unparseable frames.

== Plan slice (excerpt from plans/06-9p-namespace-server.md @ a7b6585) ==

Server architecture: One daemon per host. All 9P multiplexes over the existing per-peer TCP connections from plan 01. The FUSE mount talks 9P to an in-process loopback (no TCP); remote prefixes go out as 0x04xx Classic frames.

Public interface:
```rust
impl LocalServer {
    pub fn new(view_provider: Arc<dyn Fn() -> NodeView<'static>>) -> Self;
    /// Drive one inbound 9P frame; produce zero or one outbound frame.
    pub fn handle(&self, kind: FrameKind, body: &[u8]) -> Option<(FrameKind, Vec<u8>)>;
}
```

Op coverage (from plan):
- Tversion(100): negotiates 9P2000.L; msize clamped to 64 KiB.
- Tattach(104): afid==NOFID; aname=='' returns root.
- Tflush(108): no-op + Rflush.
- Twalk(110): up to 16 names per call.
- Tlopen(12): read modes only; write flags -> EROFS.
- Treadlink(22): /svc/<name> is a symlink whose target is NetIds.
- Tgetattr(24): synthetic stat (mode, size, mtime; uid=gid=0).
- Treaddir(40): variable-length entries, lexicographic order.
- Tfsync(50): no-op + Rfsync.
- Tread(116): synthetic content; always uncached. /dev/gpu/<i>/in_use consults classic-cap broker per read.
- Tclunk(120): releases a fid.

Rejection: read-only writes -> EROFS; auth/xattr/lock/legacy -> EOPNOTSUPP. Always well-formed Rlerror; never close connection.

Fid table: per-attach map Fid -> tree-node handle. Allocated on Tattach/Twalk(newfid), freed on Tclunk.

== Inputs ==
- Depends on: 9P codec task (T/R messages, Qid, DirEntry, Stat, Rlerror).
- Read: classic-fs::proto module.
- Existing crates: classic-proto::FrameKind, NodeAd from classic-ad, ServiceTable/ProcTable from classic-mbox, Broker from classic-cap (read-only consumers; do not implement, just import the trait shapes from those crates).

== Outputs ==
- crates/classic-fs/src/server/mod.rs: LocalServer, fid table, handle() entry point.
- crates/classic-fs/src/server/ops.rs: per-op handlers (handle_tversion, handle_tattach, handle_twalk, handle_tlopen, handle_tread, handle_treaddir, handle_tgetattr, handle_treadlink, handle_tflush, handle_tclunk, handle_tfsync, plus all rejection handlers returning Rlerror{EROFS} or Rlerror{EOPNOTSUPP}).
- crates/classic-fs/src/server/view.rs: NodeView struct (provider abstraction over NodeAd, ServiceTable, ProcTable, Broker).
- Errno mapping: every documented rejection returns the documented code (table in plan).
- handle() returns Some((FrameKind::NineRsp, body)) for every input frame (or None only for the unparseable case where the connection should close).

== Test plan ==
- server::ops unit tests via in-process driver:
  - Tversion msize clamp: client requests 1 MiB, server returns 64 KiB.
  - Twalk with 5+ names: walks correctly through synthetic tree.
  - Tread offset past EOF: returns 0-byte read (no error).
  - Tclunk releases a fid: subsequent op on same fid returns Rlerror{EBADF}.
  - Twrite on any fid: returns Rlerror{EROFS}.
  - Tauth: returns Rlerror{EOPNOTSUPP}.
  - Tstat (legacy 9P2000): returns Rlerror{EOPNOTSUPP}.
- errno test: every rejected op returns the documented code (table from plan op-coverage).
- Connection robustness: garbage bytes produce Rlerror or unparseable signal; never panic.

== Acceptance ==
- LocalServer::handle correctly dispatches all 11 implemented ops and all 18 rejected ops.
- Fid table supports allocation, lookup, and clunk; double-clunk returns EBADF.
- All rejections produce well-formed Rlerror with the documented errno.
- cargo test -p classic-fs --lib server passes; clippy clean.
acceptance_criteria: Server dispatches all 11 implemented ops; all 18 rejected ops return well-formed Rlerror with documented errno; fid table correct on attach/walk/clunk; cargo test/clippy clean.

title: Feature: Placement groups
description:
PACK/SPREAD bundles with two-phase commit reservation/spawn for atomic multi-process placement.

A placement group is an atomic bundle of related processes spawned together under a co-placement strategy. v1 supports PACK (every member on the same node) and SPREAD (every member on a different node). Each member carries its own predicate Requirement, argv, and env, so members may have different hardware needs (e.g. one rank-0 trainer needing an 80 GB GPU plus N workers needing 40 GB GPUs). Two-phase commit (2PC) layered on plan 04's spawn frames provides atomicity: either every member is running or nothing is. TTL'd reservations (10 s default) bound worst-case resource hold so failed coordinators cannot strand caps.

Plan: plans/07-placement-groups.md @ commit a7b6585. Read with: git show a7b6585:plans/07-placement-groups.md

Foundations: plans/ARCHITECTURE.md (frame ranges, identity types NodeId/MboxId/NetId, encoding bincode v2 fixed-int LE, transport). Read with: git show a7b6585:plans/ARCHITECTURE.md

Workflow rules: AGENTS.md "Feature Development Workflow" section.

Owner crates: crates/classic-place (PlacementGroup types, place_group algorithm) and crates/classic-spawn (2PC orchestrator + per-node reservation table). CLI subcommand in crates/classic-cli. Frame kinds 0x0320..=0x0327 within plan 04's classic-spawn band 0x0300-0x03FF.

Depends on plan 03 (Requirement, place_one) and plan 04 (single-spawn frames 0x0300-0x030F + cap/cgroup machinery reused by phase 2 commit).

Out of scope (v1): cross-group bin-packing optimization; group respawn on member crash; anti-affinity beyond SPREAD; group quotas / fair-share; topology-aware strategies (PACK within rack, SPREAD across racks); persisting group identity across daemon restarts; STRICT_PACK / STRICT_SPREAD best-effort fallback variants.
design:
Group placement spans two crates: classic-place (pure algorithm; no I/O) and classic-spawn (2PC orchestrator using new frames; reuses plan 04 local-spawn path inside phase 2). classic-cli parses TOML and calls into both.

Key data shapes:
- classic-place: PlacementGroup { strategy, members }, GroupMember { label, req: Requirement, argv, env }, GroupStrategy { Pack, Spread }, place_group(group, ads) -> Result<Vec<(label, NodeId)>, GroupPlaceError> returning a complete assignment or error — never partial.
- classic-spawn: GroupId([u8;16]) random per submit, GroupSpawnError, GroupSubmitResult { group_id, members: Vec<(label, NetId)> }, GroupCfg { reserve_timeout=10s, commit_timeout=30s }, async submit_group(group, ads, cfg).

Wire format additions in 0x0320..=0x0327 (within classic-spawn band 0x0300-0x03FF; plan 04 owns 0x0300-0x030F for single-spawn): GroupReserve=0x0320 coord->node, GroupReserveAck=0x0321, GroupReserveDeny=0x0322, GroupCommit=0x0323, GroupSpawned=0x0324 carries NetId per member, GroupCommitFailed=0x0325, GroupAbort=0x0326 idempotent, GroupAbortAck=0x0327. bincode v2 fixed-int LE.

GroupReserve payload: GroupReserveFrame { group_id, members: Vec<ReservedMember{label, req, argv, env}>, reserve_ttl_ms: u32 }. GroupReserveAck carries (GroupId, Vec<(label, reservation_token: u64)>). Token opaque to coord; coord echoes in GroupCommit.

PACK algorithm: candidates sorted by rank_pack_desc (most idle GPU mem first; tiebreak by node_id for determinism); for each candidate ad, try_pack_on with mutable free-pool deduction. place_one_in_pool is a thin variant of plan 03's place_one() that scores against a mutable free-pool. Member declaration order breaks contention ties (first-listed wins). Return PackInfeasible(n) on failure.

SPREAD algorithm: bipartite matching members-to-nodes via greedy backtracking; build per-member candidate lists; sort members by ascending candidate-count (Minimum-Remaining-Values heuristic from CSPs to prune backtracking); used HashSet<NodeId> tracks taken nodes; backtrack returns first valid assignment; reorder result back to caller's member order. SpreadTooLarge if members > nodes; SpreadInfeasible if no perfect matching.

2PC orchestrator: place_group -> phase 1 GroupReserve in parallel with reserve_timeout; on Deny/timeout best-effort GroupAbort to acked nodes (others TTL-sweep); phase 2 GroupCommit in parallel; on CommitFailed Kill (plan 04 frame) already-spawned members + GroupAbort uncommitted nodes.

Per-node reservation table in classicd: HashMap<GroupId, Reservation { group_id, members: Vec<ReservedMemberSlot>, deadline: Instant, state: Held|Committing|Released }>. GroupReserve revalidates Requirement against current state, attempts cap+slot reserve; success Ack, fail Deny. GroupCommit drives plan 04 local-spawn using reserved caps. GroupAbort releases caps+slots (idempotent). TTL sweeper thread wakes every 1 s; reservations past deadline auto-released — leak-prevention net.

Failure modes analyzed: F1 phase-1 ack-timeout (TTL sweeps slow node, no leak); F2 GroupReserveDeny (coord aborts rest); F3 coord crash before ack/timeout (TTL sweep cleans all daemons); F4 GroupCommitFailed after exec error (Kill spawned + Abort rest); F5 coord crash mid-commit (orphaned independent tasks; group atomicity NOT preserved, documented); F6 reservation expires exactly as Commit arrives (mutex-resolved at daemon, replies CommitFailed { reason: ttl expired }); F7 duplicate GroupReserve same group_id (rejected with Deny duplicate group_id).

Crate layout: classic-place/src/{lib.rs MODIFIED re-export group types, group.rs NEW, free_pool.rs NEW}; classic-spawn/src/{lib.rs MODIFIED re-export submit_group/GroupId, group_proto.rs NEW, coord.rs NEW, reservation.rs NEW}; classic-cli/src/{submit.rs NEW, group_toml.rs NEW}; classic-proto/src/frame.rs MODIFIED add 0x0320..0x0327 to FrameKind.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] AC-1: A 2-member PACK group with any(gpu) lands both members on the same node, observable via classic ps showing identical node_id.
- [ ] AC-2: A 3-member SPREAD group lands one member on each of three distinct nodes; node_ids are pairwise unique.
- [ ] AC-3: When phase 1 fails on any node, zero members of the group are spawned anywhere in the cluster (verified via classic ps).
- [ ] AC-4: When the coordinator is kill -9'd after phase-1 acks but before phase-2 commit, all daemons release their held caps within reserve_ttl + 2 s (verified via cap-pool inspection in classic node-info).
- [ ] AC-5: place_group returns the expected GroupPlaceError variant for each failure mode covered by unit tests.
- [ ] AC-6: classic submit nonexistent.toml exits non-zero with a clean message; classic submit malformed.toml reports the parse error.
- [ ] AC-7: All unit + integration tests in the testing plan pass on Linux x86_64.
- [ ] AC-8: No new clippy warnings; cargo doc builds clean for every modified crate.

title: classic-fs: 9P2000.L wire codec inside frames 0x0400-0x04FF
description:
Implement the 9P2000.L wire codec for the classic-fs crate. Encode/decode all op messages used by v1 inside Classic frame kinds NineReq=0x0400 and NineRsp=0x0401.

== Plan slice (excerpt from plans/06-9p-namespace-server.md @ a7b6585) ==

Wire format: No new payload types. Two frame kinds: NineReq=0x0400 (raw 9P T-message bytes minus the leading size[4]) and NineRsp=0x0401 (the R-message equivalent). Outer Classic length is authoritative. 9P msize clamps to 64 KiB so one 9P message always fits in one Classic frame -- no fragmentation in v1. 0x0402-0x04FF reserved for future fs-layer frames; unused in v1.

Implemented ops (T-codes per 9P2000.L spec):
- Tversion(100), Tattach(104), Tflush(108), Twalk(110), Tlopen(12), Treadlink(22), Tgetattr(24), Treaddir(40), Tfsync(50), Tread(116), Tclunk(120). Plus all matching R-messages (R<op>=T<op>+1).
- Rejected ops returning Rlerror{EROFS}: Tlcreate(14), Tsymlink(16), Tmknod(18), Trename(20), Tsetattr(26), Tlink(70), Tmkdir(72), Trenameat(74), Tunlinkat(76), Twrite(118), Tremove(122).
- Rejected ops returning Rlerror{EOPNOTSUPP}: Tauth(102), Txattrwalk(30), Txattrcreate(32), Tlock(52), Tgetlock(54), Tstat(124), Twstat(126).

Protocol carried as raw 9P bytes wrapped inside Classic frames. The 9P size[4] prefix is stripped on the wire (outer Classic length authoritative).

== Inputs ==
- Read: plans/06-9p-namespace-server.md @ a7b6585 (full plan).
- Read: plans/ARCHITECTURE.md (frame format, FrameKind ranges).
- 9P2000.L spec: https://github.com/chaos/diod/blob/master/protocol.md
- Existing crate: classic-proto (Frame/FrameKind enum -- register 0x0400/0x0401 here).

== Outputs ==
- New crate skeleton: crates/classic-fs/Cargo.toml; src/lib.rs (pub mod proto, server, client, namespace, fuse, errno).
- crates/classic-fs/src/proto/mod.rs, codec.rs, types.rs.
- types: Fid(u32), Tag(u16), Qid{type:u8, version:u32, path:u64}, DirEntry{qid, offset:u64, ty:u8, name:String}, Stat (9P2000.L getattr response shape: mode, uid, gid, nlink, rdev, size, blksize, blocks, atime, mtime, ctime, btime, gen, data_version).
- Encode/decode for every op listed above (T- and R-messages) plus Rlerror{ecode:u32}.
- Public fns: encode_t(msg) -> Vec<u8>, decode_t(bytes) -> Result<TMessage>, encode_r, decode_r.
- crates/classic-fs/src/errno.rs: Linux errno constants used by Rlerror (EROFS, EOPNOTSUPP, EIO, EINVAL, ENOENT, EBADF, ENOTDIR, EISDIR, EINTR).
- Register FrameKind::NineReq=0x0400 and FrameKind::NineRsp=0x0401 in classic-proto.

== Test plan ==
- proto::codec round-trip property test: decode(encode(msg)) == msg for every implemented op.
- Negative tests: truncated bytes, unknown op-codes, oversize Twalk (>16 names) all return decode errors.
- msize clamp test: Tversion negotiation clamps msize to 64 KiB.
- Frame-range audit: grep '0x04' across workspace shows only this crate; only 0x0400/0x0401 used.

== Acceptance ==
- All 9P2000.L op messages used by v1 round-trip via codec.
- Rlerror codec emits well-formed messages with errno from errno.rs.
- classic-proto registers NineReq/NineRsp FrameKind variants in 0x0400-0x04FF range, no others.
- cargo build, cargo test -p classic-fs --lib, cargo clippy -p classic-fs --all-targets clean.
acceptance_criteria: 9P2000.L codec round-trips all v1 op messages; Rlerror well-formed; FrameKind 0x0400/0x0401 registered in classic-proto; cargo test/clippy clean for the crate.

title: Feature: Mailbox + service directory
description:
Build the classic-mbox crate: ChrysaLisp-style mailbox IPC primitives (in-process and cross-node) plus a cluster-wide gossiped Service Directory that resolves well-known names to NetIds. Tasks allocate mailboxes, others address them by NetId; tasks also publish/lookup services by string name. Delivery is fire-and-forget; the directory is a Lamport-clock last-writer-wins CRDT keyed by (name, net_id). Auto-GC of services and mailboxes when the owning task exits. Owns wire frame range 0x0200-0x02FF.

Plan: plans/05-mailbox-service-directory.md @ commit a7b6585. Read with: git show a7b6585:plans/05-mailbox-service-directory.md

Foundations: plans/ARCHITECTURE.md (identity types NodeId, MboxId, NetId; frame format; frame ranges; encoding bincode v2; transport).

Owner crate: crates/classic-mbox. Owns frame-kind range 0x0200-0x02FF.
Consumers: classic-fs (plan 06: /svc/<name>), classic-spawn (plan 04: lifecycle hooks for GC), classic-place/spawn (plan 07: coordinator service pattern).
Depends on plan 01 (skeleton + transport): classic-proto Frame codec, FrameKind enum, ConnectionRegistry, peer connection lifecycle, Hello handshake.

Out of scope: 9P namespace integration (plan 06), spawn-pipeline stdio (plan 04 uses 0x0300-0x03FF), backpressure/flow control, persistence, authentication/encryption, reliable delivery / acks / retries.
design:
Five subsystems inside classic-mbox sharing a per-daemon registry:

- MailboxRegistry: HashMap<MboxId, tokio::mpsc::Sender<Vec<u8>>> with bounded capacity MBOX_CAPACITY=1024. MboxId allocated from a per-daemon AtomicU64 starting at 1 (mbox 0 reserved per ARCHITECTURE.md); never reused within a daemon's lifetime; counter resets on restart. MailboxRecv::drop evicts its registry entry. Full or missing channel => silent local drop.

- mail_send dispatch: local (net_id.node == self_node_id) -> try_send on mpsc; remote -> serialize a MailSend frame and write it on the live peer connection. No live connection => warn-log + drop. Fire-and-forget: Ok(()) means handed off, never an end-to-end ack.

- ServiceDirectory: HashMap<String, BTreeSet<ServiceEntry>>. ServiceEntry { net_id, lamport: u64, tombstone: bool, last_seen: Instant }. Multimap on name (multiple endpoints per name). Per-task by_task back-index for GC. Local Lamport AtomicU64 advanced on every local declare/forget and clamped to max(local, incoming)+1 on every observed remote.

- Lamport last-writer-wins CRDT keyed by (name, net_id) — single-writer-per-key (only the node owning net_id.node ever writes). Rules: local declare bumps clock and broadcasts ServiceAd; local forget bumps clock, sets tombstone (TOMBSTONE_TTL=60s), broadcasts ServiceForget; inbound deltas updated only if incoming.lamport > existing.lamport; ties broken by NetId (node then mbox lexicographic). Tombstones older than TTL may be GC'd; later ads after GC are accepted.

- GossipEngine: tokio task consuming inbound 0x02xx frames and forwarding outbound deltas. On connect (after Hello), the new side sends ServiceSync; peer replies with ServiceSyncResponse{entries} carrying every non-tombstoned entry it knows. On local declare/forget, single ServiceAd/ServiceForget broadcast to every connected peer. Inbound deltas are applied but NOT re-broadcast (originator already flooded; assumes full-mesh per ARCHITECTURE.md).

- TaskGcTracker: task_id -> {declared services, owned mboxes}. classic-spawn (plan 04) calls on_task_start(task_id) and on_task_exit(task_id); on_task_exit synthesizes service_forget for each declared service and drops mailbox slots. ServiceHandle (RAII) drop also calls service_forget. Both paths idempotent.

Wire frames (bincode v2 fixed-int LE):
  MailSend            = 0x0200  { from: NetId, to: NetId, payload: Vec<u8> } // payload <= 8 MiB
  MailDeliveryFailure = 0x0201  { to: NetId, reason: DeliveryFailureReason } // informational, best-effort
  ServiceAd           = 0x0210  { name: String, net_id: NetId, lamport: u64 } // name <= 256 B UTF-8
  ServiceForget       = 0x0211  { name: String, net_id: NetId, lamport: u64 }
  ServiceSync         = 0x0212  // empty
  ServiceSyncResponse = 0x0213  { entries: Vec<ServiceSyncEntry> }
  0x0214-0x02FF reserved.

Public API (re-exported from lib.rs):
  Mailbox::new() -> (MboxId, MailboxRecv)
  MailboxRecv::recv(&mut self) -> Option<Vec<u8>>; try_recv; Drop evicts registry
  mail_send(to: NetId, payload: Vec<u8>) -> Result<(), MailError>
  select_mboxes(&mut [&mut MailboxRecv]) -> usize
  service_declare(name: &str) -> Result<ServiceHandle, ServiceError>
  service_forget(name: &str)  // idempotent
  service_lookup(name: &str) -> Vec<NetId>          // all live
  service_lookup_one(name: &str) -> Option<NetId>   // round-robin per process

Crate layout: crates/classic-mbox/{Cargo.toml, src/{lib.rs,mbox.rs,send.rs,directory.rs,gossip.rs,gc.rs,frames.rs,error.rs}, tests/{local_roundtrip.rs,directory_crdt.rs,gc.rs,bin/mbox-e2e.rs}}.

Out of scope: 9P namespace (plan 06), spawn stdio (plan 04 uses 0x0300-0x03FF), backpressure/flow control, persistence, auth, reliable delivery.
acceptance_criteria:
AC-1: cargo build -p classic-mbox and cargo test -p classic-mbox pass on Linux x86_64 stable Rust.
AC-2: The two-node end-to-end script runs to completion, with task B's recv() returning b"hello" originally sent from task A.
AC-3: When task A is killed (SIGKILL), node B observes "coordinator" disappear from service_lookup within 2 s.
AC-4: MailboxRecv drop evicts the registry entry — verified by a follow-up send not delivering and a debug counter incrementing.
AC-5: 3-node CRDT convergence test: three nodes each declare "coordinator" with different NetIds; all three converge to the identical 3-element service_lookup result.
AC-6: Every frame in 0x0200-0x02FF round-trips through classic-proto bincode (table-driven test).
AC-7: classic-mbox emits no frame kinds outside 0x0200-0x02FF (static review + debug assertion in GossipEngine).
AC-8: rustdoc on every public item in classic-mbox::lib; cargo clippy -p classic-mbox -- -D warnings clean.

title: Feature: 9P namespace server
description:
Plan-9-style hardware-as-files via a 9P2000.L namespace server (`classic-fs` crate). Per-spawn namespaces let placement decisions express "task sees GPU 0 from node B at /dev/gpu/0" without the task knowing the device is remote. Read-mostly v1: synthetic hierarchy /node, /dev/pci/..., /dev/gpu/..., /proc/<MboxId>, /svc/<name>; FUSE bridge mounts the assembled namespace at /run/classic/ns/<MboxId>. 9P frames carried in Classic frame range 0x0400-0x04FF. Consumes NodeAd from classic-ad, ServiceTable/ProcTable from classic-mbox, and /dev/gpu/<i>/in_use comes from classic-cap broker.

Plan: plans/06-9p-namespace-server.md @ commit a7b6585. Read with: git show a7b6585:plans/06-9p-namespace-server.md
design:
Key decisions:
- 9P2000.L op subset: Tversion/Tattach/Twalk/Tlopen/Tread/Treaddir/Tclunk/Tgetattr/Tflush/Treadlink/Tfsync. msize clamped to 64 KiB so one 9P message fits one Classic frame (no fragmentation v1).
- FUSE bridge (fuser/fuse3 ABI) chosen over kernel v9fs to avoid CAP_SYS_ADMIN; v9fs deferred to v2.
- Read-mostly v1: writes return EROFS; Tauth/xattr/lock return EOPNOTSUPP; never closes connection except on unparseable frames.
- Per-spawn namespace assembled at /run/classic/ns/<MboxId> with longest-prefix-first dispatch; remote bind-mounts driven by --bind-remote node=<NodeId> at=<path> from plan 04.
- Reads of /dev/gpu/<i>/in_use call into classic-cap::Broker per read (no caching). Other dynamic fields (vram_free_mb, status) read from cached NodeAd.
- Frame kinds: NineReq=0x0400, NineRsp=0x0401 (raw 9P bytes; outer Classic length authoritative; 9P size[4] stripped).
- Stale-mount reaping at daemon startup via umount2(MNT_FORCE|MNT_DETACH) of leftovers in /run/classic/ns/. Safe because v1 has placement-at-fork (no migration).
- /svc/<name> is a symlink whose target encodes node=<hex> mbox=<u64> for readlink(2) ergonomics.
- No 9P authentication; v1 fully trusts every Classic peer per ARCHITECTURE.md.
- New crate classic-fs depends on classic-proto, classic-mbox, classic-ad (read-only), classic-cap (read-only), fuser, bytes, thiserror, tokio.
acceptance_criteria:
- AC-1: classic-fs compiles; unit tests pass; cargo clippy --all-targets clean.
- AC-2: 9pfuse mounts the local server and ls /mnt/classic shows dev, node, proc, svc.
- AC-3: Native-client test passes every implemented 9P op and every rejected op returns the documented errno.
- AC-4: FUSE smoke test passes with CLASSIC_FUSE_TESTS=1 on Linux 6.1+ x86_64.
- AC-5: classic spawn --bind-remote node=<hex> at=/cluster/B -- ls /cluster/B/dev/gpu succeeds end-to-end on a two-node deployment.
- AC-6: cat /dev/gpu/0/in_use reflects a capability acquired via classic-cap within 1 read of the acquisition (no stale-cache window).
- AC-7: Frame-range audit (grep -n 0x04 across the workspace) shows only classic-fs emits 0x04xx, and only 0x0400/0x0401 are used.
- AC-8: On daemon shutdown, all per-task FUSE mounts unmount cleanly; on next start, stale mounts from a crashed prior daemon are reaped.
